descricao: This pharmacy was founded in 1933. Located in the city centre of Porto, draws the attention of those who pass by due to the giant cross that bears on the building’s façade. It has a very singular and characteristic facade, designed by architects. Apart from selling all kinds of medicines, it is also a source of attraction for many photographs.
Cemetery built in 1887 and managed by the Foz do Douro Civil Parish.

With a typical English feel, it has a vast gardened area, and was the last cemetery in the city to be built. The Parish Council seeks, as much as possible, to make it a  light  and “welcoming  space within the respect and piety that is due at that location.
Cemetery built in 1862 and enlarged in 1989, under the management of the Ramalde Civil Parish.

Opened in 1839 as the first public cemetery in Porto, it is considered a true  museum of death .
 
The Porto s funerary art is unique and its main features are the neo-Gothic dominance, the use of granite and monumentality, especially the chapel-tombs. 

Of the numerous monuments of historical and artistic value, we highlight, for example, the chapel of the cemetery  in fact, what remains from the unfinished Church of St. Victor , the ossuary of the former nuns Convent of St. Benedict s of Hail Mary and its former cross  the cross from the graveyard itself, with an iron Christ, as well as remarkable tombs and mausoleums of personalities from various fields.


November 1st and 2nd: 8:30am-6pm
Cemetery built in 1872 and managed by the Lordelo do Ouro Civil Parish.
Cemetery built in 1867 and managed by the Campanhã Civil Parish.

Cemetery with several monuments of great value, highlighting its chapel, whose construction took place between 1870 and 1874, through a project by Eng. Gustavo Adolfo Gonçalves e Sousa.

The presbytery was later increased in 1906 under the management of Marques da Silva, which was when the byzantine inspired paintings by Silvestro Silvestri were made.

Highlight goes to the various mausoleums which have sculptures by Soares dos Reis, António Teixeira Lopes and Alves Pinto.

In the municipal sections there are various art personalities from Porto who have been buried here, such as the painter António Carneiro, the violinist Guillermina Suggia, the photographer Emílio Biel or Tomás Soller.


November 1st and 2nd: 8:30am to 6pm

Considered the oldest  modern  cemetery in Porto, it was initially blessed in 1838 and the first monuments arose in 1839.
The entire lateral section  west and east  is worth a special mention as it is unique in the country, both in terms of architecture but also in terms of greatness, thanks to the presence of sole elite Porto figures from the 19th century  big businessmen, proprietors but also many Brazilians with a noble title .
Some famous 19th century writers are buried here, such as the romantic poet Soares de Passos, the novelists Arnaldo Gama and Camilo Castelo Branco and the poet Maria da Felicidade Couto Browne.

Located in the former “Bouças do Agrello”, it was built in 1879 and doubled in size in 1910. 
In recent times, in addition to the extensions, mortuaries were built for the non-catholic population.
Buried in this cemetery we have the Blessed Mary of the Divine Heart, born in Germany and that came to Portugal in 1894, and who was sent to Porto as the Mother Superior of the city House of the Congregation.

local: Parque da Cidade  City Park - south entrance  parking
descricao: Free municipal car park located at the southern entrance of the City Park.

Private Porto cemetery which represents a more romantic environment.
Small tombstones hidden by ivy and large trees give it a sober and strongly melancholy gloom.
From 1820, the first open air perpetual funeral monuments were erected.
In this cemetery there are graves belonging to British families linked to Port Wine, but also German families and families from other nationalities. Highlights go to the mausoleum of Count Moser, the Consulate John Whitehead, founder of the cemetery, and which occupies a central position within the area, and also the Baron of Forrester.
As it is closed to the public, you must ring the bell, preferably from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m.

Private Cemetery of the Brotherhood of Our Lady of the Blessed Sacrament and Our Lady of Bonfim da Boa Hora, established in 1849/50 next to the corresponding church.

Of the existing private catholic cemeteries, the Lapa and Bonfim ones have maintained their own location because, even though they are not public, they were already cemeteries with modern urban conceptions in elevated areas and at a reasonable distance from houses.

In the cemetery, north of the church, there are two granite crosses, placed there in 1869. One of them is perhaps from the 15th century and represents the Lord of Consolation. The other seems like it comes from the 17th century and has the particularity of having the Crucified with feet apart and not overlapping.

Created in 1992 by the Club Naval de Leça, and Clube de Vela Atlântico and Sport Club do Porto.

This association of clubs manages the area of the Port of Leixões and is dedicated to recreational boating.

In addition to the simple docking area, a series of complementary services are available to recreational vessels  motor or sail , such as a crane, electricity, drinking water, security, minor repairs and bathroom facilities among others.

Placed amidst an attractive tourist destination, Porto, Leixões has increased its cruises’ operation.  Following the open of the new Cruise Quay, in April 2011, the Port of Leixões started to have two docking berths for cruises and to receive larger ships  up to 300m . In 2012, the number of passengers increased 81% and the number of ships 25%.  75.613 passengers have visited Porto. In 2013 the Port of Leixões expects around 70 cruise calls corresponding to more than 66.000 passengers and 30.000 crew members visiting the region.

The building is open for visits on Sunday  from 9:30am to 12pm, and from 2:30pm to 5pm.

local: View point – D. Fernando Wall - Guindais
descricao: From this stretch of the wall one can see the Douro River from the D. Maria Pia Bridge, passing by the Luis I Bridge and the Guindais funicular, up to the Cais de Gaia.

Century-old institution founded in 1911, with deep-roots and well ingrained in the promotion of sports, with a privileged complement in human training, integrating physical, behavioural and leisurely aspects.   
It is possible to practise many forms of sports such as aikido, handball, basketball, billiards, African dances, school sports, gymnastics, Goalball, hip hop, roller hockey, karate, kung fu, taekwondo, and archery.

The lane has an excellent view over the river, down to the river Douro mouth bar.
In the foreground one can see the Fountain of Virtudes and the terraces that go all the way down to São Pedro de Miragaia. Along the river, it is possible to see the imposing building of the former Customs House.

local: View point – Church of Nossa Senhora da Vitória
descricao: From the square next to this church, you can enjoy an interesting view of the Cathedral and the Bishop s Palace, over the houses of the parish of Vitória, as well as over the River Douro, the D. Luis I Bridge and the Gaia riverside.
With the mouth of the Douro River as a backdrop, the frame provided by the Arrábida Bridge, makes this landscape unique at sunset.

local: D. Fernando Wall - Caminho Novo Stairway
descricao: Descending by the narrow stairway, surrounded by the wall and the houses of Miragaia, you can see a small part of the slope of Gaia.

local: View point – Gardens of Palácio de Cristal
descricao:
A garden in a privileged location with many small viewpoints.
With the River Douro in the background, the Serra do Pilar, the former Customs House building, can be seen from these gardens as well as all the riverside area of Vila Nova de Gaia down to the mouth of the river, standing out the Arrábida Bridge.

local: View point – Tower of the Porto City Hall
descricao:
Privileged view over Avenida dos Aliados  avenue , covering the water mirror, in the centre, and the set of trees along the avenue, as designed by the architects Álvaro Siza Vieira and Souto Moura.
At its bottom we can see Praça da Liberdade  square , with special mention to the Cardosas Palace.
From this tower one can also see the upper part of the city of Vila Nova de Gaia, on the other side of the river Douro, including the building of El Corte Inglés and the tower of Monte da Virgem.

Visits to the tower take place on the 1st Sunday of every month and must be booked in advance.

From this location, next to the Infante Bridge, one can enjoy the view over Vila Nova de Gaia, on the other side of the river, and a partial view of the D. Luís I Bridge, a popular spot to watch the São João fireworks.

local: Tower of the Church of Senhora da Conceição
descricao:
One of the city’s highest view points, from where you can see, besides the city centre, the mouth of the river Douro and the city of Vila Nova de Gaia.
Special mention goes to Praça Marquês de Pombal and the church yard.
To get to the top you have to climb a magnificent stairway and pass the tower clock machine and its bells.
View point on Rua da Restauração, by the crossroads with Rua Jorge Viterbo Ferreira, from where you can admire the landscape from the Gaia Quay up to the Arrábida Bridge.

The Portuguese Federation of Canoeing has, as its main objective, the promotion and regulation of canoeing in all its aspects and specialties.

Over the Molhe beach, this large space with park benches is used for sports practice and for small events.
It offers a view of the entire coastal area of Foz.
From here one can see the Gaia riverside down to Afurada, highlighting the Arrábida Bridge.

descricao: Settled in 1966, this club is dedicated to football 11.

The Arco  arch  or Porta  door  of Sant’Ana das Aldas, was one of the four doors of the old Porto borough, giving access to the river and to the merchant area.
The Arch was of simple architecture:  a bit narrow, winding and tall. However, its special nature and form gave the old street an extremely gracious, picturesque and original character. 
The demolition of the Arch started in June 1821. As a remembrance of the Saint from which the Arch was named after, there is only a small image placed on a small sanctuary of glazed wood.  It can still be seen today hanging or carved on the wall by the stone stairs that lead from Rua de Sant’Ana to Rua da Pena Ventosa.

local: View point – Fort São Francisco Xavier  Cheese Castle
descricao: Located on the rocky beach of Castelo do Queijo. This area provides a wide panoramic view that extends from the beach of Matosinhos and the famous sculpture of the Anemone, passing through the transparent building and the City Park, down to the ocean.

Aerodrome in Maia.
It has a runway which is 1700m long, 30m wide and that has an incline of 1.2%.  It is limited to light aviation.

local: View point – Chapel of Santa Catarina and Senhora dos Anjos
descricao: Located in one of the highest points in the parish of Lordelo do Ouro it offers a comprehensive view of the Douro River estuary, including the Garden of Calém, and reaching the sea.

Certified runway for motorized light and ultralight aircrafts, the strip is 465m x 38m, asphalt, with a resistance of 17 ton/r.i.s.
There are no radio assistance or radio frequency. The airfield has a pilot room, briefing room, classroom and fuel/oil depot and two hangars. The Aero Clube da Costa Verde Complex includes a aeromodelling field, horse riding centre and restaurant.
View over the famous Ponte D. Maria Pia, by Gustave Eiffel, and the São João Bridge, both dedicated to rail transport, although the first is now disabled.

An eighteenth century building, typical of the urban planning reforms instigated by the Almadas, where João Baptista de Almeida Garrett, was born in 1799 and where he lived until 1804. 
In 1864, Porto City Council mounted an oval plaster plaque in the middle of the first floor façade. This is decorated with garlands of laurel leaves and an inscription paying homage to the writer.

local: House at no. 59, Rua da Reboleira
descricao: This large fortified house was probably built in the 14th century. To this day, the original structure of the tower-house is practically intact.

local: House at no. 55, Rua da Reboleira
descricao:
This is one of the most interesting buildings of the civil architecture of the late Middle Ages. It is one of the biggest buildings in the area and has elements of different epochs.
At basement level, with access to Rua do Outeirinho, some medieval structures that might date back to the 14th century can be seen.
The Rua da Reboleira façade still maintains the ground floor gothic portals. 
The higher storeys were changed, probably in the 17th century, as well as the fortified surrounding ring.

Given the context we are currently living in and in order to guarantee the best hygiene and safety conditions for everyone, the number of classes and the number of places per timetable are limited. In addition, some rules for the use of this facility are in effect, namely:
- Wearing a face mask inside the facility is compulsory, except during sporting activity 
- Mandatory compliance with the entry and exit flow routes and physical distance rules 
- Free use of the pool and gymnasiums requires prior booking at the slots available at the reception and is limited to 60 minutes per use 
- Registration in classes is only allowed once a week 
- It is strictly forbidden to enter gymnasiums with shoes used on the street 
- It is mandatory to use a towel in the weight room and in the Gym classes 
- The maximum time for using the showers is limited to 15 minutes 
- Using lockers is mandatory. Putting clothes on the benches’ hangers is not allowed 
- Teaching material should be placed in the disinfection area after use.

Registrations must be made via email remupi@agoraporto.pt.

Opened in 2000, a variety of sports can be practiced here: swimming for babies, children, adults as well as free use.  There are also water and deep water aerobics classes.  In 2005, a cardio-fitness room that has 13 machines was opened. There is also a 10 x 10m pool that is 5m deep where you can dive.
Founded in 1915, it has the following activities: tennis, squash, 5-a-side synthetic grass football, capoeira, taekwondo, kickboxing, aerobics, and ballroom dancing.

local: Casa do Infante  / old Royal Custom House
descricao:
Building in which, according to tradition, Prince Henry, the Navigator,  was born. Built in 1325 by D. Afonso IV, it was used as Royal House, Mint and Royal Customs House, operating until 1859.

Today is the headquarters of Porto Historical Archives, which gather important historical documents such as the Charter Act of the City.

Excavations carried out in the building discovered traces of Roman occupation, which can be seen in the museum created there.

Included in the Urban Wine Route.

Founded in 1980, it holds the documentation produced by the municipality of Porto from the Middle Ages as well as various private archives and collections. 
Its collection includes numerous handwritten series, scrolls, illuminated manuscripts, drawings, photographs, etc. In compliment to this, it has an informative, auxiliary library with about 5,000 print publications and also collections of old postcards, prints, posters, etc., on Porto related issues.
It is responsible for receiving, organising and creating tools for access to documentation, creating conditions for public access to documents and promoting the publication of its heritage and issues related to the city s history.

Considering the current panomara, you should contact the Gabinete do Munícipe, to make an appointment in advance.

The Gabinete do Munícipe facilitates the relationship the citizen has with the Council through an Integrated Multi-channel Service. It translates into the centralisation / integration of face-to-face, Web and telephone service channels, namely through the standardisation of forms of service and access to information and the assumption of a dual role: that of  Municipal Ombudsman   because he acts as the representative of the interests of the citizens of the Municipality  and the  Player   because from the identification of nonconformities and trouble spots, he interacts with the various services for their resolution .

This house of Flemish appearance, with the characteristic chimney placed at the top and in the middle of the façade, is believed to have been built in the first half of the fourteenth century.
The façade, half-hidden behind the chapel of the Cathedral, once gave to a lively square of the borough, bounded to the west by the cathedral’s ambulatory which was later demolished.
Located in the Alameda Shop&Spot , which is connected to the Estadio do Dragão, it has a metro station close by and offers 7 different theatre rooms.

The S. Bento Railway Station was built at the beginning of the twentieth century on the exact location of the former Convent of S. Bento de Avé-Maria. The glass and iron structure was designed by the architect Marques da Silva. The vestibule is adorned with twenty thousand tiles painted by Jorge Colaço that illustrate the transport evolution and events of Portuguese history and life.

Founded in 1869 and always identified with Porto and its civic and cultural movements. It has a rich artistic heritage of oil paintings, sculpture and pottery as well as coin and medal collections.  However, the  crown jewel  is the library itself which is considered one of the best private libraries in the Iberian Peninsula.   
Its bibliographic content is of great value as it has a collection of more than 40,000 titles and 80,000 volumes.  Highlighted, from among the many rarities,  is the first edition of  The Lusiads , an edition of the Bible dated from 1500, and some writings from Portuguese chronicler Fernão Lopes.

local: Santa Casa da Misericórdia do Porto Historical Archive
descricao:
Reopened to the public in May 2013, at Casa da Prelada, this archive stores the documentation collected by Misericórdia in the exercise of its activity, and that of closed institutions that also belonged to it. The conditions for members of the public to access documents are created here and the dissemination of the Misericórdia’s document collection is promoted through the publication of inventories, scientific works and the holding of exhibitions. In the reading rooms, access to information is done by directly consulting the documentation or its digitisation. 

The Historical Archive has a reprography service and also makes available its Digital Inventory, hosted on the AtoM  Access to Memory  platform. 

Public access is free and requires prior appointment via email arquivohistorico@scmp.pt. 

The Archive includes an open access Library, specialised mainly in the following themes: Misericórdias  History of the City of Porto  Art History.

local: Central Library of the Porto Faculty of Arts
descricao:
Primarily aimed at providing the academic community with the library resources necessary for research and teaching.   
Nowadays, it provides a significant array of electronic resources that include referential databases, full text databases, electronic journals, open consortiums, sites, etc.
The computerisation of the operational circuits, from the acquisition of the literature to the publishing of information, has facilitated the establishment of new areas of intervention.  
It also has multimedia support for the visually impaired students.

Built in the 18th century  1790  to be used as Casa Pia  Pious House , outwardly, the central body of the façade where a sober fronton appears is worthy of mention .
The building was used for several purposes from a military criminals and recruits prison to General Headquarters and Civilian Government main office.

local: Porto Trade Association - Porto Chamber of Commerce and Industry
descricao:
Since its founding, the ACP-CCIP has enriched and accumulated a unique collection in terms of various documents, books, monographs, encyclopaedias, international treaties, old manuscripts and atlases from more than 10,000 volumes available to the public, for consultation.   
You can also find a wide range of reports, brochures, yearbooks, magazines and other periodicals and collections of the city, as well as regional newspapers. 
Connected via computers to the national and international network of Documentation Centres and Libraries, it has an internet terminal, and its databases are constantly being updated.   
It still holds a substantial archive of photography and images related to Porto, to the Palácio da Bolsa  the Stock Exchange Palace  and the main events which took place there over the last 100 years.
It has edited a variety of brochures and monographs, such as reprints of the themes of  O Tripeiro , the book commemorating its 150th Anniversary or the art book on the Palácio da Bolsa.

local: Library of the Porto Customs Museum
descricao:
Opened in 2006, its main objective was to provide and make accessible to all interested users, all information and  resources regarding Customs from the Directorate General of Customs  DGAIEC , of transport and communications. br  
It continues to provide the following services: face-to-face consultation, user support and guidance, reproduction of documents  photocopying and scanning , home loan  transport and communications issues , use of personal computer equipment and use of the space as a study area.

This square-shaped beacon was built in 1527 by order of D. Miguel da Silva, prebendary of the Santo Tirso monastery who owned the S. João da Foz estate, being therefore one of the oldest in Europe. 
The inscription on the southern wall mentions that the priest “sold some of his own fields, in order to use the funds to keep a bonfire burning perpetually on the tower.”

The fortress was built at the end of the sixteenth century to protect the coast and the Douro entrance. 
Modern strongholds were added  sec. XVII  to the original structure of this fine example of military architecture and the access gate to the fort  18th century .
It now hosts the Delegação Regional do Porto do Instituto de Defesa Nacional   Regional Office of the National Defense Institute. It has been listed as a building of public interest since 6 June 1967.
Bonfim is a parish with a population of 24.265 people  2011  distributed among an area of about 3,05 km2.

local: Centros Nacionais de Apoio à Integração de Migrantes
descricao:
The Centros Nacionais de Apoio ao Imigrante  CNAI  were created in 2004 to respond to some of the difficulties felt by immigrants in their integration process in Portugal. ACIDI created a centre which brought together various services, institutions and Support Offices for the Immigrant, such as Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras  SEF , Segurança Social  SS , Autoridade para as Condições de Trabalho  ACT , Conservatória dos Registos Centrais  CRC  and Ministério da Educação  ME .
The CNAI s also have specialised departments, such as Gabinete de Apoio ao Reagrupamento Familiar  GARF , o Gabinete de Apoio Jurídico ao Imigrante  GAJI , Gabinete de Apoio Social  GAS , Gabinete de Apoio à Habitação  GAH , Gabinete de Apoio ao Emprego  with two areas, one for the coordination of the GIP Imigrante Network - Gabinete de Inserção Profissional - and the other specialised in the area of Entrepreneurship , Gabinete de Apoio ao Imigrante Consumidor  GAIC  and Gabinete de Apoio à Qualificação  GAQ .

Opened in 2001, and already its number of users has exceeded more than 1.75 million.  
This building by the architect José Manuel Soares, is located in the gardens of the Palácio de Cristal and is part of the National Public Reading Network. It is spread over 2 floors, occupys an area of 3500 m2 and is divided into three sections: Children and Youth, General Reading and Multimedia. It contains 90,000 monographs, 10,000 multimedia documents and 140 periodical publication series. Available services include home loans, catalogue search, watching films, listening to music, consultating paper documents and non-book material, copying documents, access for visually impaired readers, scanning documents and computer and Internet access.  There are also decentralized services to the community, through the Mobile Library  the Librarycar  and SABE - the Support Service for School Libraries.  In 2010 it was integrated into the Network of Associated Libraries of UNESCO.
Built in the 17th century to protect the coast from the pirates of North Africa, it is located on a cheese-shaped rocky hill, thus obtaining the name Castelo do Queijo  Cheese Castle .

Designed by John Whitehead in neo-Palladian style and built between 1785 and 1790, it was meant to be used as a meeting place for the English businessmen living in Porto. On the main façade, the ground floor is made up of seven arches that give access to the colonnaded gallery. 
In contrast with this, the main floor is formed of high openings with verandas and pediments. A balustrade attic tops the building.
Included in the Urban Wine Route.

local: SEF - Serviço de Estrangeiros e Fronteiras
descricao:
In the framework of the internal security policy, its fundamental objectives are to control the movement of people across borders, the staying and activities of foreigners within the national territory, and study, promote, coordinate and implement the measures and actions related to those activities and those with migratory movements.   
As a Body of Criminal Police, it acts in the process, according to the law of criminal procedure, under the direction and functional dependence of the competent judicial authority, performing certain actions and acts delegated by the given Authority.
Campanhã is a parish with a population of 32.659 people  2011  distributed among an area of about 8,13 km2.

Porto Municipal Public Library adapt its opening hour to the decreed state of emergency, but remain open with the due safety, hygiene restrictions and with the Clean & Safe accreditation:
- Monday: 10am to 6pm
- Tuesday to Friday: 9am to 7:30pm
- Saturday: 10am to 1pm


Founded in 1833 under the order of D. Pedro IV, the Porto Municipal Library occupied its current premises, the former Convent of Saint Anthony of the City, a building from the 18th century, now classified as a building of Public Interest in 1842, and which became municipal property in 1876.   
Its initial collection brings together bibliographic collections from libraries of religious orders and individuals. The decree that ordered its creation also demanded that it should be given a copy of any printed material in Portugal. This is a mission that continues today through the receipt of the legal deposit.   
Its special collections which date from the Middle Ages to the present day make it very popular.  The manuscripts stand out because of their unique character.
Free internet use is available by using a card reader.
The Library also offers a new service called “Digital Sound Library” exclusively dedicated to  blind and amblyopic citizens which consists of free  but restricted access  subject to authentication  to audio books.

local: Rectory of the University of Porto
descricao:
Designed by Carlos Amarante in the neoclassical style and financed
by the Literary Subsidy, a tax
on wine, 1803.
The building, of classical style, has some similarities with the Santo António Hospital. 
In 1911 it housed the Faculty of Sciences, and presently houses the Rectory and the Natural History Museum of the University of Porto.
Included in the Urban Wine Route.

The following institutions are represented in this location: ADSE - Direcção-Geral da Protecção Social aos Funcionários e Agentes da Administração Pública  Agência para a Modernização Administrativa, IP  Águas do Porto  Autoridade para as Condições de Trabalho  Caixa Geral de Aposentações  Caixa Geral de Depósitos  CTT  Direcção-Geral da Administração da Justiça  Direcção-Geral da Segurança Social  Direcção-Geral dos Impostos  EDP- Energias de Portugal  EDP Gás  Governo Civil  Instituto da Mobilidade e dos Transportes Terrestres, IP  Instituto da Segurança Social  Instituto do Emprego e Formação Profissional  Instituto dos Registos e do Notariado, IP  Portugal Telecom  Sociedade de Transportes Colectivos do Porto  Zon TV Cabo, SA.
In addition to the entities listed, this shop has a Multiple Service Station  PAM  which carries out various services, provided by several entities, at one desk.

local: Parish of Aldoar, Foz do Douro and Nevogilde
descricao: The Parish of Aldoar, Foz do Douro and Nevogilde has approximately 28.858 inhabitants  2011  distributed among an area of 7.36 square kilometres.

local: Parishes of Cedofeita, Santo Ildefonso, Sé, Miragaia, São Nicolau and Vitória
descricao: The Parish of Cedofeita, Santo Ildefonso, Sé, Miragaia, São Nicolau and Vitória has approximately 40.440 inhabitants  2011  distributed among an area of 5,43 square kilometres.

The Fernandina wall was built to substitute the old late-medieval wall that, by the 15th Century, had become too small for the growing city. 

It was rebuilt by D. Fernando, after whom it was named, between 1368 and 1437 with money from the wine
transfer tax and it was about 3000 paces long and 30 feet high. It was topped off by battlements and strengthened by bastions and towers. Two stretches still exist today. The first, called the Trecho dos Guindais, can be found next to Rua Arnaldo Gama and the other, the Trecho do Caminho Novo, is next to the Escadas do Caminho Novo  Caminho Novo Steps . This visiting point is included in the Urban Wine Route.

local: Parish of Lordelo do Ouro and Massarelos
descricao: The Parish of União das Freguesias de Lordelo do Ouro and Massarelos has approximately 29 059 inhabitants  2011  distributed among an area of 5,34 square kilometres.

This market was built in 1885, by order of the City Council, in order to replace the old Mercado da Ribeira  Ribeira Market .

It operated as a market for a very short period of time, having been used for various purposes. It represtents an important example of iron architecture in Porto, which was later renovated to become an entertainment venue, where Hard Club is now located.

local: Santo António Hospital  Centro Hospitalar do Porto
descricao:
The hospital was built between 1770 and 1825 by the Public Works Board, in neo-Palladian English style, or “Port Wine Architecture”, following a plan by the English architect John Carr.
Forming a large quadrangle around a yard, its simplicity and the clarity of its content confer the building an austere and sober aspect.
Included in the Urban Wine Route.
Ramalde is a parish in the municipality of Porto, with 5.68 km² area

Company located on the banks of the Douro River. It has bikes and segways for rent.


Business Hours:
April to October: daily from 10am to 7pm
November to March: daily from 9am to 6pm
Except January 1st and December 25th

Due to the current pandemic situation, Bluedragon is operating with reduced hours, from Monday to Friday from 9:30am to 6pm and at the weekend from 9:30am to 1pm.

It was probably built in the 13th century, although it might be founded on an older construction.

In 1737 it was remodelled  the changes were designed by Nicolau Nasoni, according to some authors.

The main façade is open by a full arch flanked by pilasters and topped by a decorated fronton.

The central window has a stone balustrade and the fronton – that exceeds the entablature – reveals the coat of arms of Bishop D. Rafael de Mendonça.

The lateral façades present equally framed windows with magnificent frontons.

Take a virtual tour through the link: http://www.diocese-porto.pt/visitavirtual/

Visit accompanied by a paper guidebook  folded  in Portuguese, English, French and Spanish and by the room sheets.
Farmácia Santa Catarina, 103 years taking care of you!

Construction began in 1920, but only in 1957 was it considered complete, after several interruptions and changes to the original project.
The building consists of six floors, a basement and two courtyards. 
The top of the 70m high, central tower, with its chiming clock, can be reached by climbing its 180 steps.
Consisting primarily of marble and granite, the interior of the building has some nobly furnished rooms, in which the most solemn ceremonies and the usual Municipal Assemblies and Executive meetings are held.
Included in the Urban Wine Route, the Porto Municipality used from the outset the income from rates and taxes on wine to improve the city´s defences, water supply, road connections to the outside and urban reforms.

The first ring of walls encircled the Morro da Sé  The Cathedral Hill  on the Pena Ventosa heights which at one point was of vital strategic importance regarding control over passage up the Douro. 
Only a few remains survive, partly hidden by rows of houses. This early wall may have been put up by the Romans and then rebuilt in the 12th Century. The wall had four doors or entrances. A small stretch of the wall can be seen near the cathedral, where it is identified by a plaque.

This Mansion House was built in the eighteenth century in baroque style, leaning against the D. Fernando wall. Some attribute its design to Nasoni, but this has not been proven. 
The building served as a home to the illustrious families of Porto, as is evident by the coat of arms in granite placed over the main door. 
It has already housed the military hospital for liberal troops at the time of the Siege of Porto, as the Porto Commercial Typography and as the Museum of Ethnography and History. 
Due to signs of degeneration caused by a fire in 1984, the Palace has been closed since 1992.

Of the 18 doors and wickets of the D. Fernando Wall, built in the fourteenth century around the city of Porto, the Carvão  coal  wicket is the only one that survived to the present day.
This wicket, situated in the vicinity of the current Praça da Ribeira, linked the Rua da Fonte Taurina to Ribeira quay, where the boats docked on the River Douro.

Vieguini is the first Bike & Scooter rental shop in Porto with the best prices and services. We know Porto like no one else and we´ll give you the best tips of where to go in town  what you can see and do, where to eat, etc.  and always recommend you the best route according to your will.  We have the experience and the tradition. In Porto there are more than 70kms of cycle way and most part of it is along the river and along the beaches. We are located right on the main cycle path, in the historic center in front of the Douro river, on the flat and safe area of the city for cycling. Rent a bike or scooter with Vieguini – Bikes Scooters Rental Porto and we show you, how  you can easily go to the” blue flag” beaches at Gaia, go to the best fish restaurants  for the locals , to historical places, know wonderful parks, museums, surf spots, etc. , to make your visit to the Porto an unforgettable experience.

The Palace of Freixo, built in the mid 18th century, is one of the most remarkable monuments of Portuguese civil baroque designed by the architect Nasoni.

The palace has a rectangular design and there are prominent turrets on the corners which are covered by slate roofs in the shape of pyramids.

The interior of the palace is extremely flush. A lot of the compartments have “frescos” and well executed stucco ceilings, some with oriental influence. Illusory painting with allegorical themes is commonly used inside the palace and was largely painted by Nasoni himself.

The garden was clearly designed according to Italian tradition, with sculptures and a magnificent view over the River.
It is now a Pousada de Portugal, preserving the entire façade of its buildings which were classified as a National Monument since 1910.

The Pousada consists of two separate buildings that are connected: the Palace and the Harmonia Mill, both of them prepared to house large events and meetings.

This tower, also named “Torre da Cidade”  Tower of the City , was discovered during the demolition work of the 1940s, on the street where the Terreiro da Sé was built.

It was then completely rebuilt, being relocated from the original site some 15 meters.

This quadrangular building is divided into two storeys, with a high arched door to the south and a gothic stone balcony, to the north.

Until 1960, it was the Office of the History of the City, which is why this building was also known as Tower of the City. 
After April 25 1974, the Tower was publically occupied, who installed in it the Social and Cultural Centre of Sé. Here operates the Official Tourism Office of Sé.
A diversified choice of cruises in the Douro valley allowing different ways of seeing the river, the riverbanks and the landscape.

A recent addition to the city that is located right next to Campo Alegre. 
It is part of the “Romantic Trails”  a group of walks located along the Massarelos Valley and set up by the city as part of the Porto European City of Culture celebrations in 2001. 
Of evertyhing that used to be here, only a single imposing palm tree remains.  Lawns and a flower bed have been planted around it alongside a wooden bench.

Located in the eastern part of the city, its garden was re-qualified as a municipal garden in 1993. 
It is characterised by its sycamore trees and has some areas that allow the practising of sport.

Built in 1915-1916, it is a rectangular garden, surrounded by lime trees and American oaks.  The grassed lawns surround the symmetrically planted palm trees and blackthorn with purple foliage.  
Adorning the garden we have several sculptures:  the Bust of Bacchus, the Republic and the figure of Father Américo.  
The eastern side of the garden is surrounded by a number of small palaces.

Located at the famous Rotunda da Boavista  roundabout , it stretches out around the imposing  Tribute Monument to the Heroes of the Peninsular War” which stands in its centre, designed by Marques da Silva.  
In the garden surrounding the monument special attention goes to the liquidambars, oaks, linden trees, tulips and some palm trees.

The Foz Gardens stretch out along Avenida Montevideu and Avenida do Brasil, on the sea front of Porto. 
Going through these gardens, we can see that there are several points of interest that bring back memories of different historical and allegorical characters.   
At the beginning of Avenida de Montevideu, there is a fountain which was opened in 1931 and designed by the architect Manuel Marques. It is one of the rare examples of monumental fountains built in Porto in the 20th century.   
Also on Avenida Montevideo, we can find the  Homem do Leme” statue  Man of the Rudder  which was inaugurated in 1934 and that pays tribute to the men of the sea.  
On Avenida do Brasil, there is a statue of Luís de Camões, one of the most prominent figures of Portuguese culture from the Renaissance period, created by Irene Vilar.  Also on Avenida Brasil, there is a sculpture from 1937 created by Henrique Moreira which represents a lifesaver in the middle of a storm.  There are areas and equipment for practising sports.

Small garden located in front of the Market of Foz. 
Works like a garden of passage rather than as a walking area, and in it, in the centre, we have a bust of the writer that gave it its name.

Small garden area which is located very close to the mouth of the Douro River.   
It offers a magnificent view over the river, of which we highlight the high black poplars, as well as a monument which commemorates the expedition of Ceuta in 1415. The garden is lined by sycamore trees, stretching down to Passeio Alegre. 
Along the way, in Sobreiras, some lawns and tree and shrub plantations extend the green space.

local: Praça da Rainha D. Amélia Garden
descricao: Small wooded garden, landscaped with grass, seasonal flowers and flowerbeds, operating more as a passing point than a space of leisure.

Created in the early years of the Republic, between 1912 and 1914, most of its trees still originate from the time of its creation.   
Normally used as a walkway, this triangular garden incorporates an irregular lake around which the trees are predominately young cedars and chamaecyparis. The garden is dominated to the north by a viewpoint that was built in here and which increases the natural slope of the terrain.  Sycamore trees were planted around the garden.  To the south there is a series of Jacarandas from Brazil that are very rare in the north of the country.  
A sculpture in the garden representing Afonso Costa is worth highlighting.

local: Largo da Maternidade Júlio Dinis Garden
descricao: Small green space, which differs somewhat from its original format  a triangular format  and which has its drinking fountain dating back to 1894. It offers an area for sitting, inviting you to appreciate life on the square.

Existing since 1885, this square is surrounded by Mercado Ferreira Borges and Palácio da Bolsa, located in the historical center.

Here, there is a small garden, under which an underground car park was built and a Monument to the Infante D. Henrique, inaugurated on the occasion of the 5th centenary of his death. Its construction started in 1894, was completed in 1900, and represents the triumph of Portuguese navigation and faith.

Designed in 1897, it is considered by some the last Romantic Garden in Porto with 18th-century features, with highlight to the twisting lake in the centre, crossed by a bridge, surrounded by leafy trees. We can distinguish the resinous trees that shape an atmosphere of mountain forests, and even the cedars, sequoias and araucarias. Special note should be given to the sculpture of the doctor, professor, researcher and artist Abel Salazar, by the sculptor Helder de Carvalho.

Located close to the Hospital de St. António, here one can appreciate the quietness offered by its romantic layout and dense vegetation. It has several period plants that bloom in profusion.

Fun fact: In the past, there was a century-old giant sequoia  Sequoiadendron giganteum , whose importance was recognized by all, in such a way that it gave rise to a set of performative and temporary interventions, entitled  Soft Monuments .

It still incorporates some romantic features from the original garden. Some of these are the iron bandstand, two lakes with water features, camellias and sycamore trees although they have been altered by subsequent renovations.

The garden currently has two access points to the underground station and these accesses are camouflaged by long camellia hedges.

This garden located in Praça Marquês de Pombal is from the beginning of the 19th century and is situated in the old Largo da Aguardente.

Circa 1850, the square already had its current configuration, and in 1870 the Aguardente bullring was erected, which together with the Rotunda da Boavista bullring were the only two bullfighting venues in the city of Porto. It was opened in 1898 and had a romantic aspect.

Fun fact: It was one of the city s defense lines during the French invasions and during the siege of Porto.

local: Largo José Moreira da Silva Garden
descricao:
Small garden located between Rua da Alegria and Rua Dom João IV, next to the Escola Normal ramp. 
A highlight of this garden in the bronze bust which represents José Moreira da Silva.

Small wooded garden practically covered with grass.   
There we can see the Monument to the Young Sales Professional.

Located in the Antas area, once called Praça Velasquez Garden, it was built in 1948. It has a circular shape and an allusive sculpture of Francisco Sá Carneiro. It is a wonderful spot to enjoy a pause in the middle of the city, providing services and several coffee shops on the surrounding area.

The three-metre high bronze woman looks as if she is striding determinedly towards the barracks with an olive branch  the symbol of peace  in one hand and an astrolabe in the other. On her head she wears a Frisian cap which Bruno Marques, the sculptor, admits he borrowed from the French Revolution. The statute was modelled on a 22-year old woman from Porto called Maria do Carmo.

The SPASO ZEN concept includes a Wellness centre with complete image, beauty and relaxation services, combining the aesthetic benefits with the therapeutic benefits of a given treatment to care and rejuvenate the body and also relax and revitalise the spirit for a healthier and happier life.

Protecting the environment is an integral part of the philosophy of Spaso Zen and the brand uses organic and biological products in the provision of various services, offering Yoga and Tai Chi classes, meditation, courses and workshops, and organises spiritual retreats.

Oporto Medical SPA, a pioneer in therapeutic treatment where techniques are combined with the most advanced technologies and solely executed by physical therapists.  
It offers all types of massages and body treatments as they consider physical and psychological well-being as fundamental to a happy life.

The Pharmacy Museum has a collection spanning 500 million  years of the universal history of health and disease. It brings together objects of rare historical, artistic, anthropological and scientific value from such far-flung and bygone civilisations and cultures as those of Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome, the Incas, the Aztecs, Islam, Africa, Tibet, China and Japan, amongst others. Portugal’s pharmaceutical heritage is represented by the excellent reconstruction of Porto’s Estácio Pharmacy, which used to be in Rua Sá da Bandeira. The reconstruction of the Islamic Pharmacy that was once housed in a 19th Century Ottoman Empire Palace in Damascus was also recently opened to the public.

Located in the Boavista area, by the Romanesque Church of S. Martinho de Cedofeita, it is 2 minutes away from the metro  Carolina Michaelis , 15 min from the beaches and the Port Wine cellars and 21 minutes away from the airport.
It offers 21 rooms with bathroom facilities, air-conditioning, heating and cable TV as well as an inviting terrace and a breakfast room.

Created in 2005 with the objective of promoting and sharing the Douro river and its region, it began its activity by carrying out daily cruises between Porto and Barca d Alva in rebelo type ships,  Catraios do Douro .

It currently has two more ships, “Riba Douro” - cruise ship, and  Quinta da Eira Velha  - rebelo type boat, and it also has a small yatch, “S. Rafael”.

It also organises events such as lunches, dinners, weddings, birthdays, meetings and conferences.

Pipadouro is a river tour company whose mission is  quality with excellence .

It has two vessels with an individual capacity of 12 passengers and has a choice of cruises on the Douro, and the commitment to quality and attention to detail is constant.

Young but very dynamic company whose purpose is to introduce the natural beauty of the Douro River, offering multiple service alternatives.

Its activity can be divided into two main services:
- Cruises on the Douro River: from the mouth of the River to the border with Spain, on ships with every comfort and safety -the vessels have a capacity of between 6 and 64 people.
- Nautical Events: Rentdouro carries out various events for all types of groups through personalised programmes.

The Chá Clube  Tea Club  has been a reference as a tea House for 25 years. Of a classical style, very British both with the comfort and decoration it provides, the Chá Clube offers daily at tea time scones, varied home baked cakes, with special mention to the Turkish chocolate cake and orange cake, as well as teas from all over the world. The tea cups are British, the teapots are made of silver, and th
e tablecloths and napkins in pink linen give the tea a very stylish look. At lunch the ambience is executive, as the Chá Clube is located in one of the city’s main business centres and you can enjoy a light meal.

The Bank of Materials is installed in the Palace of the Viscounts of Balsemão, with a new concept of visitable reserve, showing the public the various aspects aimed at safeguarding the City characterizing materials of such as tiles, plaster, iron, etc.

In this sense, the general public can enjoy the exhibition of various decorative and constructive pieces that integrate, very expressively, the Porto architecture, differentiating it. The Bank of Materials collects materials from degraded buildings, to be demolished or altered, in order to make them available free of charge for the recovery of the facades missing some elements of the same type. Thus, the Bank of Materials, promotes the maintenance of the urban image of Porto.

The guided tour to the venue is free but advance booking is required.

Table, kitchen and decorative items. There are wedding, birthday and wish-list services  available online . Whether you are a professional of the upmarket hotel or restaurant industry or an individual client, you will find the boldest contemporary items inspired by current tableware trends. You can try our latest products in our experimental kitchen which also hosts culinary workshops.

Closed

Remarkable building with visible Neopaladian traits. It has English influence, something which was uncommon in nineteenth century Porto architecture and which is marked by French fine arts. 
English made tiles cover the building. They were produced at Minton, Hollings & Company, from Stoke-on-Trent. 
The Porto City Council bought the property from its heirs in 1966 for the Music conservatory, which operated from here from 1975 to 2008.
In the former recovered stables, the City Council began, in 1986, to install the reserves of the municipal museums and prepared a strategic concept which led to the creation, in Pinto Leite Palace, of one of the multi-cultural houses. It is currently being used as an exhibition centre.
Visitors are welcome with or without appointment and may visit the building and the gardens. There is as well the possibility of space rental for exhibitions, conferences, workshops, photographic sessions, fashion events, theme parties, upon payment of current Municipal rates. Lending Regulations for Municipal Venues can be consulted at the City Council’s website.

Settled in a renovated building with an intimate, relaxed and comfortable ambience.
It offers 4 double rooms and 3 dormitories all equipped with bathroom facilities, complete kitchen, lounge and 24 hour reception service and includes breakfast, free wireless internet, bar and a nice garden to relax and get together.
Japonese restaurant on Rua de Santa Catarina.

Almaviva – name inspired by the Count Almaviva, a character of the Opera The Marriage of Figaro, by Mozart. br 
Settled in 1997, the Almaviva keeps on being one of the cities’ iconic shops, with clients coming from outside Porto, just to visit it. br 
It specialized in natural and recycled products, and above all it has a special taste for revival articles such as tin foil and wooden toys, which stand out to the eyes of those visiting the store. Another characteristic of this shop that delights those who still have reminiscences about it, are the gift packages made out of “papel riscado”, an old sort of paper which was used in bygone times, to buy rice in grocery stores. br 
Come and pay us a visit, it will be worthwhile!
Located close to Avenida Boavista, this restaurant is a traditional Chinese one in which its decor is enhanced with Oriental motifs.

Founded in 1877, you are likely to come across different products from chequebook cases, purses, pencil cases, ladies and men’s wallets, belts, braces, suitcases, travel bags and also a large selection of umbrellas with a variety of wooden handles: java, malaca, gorse among others.

We have a great variety of products of great quality and an always personalized service.

This establishment is recognised as a landmark of tradition and prestige in the city.
Castelbel is a 100% Portuguese company dedicated to the handmade manufacture of luxury soaps and fragrances for the body and the house, inspired by the aromas, colours and environment of Portugal.

Inaugurated in 1984, David Rosas shop in Porto has made an enormous impact both for the innovative architecture of the shop and for the quality of its pieces. Dedicated to the creation of exclusive jewellery pieces, also offering a wide range of the most prestigious high jewellery brands.
It is also associated with prestigious brands of watches, of which Patek Philippe is a highlight and is an exclusive agent of his designs for Portugal.

Inaugurated in 1859, this jewellery shop is a landmark in the city for its history and age. It has been able to preserve a service of excellence recognized to this day. The silverware and the filigree pieces are the flagship products of this century-old house. It also has a complete range of gold/silversmithing, jewellery, watchmaking products and transforming jewellery.

Perry Sampaio” has, since 1960, been solely dedicated to the sale of home furnishings. br 
Specializing in the production of hand-embroidered table-cloths, it also has lighting products, table lamps among other articles for the decoration of every corner of the house. At Perry Sampaio it is also possible to find gifts for all occasions!

This owes its planning to the visionary spirit of the Almadas whose restoration work brought a new brightness to the city of Porto in the second half of the 18th Century.

The road was extended as far as Alameda da Aguardente, today’s Praça Marquês de Pombal.

Its imposing façades house some fine examples of the Art Nouveau style.

Over 1500 metres long, the Rua de Santa Catarina, part of which has been pedestrianised, is the busiest shopping street in the city centre.

Club located at the Dragão Stadium, in front of the subway’s Parking lot 1.  
It has 6,535 m2 spread over 2 floors, offering a wide range of services from a gym, studios, squash courts, swimming pools, thermal tanks with a spa with body treatments, massage, beauty treatments, etc. .

Located on Avenida da Boavista, this Arcádia House of Chocolate follows a concept that combines traditional coffee shop and tea house service with the most contemporary needs. Settled in a 250 m2 space, you can choose between its quiet and exquisite interior or sitting comfortably on the terrace and experience the ambience and bustle of the city.
Arcádia offers you good taste, unique moments and a great variety of chocolates that challenge your senses.

Located next to the iconic São Bento Train Station, Óptica da Baixa has over the years built up a solid base of customers who are looking for quality service and professionalism from those behind the counter. This shop, with a journey that cemented its inclusion within the scope of traditional shops, sells optical frames from a variety of leading brands, monofocal and progressive lenses, as well as contact lenses and sunglasses. This establishment also features an optometry service, assessing your eye health and visual efficiency on the spot, which enables professionals to provide personalised advice tailored to your needs.

ROSIOR expresses art when it manufactures each of its high jewelery pieces. The founder and creator of all the unique pieces is Manuel Rosas, an artist with a brilliantly creative mind that manages, in his workshop in Porto, its artisans in the manufacture of each Rosior masterpiece. The brand has stores in Porto and Lisbon, at the Aviz s store, is the showroom of the most exclusive pieces of Rosior.

Cited already in 1389 as being of medieval origin, this square is thought to be one of the oldest in the city. A busy commercial centre, with tents serving as shops and a fish market, it came to the attention of João de Almada e Melo who set out to completely remodel the square in the 18th Century. The works carried out at this time by the Public Works Board, under the influence of John Whitehead, were financed by revenue from wine. Of his original plans, only the sides to the north, with its monumental Praça da Ribeira fountain, and the west were ever completed. The wall to the south was knocked down in 1821 and the medieval buildings along the east side survive to this day. Archaeological work in the 1980s led to the discovery of a 17th Century fountain. Reconstructed in its original location, it was crowned with a sculpture by José Rodrigues known popularly as the “Cube of the Ribeira”. 
On the 24th of June 2000, a statute of St. John the Baptist by João Cutileiro was unveiled in a niche in the Praça da Ribeira fountain. 
A must see, with lots of exciting nightlife on offer as well.
Included in the Urban Wine Route.
Silvermoon is part of the Rosior group and it is located in the Foz do Douro área and the latest 925 silver collections are available.
Traditional shop located in the Foz area, selling decorative items.

Founded in 1862, it is proud to have maintained its clients over several generations. 
Space located on Porto’ riverbank area, it offers different flavours of products which are uniquely Portuguese. 
Specialists in the sale of olives which continue to be the highlight of the shop. Other strong points are Port wines, table wines, olive oils, cheeses, sausages, jams, honey, dried fruits, preserves, traditional, homemade corn bread from Avintes, vegetables and made to order hampers.

Located in the Ferreira Borges market, this is an important building which demonstrates typical cast iron architecture and is considered as a firm point of public interest. 
It acts as a multidisciplinary stage aiming to offer access to all forms of art, from music, visual arts, modeling, cinema, publications and literature to socio-cultural activities, workshops and conferences, education, recording and tourist activities. 
It has function rooms for large events, recording studios, indoor and outdoor cafés, a lounge, bookshop, disco and merchandise shop.

Shop located in the Foz area, specialist in clothing for children aged from 0 to 8, it offers a wide range of products - interiors and exteriors - designed in accordance with the highest standards of quality.
Shop selling varied products, including Port Wine.
Handicrafts store where you can find Alentejo ceramics, tiles, costume jewellery, cast iron designs, wood carvings and Barcelos handicrafts.

Since March 2006, MUUDA has had an open space in Rosario Street, Porto. As part of the Miguel Bombarda Cultural Circuit, it has presented paintings, photography, illustrations, sculpture, street art and media exhibitions and interventions, through the active promotion of new artist´s work as well as puting on a schedule of exhibition openings.
Integrating a concept store, it includes pieces mainly by emerging and established Portuguese stylists and designers who regularly present their collections and display the items and accessories they have been developing. 
The programming of the space also includes events like book launches, project presentations, performances and a calendar of workshops and courses on various topics, always from the viewpoint of its concept that links art, design and flavours.

Located in the Foz area, ALI-JO sells clothing and fashion accessories, most of which are imported. The selection of pieces is carried out with much care so as to meet the specific tastes of each client.

Located in Porto City Centre for more than 100 years, BFG - Ferragens & Decoração is a shop full of charm, where tradition, style and history are always present. Hardware ranging from the rustic to the vintage, with handcraft ironmongery and style pieces for antique furniture or replicas.

Open since 1960, Armazéns Marques Soares are a reference in the city. 
They began by only selling cloth for clothing, knitwear and shirts.  
Over the years it has diversified the area of business and today has clothing for men, women, youths and children, lingerie, shoes, leather goods, sports goods, optics, home textiles and decoration, fabrics, perfumes, watches, electrical appliances and a restaurant.

Gio Rodrigues is a Portuguese designer based in Porto. 
He is known for his creativity in areas such as made-to-measure clothing, tailored items, brides, jewellery, and interior design among many other areas. 
Gio Rodrigues equals quality.

A concept store which brings together, in the same space, a fashion boutique, a type of ‘magazine shop’ and a coffee shop.

It dedicates itself to the man lifestyle, in a sophisticated and cosmopolitan environment, where you will find the authentic ‘one-stop-shop’ for the modern man, with clothing and accessories from well-known brands, books, CDs, selected perfumes, and also a space where they can enjoy breakfast or stop for a break and taste Mariage Frères tea and homemade pastry.

Stocks brands like Armani, Azzaro, Moschino, Marc Jacobs, Ben Sherman, Alessandrini, among others.

The imposing architecture and its central character turn this Avenue into the city s  living room . It becomes a location of excellence where the people of Porto gather to celebrate special times. There are granite buildings, many of which are crowned with skylights, domes and pinnacles.
It is marked by a large central square completely paved with granite cobblestones, where statues come to life and where the reflecting pool is worth highlighting. 
The City Hall is located at the head of the Avenida.

Created by Diogo de Macedo in 1930, the statue represents Afonso de Albuquerque with a daring look, holding a ship on his hands, symbolizing his fleet leadership.
Afonso de Albuquerque was born around 1462 and he was educated in the royal residence of D. Afonso V. In 1503 he was chosen to lead a trip to India, which took place only with D. Manuel II, by whom he was designated Viceroy of India. He conquered Ormus, Goa and Malacca and became known for his strong policy of land defense. He died in 1515, while returning from Goa to Portugal.

Building with bold forms and lines, intended to confirm the identity and values of Vodafone itself. It was designed by the architects José António Barbosa and Pedro Guimarães.

A white concrete building with a three-dimensional shape, cut by geometric lines that appear to intend on forming juxtaposed diamond shapes, some of which have been turned into huge windows, with a futuristic look.

Elected Building of the Year in the competition 2010 ArchDaily Building of the Year Awards, in the Institutional Category. During the same year, it was also considered one of the 20 most surprising and creative offices in the world by the site The Cool Hunter, in culture and design.

It has four open space upper floors, a ground floor with an auditorium, canteen and shop, and three underground floors, two of which are for parking and the other is for training, a technical area, storage and underground-shop.

Stone statue created by Vilar in 1971, depicting Garcia de Orta, Portuguese physician born in 1500 and graduated in the Spanish Universities of Salamanca and Alcalá.
In 1526 he started working as a doctor in Portugal and became a professor. In 1534, as a precaution, owing to his Jewish origin, though already a Catholic by that time, he moved to Goa, where he studied local plants and wrote a book considered to be a master piece of medicine: «Colóquios dos Simples e Drogas e Coisas Medicinais da Índia».

Brand of high quality jewellery in a modern and exquisite space where the jewels take centre stage as they are displayed on boxes that resemble rough diamonds, lacquered black with a red interior.  In fact, the black, red and gold form the palette of colours that fill the space, taking on a classic and timeless yet still warm and irreverent character.

With regards to the interior design, emphasis goes to the use of furniture pieces by Boca do Lobo and the Spanish designer Jaime Haydon.  
The trademark symbol, by symbolizing a gemstone, illustrates brilliance and nobility.

Children s theme park, for all the family but where the real  Little Kings  are of course the children.  
A place to dream and have fun, designed so that the little ones can build their own universe of fun, in healthy contact with other children of similar ages, allowing them to develop communication and social relationships and to develop their psychomotor.  
Thinking of parents, in addition to the cafeteria which has been designed for them, this space was put together according to the strictest safety standards, and all activities are constantly monitored by a group of qualified professionals.  
It also has an area available to the children where they are allowed to play at will, where you can organize and celebrate their birthday with their friends. This space can also be the stage for any other type of social events aimed at children.
Shop selling travel items, briefcases and leather products.
Shop selling several brands of original clothing.

Located near the main access points into Porto  A3, VCI, Circunvalação, Via Norte  and a few minutes from the São João Hospital and the largest campus of Porto University.

It offers 64 apartments with about 40 m2  with balcony or veranda and a totally equipped kitchenette , 6 rooms for meetings, restaurant, bar, business centre and 2 car parks  outside the building and another one inside - 8€/per day .

Accomplishing 30 years of history in 2013 and counting already five shops, Fátima Mendes is a pioneer in the divulgation of the main fashion brands in Portugal, representing brands as Céline, Chloé, Christian Dior, Lanvin, Gucci or Givenchy.It sells brands such as Gucci, Dolce Gabbana, Lanvin, Céline, Marc Jacobs, Burberry Prorsum, Givenchy, Marni, Chloé, Barbara Bui, DSquared2, Dior Homme, Citizens of Humanity, J Brand... among others.

Inaugurated in 1893, it is a rectangular construction with a single nave with religious neoclassical architecture, completed by a bell tower and two elongated lateral bodies.  
The main facade, as well as its interior, has blue and white tiles designed by Jorge Colaço.  
In its interior, focus goes to the plastered ceiling which is decorated with golden centred medallions.

A renovated country house that offers a beautiful panoramic view of the Douro river from its second floor.
Here, the diner can enjoy traditional dishes and appetizers at modest prices as well as meals prepared specially for coeliacs
Modern shop that has been open since 2007.  Representing the Dutch brand Geddes & Gillmore,  they offer casual clothing for both men and women.

Started in the fashion world in 1980 and since then has managed to maintain a style which fits in with what is going on in the main international catwalks.

The designs reflect love, humour and optimism through the extraordinary use of colours and shapes. This combination is consistent in all its objects: textiles, furniture, watches, plates, curtains and items for children.
Bar located near the Infante Bridge, mainly recommended for the bear community.

Top concept store providing a selection of clothing and accessories for men and women, with renowned trademarks such as Prada, Etro, Missoni, Miu Miu, Stella McCartney, Jil Sander, DKNY, Dsquared or Marnique.
The space, designed by the architect Eduardo Souto Moura, is minimalist inspired, decorated in a diaphanous and luminous way, thanks to its glass front. Besides exhibiting highly fashionable pieces in a perfumed ambience, and always to the sounds of lounge music, it also rouses the senses, with its perfumes, cosmetics and candles, and available music, including a spot for ipod audition.

Anjos Urbanos is a unisex space which is dedicated to hair care. Benefitting from a shiatsu massage on the comfortable chairs, this hairdresser s focuses on healthy textures, soft to the touch, with intense colours and shines.

The torso of Arthur Wellesley, Duke of Wellington, located near the Soares dos Reis National Museum  on the crossroads of Rua do Rosário and Rua D. Manuel II , is the city’s tribute to his heroic role in expelling the Napoleonic invasions during the Peninsular War.
Original, designer, vintage,  one of a kind shop.

Situated by the Ribeira tunnel, the Ribeira Negra  black Ribeira  is a sandstone mural which made by Júlio Resende and inaugurated in 1987.
This monumental panel represents the typical bustling of the urban riverside area.  The human and animal figures throughout the space depict the scenery of colour and light that are representative of this very area.

local: Building of the former Gaol and Court of Appeal
descricao:
Granite building, dating from 1582, rebuilt in 1767 in neo-classical style, according to the design of the architect Eugénio dos Santos, by initiative of João de Almada e Melo and financed by Companhia Geral da Agrigultura das Vinhas do Alto Douro. It presents a geometric sequence of windows  103 on the whole . This polygonal-shaped building has 4 façades, two of which are related to its main functions: the main façade, on Rua de São Bento da Vitória, allows you to enter the Court sector. The other façade, opened to the Cordoaria Garden, was formerly constructed for the direct passage of prisoners and is currently the building’s main entrance. 
Camilo Castelo Branco, who was one of Portugal s most famous writers and was charged with adultery as well as the famous Zé do Telhado who was charged with theft were kept here.
Between 1999 and 2002 the building was restored under the direction of Eduardo Souto de Moura and Humberto Vieira, to become the current Portuguese Centre for Photography, including an exhibition centre. 
Guided visits to the building are available.
Included in the Urban Wine Route.

Contemporary studios in a traditional house in the historical centre of Porto. The studios and art gallery are on the ground floor with the apartments being on the 3rd and 4th floors.   
Each studio has a fitted kitchenette and private bathroom.   
The rooms are equipped with towels, sheets, duvets and shower gel. You will also find salt, pepper, olive oil and vinegar for the kitchen.   
Check-in is from 5 p.m. to 9 p.m.  if you arrive earlier, you can leave your luggage for free .   
Late check-in  between 9 p.m. and 9 a.m.  requires an extra fee of €15.   
You should always notify of your arrival time.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.
Transport company located in the city of Maia providing a connection between the cities of Porto and Maia, among others.

Founded in 1948, it is an airline from Luxembourg, connecting the country to 23 European cities.   
It has a modern and comfortable fleet consisting of Boeing 737 s, Embraer Regional and Bombardier Q400 Jets.  
It is not just a business oriented company, it is also family oriented.  
On-board you may enjoy a new catering concept, based on fresh, quality ingredients.

Founded in 1957, it is the first Moroccan airline, based in Casablanca.  
It offers international flights from Morocco to Africa, Asia, Europe and North America, as well as domestic flights and charters.

local: PortoSigns - Produtos Turísticos e Culturais, Lda
descricao:
Portosigns is located in a building with more than 200 years, in ribeira do Porto: Rua da Alfândega nº 17 and Rua Infante D. Henrique, nº 71. The sale of products exclusively Portuguese is a must! The great National brands that it represents, make a lot of sense for the great quality linked to the “modern” tradition in products of daily use and highly designed.
The great Cork oak in one of our stores tells the cork’s story... and all the cork products of national quality are on display and in great variety: bags, wallets, belts, umbrellas, shoes, ties... and even pieces of kitchen use, aprons and ceramic pieces wrapped in cork that can go to the microwave, oven and dishwasher - colourful pieces and lots of contemporary beauty! The hand-painted ceramics is also a queen for the usefulness and variety of images and colours: colourful swallows and Santo António, plates, platters, bowls with sardines or tiles ... The Port wine shop and gourmet products based on Port wine, arise the curiosity of everyone: jellies, jams, marmalades and cookies.We also suggest a variety of tinned sardines and other specialties without forgetting the lamprey...
If you visit us, come in time because the huge products in our stores don’t stop here... you just have to see it!

French low cost airline, it was founded in late 1965 with the name Transavia Limburg N.V.

In 1991, the year it celebrated 25 years, Transavia became a subsidiary of KLM, an airline from the Netherlands.

Since December 2004, its business name changed from Transavia Airlines to transavia.com.

In 2006, it underwent restyling. The staff received new uniforms, designed by Broeckmans Fashion House, the aircraft adopted a new exterior look and all interiors were refurbished.

The fleet consists solely of New Generation Boeing 737 s.

In the summer it flies to around 90 destinations and in the winter it flies to about 35 destinations.

As a low-cost airline, and to keep fares low, customers only pay for the basic service. However, transavia.com has many additional options which can be used at the discretion of the customer, through extra payments.

Largest Canadian airline specialising in leisure travel.

It flies to 60 destinations in 25 countries, with a fleet of 18 A330 and A310 Airbuses.

Subsidiary of Transat A.T. Inc., which operates mainly in Canada and Europe, as well as in the Caribbean and in Mexico.

In 2011 it was selected by Facebook users as one of the top six airlines for family trips.

Low cost airline founded in 1995, it offers a safe and quality service at low prices.

It has been operating in Porto since June 2007.

It flies to most major tourist and business destinations in Europe.

Rates are economic given the reduction in all unnecessary costs and the earlier the booking, the lower the cost of travel.
Company providing cruises on the river Douro.

At the SIGHTSEEING TOURS SHOP you may buy the whole tourist offer existing in the city, namely regular and private guided tours, in Portuguese, English, French and Spanish.

The SIGHTSEEING TOURS SHOP aims to be the place in which the Tourist may find the whole tourist offer in the North of Portugal and in other regions as well.
Located in the city centre and known for its  Francesinhas . Also serving steak sandwich, steak with fries and  alheira   typical sausage .
Open since 1951, but now offering a totally renewed space.

6-floor, non-smoking hotel, with 56 rooms with air conditioning  small, standard and twin ,offering 24 hour reception service, 24 hour hot and cold drinks and snacks service, as well as luggage depositing. 
No animals allowed, except for guide dogs.
The Yellow Bus double-decker open-top buses allow you to discover Porto in an easy and comfortable way.

Centenarian House, founded in 1896.

When it opened its doors, that morning it received some of the wealthiest people in Porto who had stopped there to have breakfast before visiting the Bolhão Market. 

During this time, the establishment maintained its features, structures and original colours, having just introduced functionality details.
Today you can find a bakery and pastry shop with homemade goods,  frequently throughout the day , a customer service room,  where you are happily received  and in the other floor, a restaurant with menu service and daily menus.

Friendly atmosphere and a diverse range of quality products offered such as great customer service and helpful staff make this place into a unique space in Porto.

The Bouça Apartment Block, in the centre of Porto, by Rua da Boavista, was completed nearly thirty years after the construction of its first phase.
The project was commissioned by the Fundo Fomento da Habitação  Housing Development Fund  in 1973. After the Revolution of April 25, 1974, the people organized themselves into neighbourhood committees, framed by the state program – SAAL, Serviço de Apoio Ambulatório Local  Local Support Service . The second phase of the work was only resumed in the early years of 21st Century.
In the Bouça project, architect Siza Vieira kept the original design by recovering materials and covers from the two existing blocks. The missing trims and borders were then built as well as the coffee shop, the cooperative headquarters, the leisure studio and adding an underground car park.

A neoclassical building, built in the 19th century, according to a project carried out by the engineer Jean F. G. Colson. It has two facades, one facing the Douro river and one facing the city. Note the structural solutions that resorted to the use of iron in conjunction with other materials - stone, brick or wood - depending on the functionality of the different spaces. In 1987, it was decided to house the future Museum of Transport and Communications, in the building belonging to the Association with the same name. For this, restoration and adaptation works were carried out, according to the project by the architect Eduardo Souto Moura. The Association for the Transport and Communications Museum  Associação para o Museu dos Transportes e Comunicações-A.M.T.C  also manages a Congress Centre, spread across various areas in the Building of Alfândega Nova do Porto.

Praça da Ribeira was once separated from the Douro River by a section of the Fernandina wall where the main door connecting to the river was opened. Various roofs were opened for this square, and only one remained, on the east side, along the internal side of the wall.

Located near Praça da Batalha, it is known for quality pastries.   
Cakes are supplied from here to several cafes around the city, and birthday cakes or cakes for any other special occasion are made too. 
The Christmas period is very busy because of the “bolo-rei”  king s cake  and other typical cakes of this festive season.

Building No. 5 in the Rua de Baixo represents the oldest example of civil architecture from the medieval period to have survived in the Barredo neighbourhood. Its construction probably dates back to the 13th century.

The building with the no. 47 to 52 has the shield of D. João I on its facade. 
This was the house that the King gave to the merchants in 1402 to set up the first City Trade Exchange. 
On the ground floor, a passage was open to Casa da Moeda, where you can see the medieval structure in the interior.

Second head office of the General Company for the Agriculture of the Upper Douro Vineyards, where it stayed until 1961. It was originally located in the house at Rua Chã, belonging to the Figueiroa Pinto family.
It currently houses the Youth Foundation which moved in in 1989.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Documentally known since the 15th century, the tower belonged to descendents of Pedro do Sem, Chancellor of King D. Afonso IV, and was then passed onto the branches of the Brandões, who sold it to Mitra do Porto.
In spite of the various alterations it has gone through, the tower maintains a general medieval aspect. 
In 1919, the property was purchased by the Diocese of Porto, which set up its Episcopal palace here. 
In 1986, the tower was adapted to the residential area of the diocese, through a project by the architect Abrunhosa de Brito, fully transforming all and any medieval or modern structure which, until then, had survived. The building currently houses the SPES Foundation.

Built in the XVIII century by José Anastácio Guedes da Silva da Fonseca from Quinta da Aveleda, during the Siege of Porto  1833-34  this property was occupied by troops of D. Pedro IV who turned it into a hospital.
Its facades have the coat of arms representing the family Silva, Guedes, Almeida and Meireles.
