During the XX century, it was occupied by the Postal and Telegraph Services. 
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Project by Carlos Amarante, ordered by the General Company for the Agriculture of the Upper Douro Vineyards in 1806.
Laid on 20 pontoons, and despite its fragility, it was an indicator of progress for the region allowing rapid communication between the two riverbanks and facilitated the trade of goods and people. It had the peculiarity of being able to be dismantled and its 20 pontoons being moored to the riverside when the level of the river suddenly rose. After the destruction of the bridge by Napoleon s troops in 1809, during the French Invasions, it was rebuilt, in a productive time, but with 33 pontoons. 
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

First warehouse belonging to the General Company for the Agriculture of the Upper Douro Vineyards, it was founded in 1756 by Marquês de Pombal.
It was the first regulating body for the production and sale of Douro Wines.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Tour Operator in Portugal, specialised in group or private tours in Portugal, awarded with the “Best Wine Tourism Awards 2019.”, which honours the most important wine tourism agents, who stand out for the excellent service provided. In 2017, they won the  Best Tourism 2017  award from Turismo de Portugal.

From group trips to totally exclusive and personalized tours / itineraries, get ready to discover the best of Portugal, with an incredible trip, full of inspiring and enriching experiences.

These tours include all services, from the itinerary, guides, meals, transportation, accommodation and a Professional support available 24 hours a day.

local: Comissão de Viticultura da Região dos Vinhos Verdes
descricao: The Casa do Vinho Verde, home of the Viticulture Commission of the Vinho Verde Region - installed here since the mid-forties - is one of the richest examples of the Portuguese  that livwhen he returns to the city of Porto, in the middle of the XIX century. , the house was knowned as “the most luxurious” of Invicta, called Palacete Silva Monteiro at the time.

Located in one of the rooms in the Taylor´s cellars, it has been open to the public since 1994.
It has a spectacular panoramic view of Porto, in particular of its historical centre.
The commitment to the quality of the food and care with the service provided are the most favourable appreciations made by food critics.
“Port Wine Restaurant” offering a specific menu to pair Port Wine, and a rich Port Wine list with 30 references, with special mention for a wide set of options of Taylor’s, Fonseca, Croft and Romariz Vintages.

The opening of Rua Mouzinho da Silveira to connect the high and low areas of the city  creating a new hub for the distribution of traffic and the movement of goods  was financed by the Wine Levy in 1872. It is the road that links S. Bento station to the riverside area Ribeira, currently the target of recovery works on the houses which run along it and where varied commerce can be found.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

The name of the road pays homage to the memory of the Porto merchant who was responsible for the 1st commerce treaty between Portugal and England in 1353.

The popular expression  as smart as a garlic  which refers to someone who is lively and with a knack for business comes from his surname.

local: Largo da Igreja de Santo Ildefonso
descricao:
The redevelopment project of Largo de Santo Ildefonso, including the construction and improvement of the staircase and roads 31 de Janeiro and Santa Catarina, was approved by the Board of Public Works, in the XVIII century and financed by wine taxes.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Rua do Almada currently houses some bars and restaurants and several traditional and alternative commercial shops, located in old buildings, some of which have been recovered.   It follows the city centre, crossing various points of interest.
Opened by the Board of Public Works in 1764, with a project by Francisco Xavier do Rego and Francisco Pinheiro da Cunha, and driven by João de Almada e Melo, it follows the route of the ancient Roman road from Porto to Braga, having been financed by income paid at the rate of one real for every pint of wine consumed in the city.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Formerly known as Terreiro or Praça de S. Domingos, it was urbanised around 1320.

Due to its strategic position at the confluence of major arteries connecting the riverside to the high part of the city, throughout the centuries it underwent important conservation and maintenance works, especially the project by John Whitehead in 1774, conducted by the Board of Public Works and financed by wine taxes.

local: Current civil parishes of Vila Nova de Gaia
descricao:
The municipality of Santa Marinha e Mafamude, currently belonging to Vila Nova de Gaia, were connected to the city of Porto for the charging of taxes on the goods which entered and left the city.
Until 1934, the main wine warehouses were located here.
Part of the Urban Wine Route.

Tait House adapt its opening hour to the decreed state of emergency, but remain open with the due safety, hygiene restrictions and with the Clean & Safe accreditation:
- Tuesday to Friday: 10am to 5:30pm
- Saturday and Sunday: 10am to 1pm


Located on a long and pleasant green area protected by high walls, with a privileged view over the Douro River, it has retained a deep hallmark of the English families which inhabited it. 
It was bought in 1900 by William Tait, a Port Wine negotiator who had a considerable fortune and who dedicated himself to the study of fauna and flora. Muriel Tait followed William Tait, selling the house to the Porto’s City Hall, under the condition that it would be transformed into a public green space.
The building has a room where temporary exhibits are held, and the gardens are of free access.

650 metres long, Rua de Miguel Bombarda is one of the cultural arteries of modern times. With several art galleries and new commercial trends, it has become an obligatory meeting point in the city for all those who enjoy art and innovation.
New exhibits are regularly inaugurated simultaneously, with plenty of street entertainment, attracting art lovers, investors, artists, followers of alternative ways of life and many onlookers. 
The road also has restaurants, book shops and several retro-cool furniture shops, with alternative decoration, design, fashion, music and others.

Cultural association dedicated to animation film, born in 2001 with the support of Porto 2001 - European Capital of Culture.

It regularly holds movie screenings, develops training activities, exhibitions, conferences and meetings, and hosts various cultural initiatives.

One of its programmatic focuses is organising the Festa Mundial da Animação  World Animation Festival , an annual event that emerges as a celebration of International Animation Day, celebrated on October 28th. It is an international event that, in Portugal, has been celebrated by Casa da Animação since 2001 and in a travelling format, since 2013 when the Prémio Nacional da Animação  National Animation Award  joined this event.

Project by Teixeira Barbosa designed to market non-profit art. 
Initially projected to exist only virtually, meaning the exhibitions were only to be held online, however, a physical space was created.
Nowadays, the Extéril project is regarded as a piece/gallery which is constantly under transformation. 
The availability of the spaces, physical and virtual, is the responsibility of the gallerist as is the programming and advertising of the exhibits.
The connection of the physical space to the virtual space allows artists to exhibit their work and advertise it without any interference from
Extéril.

Located in the heart of old Porto, in the parish of Vitória, the São Bento da Vitória Monastery –designated a National Monument in 1977 – is one of the most important religious buildings in the city. It was once within the city’swalls, next to the Porta do Olival, occupying a part of the old Jewish quarter. Currently, it is part of Cordoaria, just near the former Gaol of the Court of Appeal, a building that now houses the Centro Português de Fotografia.

At the end of the 16th century, after a series of difficult negotiations, the monks of the old Portuguese Benedictine Congregation decided to build the Monastery as a sign of monastic presence and a support point for clerics who moved from North to South and vice versa. The city of Porto was, at the time, a hotbed of illustrious monks and the Monastery stood as a prominent monument due to its architectural grandeur and to the monks’ work, especially in terms of music and singing, of which they created a real school here, of which the imposing organ of the Church still shows. Granted in 1598 the necessary royal authorisation, the project was commissioned to the architect Diogo Marques Lucas, a former disciple of Filippo Terzi. Construction began in 1604 and lasted until the end of the century. The adjacent Church was built in 1693, but its interior decoration works were only concluded at the end of the 18th century. A drawn-out process, which is reflected on both the architecture, of Mannerist and Baroque typology, as well as in the Church s ornamentation, with pieces from different periods and of great significance in the context of Portuguese art history.

The foundation stone of the Noble Cloister was laid in 1608. A monumental building, built in granite, the cloister was completed in the 1725-1728 triennium. The magnificent monastic house would, however, have a troubled history. In 1808, during the Peninsular War, the Monastery was turned into a Military Hospital and, in 1835, after the Religious Orders’ expulsion, it became a Military Court and Prison, as well as a barracks for the 31st Infantry and Engineering Corps.

From 1985 to 1990, Instituto Português do Património Arquitectónico-IPPAR ordered the Monastery’s restoration works  overseen by architects Carlos Guimarães and Luís Soares Carneiro , maintaining the original design and various elements of architectural value, and preparing it to house Benedictine monks, along with the Orquestra Nacional do Porto and the Arquivo Distrital do Porto. Within the scope of Porto 2001 – European Capital of Culture, the Noble Cloister was covered by an acoustic shell, a metallic steel structure supported by four pillars, and got a wooden floor.

In 2007, the State conceded a significant part of the building to the Teatro Nacional de S. João  TNSJ  – the east wing, part of the south wing and Noble Cloister, which now hosts theatrical performances, concerts and special events of TNSJ’s programming, as well as hosting various events organised by outside institutions.

Mariana de Castro Art-Studio is the visual arts atelier of artist Mariana de Castro  FBAUP .
Located in the Co-Working
-SOU
-Mind Stiker
-Luís Castro Fernandes Arquitectura

Centre to support the creation of companies, consisting of 16 offices spread over 2 floors.  
The building is designed to accommodate new businesses in cultural and artistic areas, allowing the realisation of cultural events.

Contemporary art gallery with a well defined purpose: to present Portuguese contemporary art in a space within other space, where each exhibition will be a fusion of spaces with the possibility of emerging into a single space.

With the support of the Galeria Modulo, the artists and the Commissioner and friend Fátima Lambert, we have a project built so that it can grow from a space and value new aesthetic concept contributing to the interaction with new audiences within the space with already existing audiences, thereby promoting, and once again the true social inclusion, without any soppiness, but with feeling, aesthetics and human sensorial crosses, among all.

 Espaço t  wants Quase Galeria to further open its doors to the city as a starting point to create synergies of concepts, opinions and interactions between humans with the objective that everyone dreams of - happiness.

Neoclassical lighthouse with a hexagonal format, ten meters high, dated from 1886. It has a red balcony and lantern and a small annex building with plastered walls painted white.

The name  Molhe de Felgueiras  was given because it was built facing the stone of Felgueiras, to the West.

It was modernised in 1945 and automated in 1979, and was controlled from a distance from the Leça lighthouse. It underwent conservation works in the 90 s. Before being deactivated in 2009, the light has a range of 9 nautical miles, issuing a red light every 5 seconds.

It currently only has the sound signal.

Typical bourgeois house with a sunny characteristic which, facing west, has a wide view of the Douro River.
Its guests included city merchant families, the St. Bernard Congregation and the Porto English Club. 
To the side, the house s terrace is set on one of the towers of the old Fernandina wall. This tower, 15 metres tall, is visible from the Escadas do Caminho Novo and is a precious relic from the 14th century wall. 
Currently in the possession of the Portuguese State, it houses a social and charitable organisation, the Order of Malta.

Housed in the headquarters building of Altice Telecom, in Porto.
Espaço Tenente Valadim, of Fundação Altice, hosts every month an exhibition of different categories of Plastic Arts, Photography, Ceramic, Video Art, etc. Usually, the exhibitions are inaugurated on the first Thursday of each month.
It is a place of patronage of the arts run by Fundação Altice.

Church with a baroque design, it was built during the second half of the 18th century. 
The Recolhimento do Ferro initially located at Rua Escura was transferred here. According to an 18th century author, the name comes from an iron beam which crosses the door of the primitive church. It was located near the old jails and, according to tradition, the condemned who reached the iron were freed from death.

local: Palácio das Artes - Fábrica de Talentos
descricao: Situated in what remains of the Monastery of St. Domingos, it was founded in 1238 and hosted the Porto branch of the Bank of Portugal, the first assay in Porto, the space of plenary meetings of the Municipal Assembly, a meeting centre for merchants and the now extinct Companhia de Seguros Douro. The Palácio das Artes - Talent Factory is a project of the Youth Foundation which has 7 large rooms for exhibitions, workshops, seminars, events, the restaurant  DOP  owned by chef Rui Paula, A Loja das Tábuas and the Castelbel Shop.

Building where the Confraria de Nossa Senhora da Silva has operated since the 15th century. On the facade, on the first floor, there is an oratorio from the 18th century with the images of Nª Srª da Silva, S. João Baptista and S. Baldomero, protected by a wooden carved surface. 
Inside the building is the chapel of the brotherhood, which along with an inn, was administered by City blacksmiths, boilermakers and fish hook makers.

Quinta de Bonjóia, with approximately 40,000 m² and an XVIII century house  with lines attributed to the architect Nicolau Nasoni , surrounded by gardens and woods, provides visitors with a broad range of facilities, where you can organize and/or hold parties, dinners, conferences, meetings, promotional activities, training sessions, concerts, sporting events, radical sports and many other activities. 
The spaces include a restaurant/training centre equipped with an industrial kitchen and bar, a modern training centre, sports ring with changing rooms, conference and/or meeting rooms and Casa da Brincad Eira, especially designed for children and youths.
The head office of the Porto Social Foundation is located here, a non-profit private institution, whose mission is to  promote inclusion and social cohesion  in the county of Porto.
Building designated public interest monument.

In 1968, history was made because D. Heitor Gil de Vilhena made a pact with fado, thus appearing the cathedral of Fado in Porto, the Casa da Mariquinhas.

The Casa da Mariquinhas is located in the heart of the city, in the typical district of Sé, in front of the Arco de Santana, immortalized by Garret and at the foot of the Sé cathedral.

With 50 years of history this mythical site exudes fado by the pores of the granite walls. Through here they passed and continue to pass all the great names of the national fado, without microphones, with the greatest intimacy and proximity to the public, make the Casa da Mariquinhas the ideal place to enjoy the best fado that the Porto sings and plays,accompanied by the best traditional Portuguese gastronomy by the hand of the Chef. Sandra Santos and the best nectars of our Douro.

For those who come to Porto it is an inescapable visit to this establishment recognized as of historical and cultural interest.

During the pandemic, the chapel is only open for funerals, during the festivities in honour of Nossa Senhora do Ó  19 September  or by appointment.


Former chapel of Nº Srª da Piedade or Cais, its gets its name because the image of Senhora do Ó was transferred here from the Porta da Ribeira Chapel, which was demolished in 1821. The building dates back to the 17th century and was remodelled in the 19th century after the destruction which occurred during the wars of independence.

Inside, focus goes to an altarpiece carving from the early 18th century, by João da Costa.

Wrong Weather is a fashion and lifestyle store and web site for contemporary men. Opened since September 2009, the store is located in Avenida da Boavista 754, in Porto, next to Casa da Música. The design concept of WW was distinguished with the award Best Design Store by Fashion TV Portugal. Our eclectic brand selection features names such as Kenzo, Maison Margiela, Rick Owens, Juun.J, Ambush, Champion, Raf Simons, Common Projects, Damir Doma, Dust, J.W. Anderson, Mykita, Y-3 by Yohji Yamamoto, Stephan Schneider, Unravel Project, Cottweiller, Yezzy... WW is not just a store  it is also an Art Gallery, having already exhibited works by artists such as Pierre Debusschere, Mustafa Sabbagh and Bruce LaBruce.

Classified as property of public interest, Villar d Allen is a rare survivor of the recreation farms that in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries surrounded the city of Porto. Property of João Allen, an English businessman, it features a building of eclectic architecture, typical of the Romantic period of the nineteenth century. In addition to a large collection of camellias, Villar d Allen also has a formal garden  à la française , and the “parterre” born of the enthusiasm for  landscape  gardens in fashion in England. The romantic nature was accentuated with the enlargement of the gardens, creating lakes, streams and artificial waterfalls and a wide variety of exotic plants. It also features sculptures by Nasoni. It is now a plant nursery of outdoor ornamental plants. In addition to guided tours, Villar d Allen also organizes family, cultural or business events.

The Farggi concept was born from the idea of producing high quality ice cream to be savoured in a unique and enjoyable environment.  
Here you can find ice cream  made with milk  and ice lollies  with ice  to which you can add any number of toppings, hot or cold, from chocolate to almonds and walnuts, raspberry coulis to whipped cream and fresh fruit.   
All this can be combined with tarts, pancakes, waffles, coffee, milk, and orange juice.   
Exclusive to this ice cream shop are the caipirinhas, mojitos, margaritas and vodka, all made with ice cream.

Gelataria Sincelo is the oldest ice-cream parlour of Porto to remain in its place of origin. With more than 35 years, it was renewed about two years ago, and now appears with a makeover, with a new image, but with the same quality. It offers daily new handmade flavours created by the house’s master ice cream maker.

For those who enjoy tea, this is a hotspot, with more than 300 varieties of teas and sui generis decor.   
The entrance is a room with Indian decor and, further on, a door leads you to a chillout style exterior with a garden with Moroccan influences, ending in a shop with oriental objects, clothes, exotic furniture and colourful accessories. Upstairs there is a restaurant, in a room also decorated with oriental elements and bold colours, where you can enjoy a buffet lunch during the week and themed dinners at the weekend  Fridays and Saturdays .

Saldanha Desgin House is a loft styled private apartment that lodges up to 5 people. The kitchen has dish washer and washing machine. br 
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

InterContinental Porto – Palácio das Cardosas, heritage and authenticity right in the heart of the city. Settled on a palace from the 18th century with a privileged view over the splendid boulevard Aliados Avenue and close to all the main cultural highlights such as the Bolhão Market, the Ribeira or the Church of Clérigos, the Palácio das Cardosas is the perfect place for those wishing to know the true essence of Porto.


Valet parking: 29€/per day
Restaurant located in the City Centre, in the busy street “Rua de Santa Catarina”.

The iconic Café Astória, part of the city’s history, has become the elegant Restaurant Astória. Open to the city and offering a ravishing view over the Aliados Avenue, here one may discover a typical Portuguese cuisine with a contemporary flair in which local products play a prominent role. Over lunch, our chef proposes three menus – Menu Express  12,00€/person, couvert+dish of the day+drink+coffee , the Executive Menu  17,00€/person, couvert+ entrée or dessert+dish of the day+drink+coffee , the Aliados Menu  couvert+entrée+dish of the day+dessert+drink+coffee .
A centennial traditional shop located in the city centre, specialized in products such as cod, Port Wine, cheese, traditional sausages, regional bread.

Restaurant located in the Ribeira area, Ribeira Quay, by the D. Luís I Bridge. It offers a cosy environment with a nice view over the River Douro. The restaurant specialties are codfish, octopus, fish, seafood, cheeses, smoked sausages and Port Wines.

A shop dedicated to those who love second-hand and collection items. Here you can find rare and old books, old and modern advertising posters, pottery of renowned brands, and many other objects, mostly original and in good condition.

Innovative concept for a cocktail bar, whose name was inspired by the wallpaper which lines the walls.  RETRO  decoration in all the objects which are on sale for those who value unique pieces.  
The music is varied and fits in with the atmosphere of the space, ranging from the revival of the 80 s to jazz and Bossa Nova.  
It also works as an art gallery, where, in addition to books, you can find classic games like chess, dominoes and even mikado.

Dedicated to francesinhas, it belongs to Filipe Pereira, son of the owner of the famous Café Santiago, located metres further down.  
The letter F refers to the son, Filipe, as well as to francesinha.
The opening of this space was a way to create his own business, but also to help the mother to dispose of some of the clients they usually get.
Restaurant serving Portuguese traditional food, located near Praça Carlos Alberto.
Restaurant offering a great variety of typical Portuguese food.

local: Comércio do Porto by Oporto Tourist Apartments
descricao:
For stays of medium and short duration, these 7 apartments are located in the historical centre, just 100 metres away from the River Douro.  
The buildings were recently renovated, combining typical architecture and the charm of old buildings with the comfort of a modern apartment.  
They have various facilities and services, which vary depending on the chosen apartment: air conditioning, wi-fi, kitchen with stove, refrigerator, microwave, toaster  bathroom with towels, shampoo and shower gel, hair dryer and iron on request, among others.
Check-In between 15:00 and 18:00 / Check-Out until 11.00.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

Located at the top of the HF Ipanema Park, and inspired by the New Yorker cosmopolitan concept of using the terraces of grand buildings, it has a magnificent view over the mouth of the Douro river and the city. Committed to innovation and exclusivity, in an elegant, trendy and yet relaxed and informal atmosphere, it offers a diverse programme, from Clubbing Parties to Fado nights and Bossa Nova. On specific dates, pool and sunset parties are held in the afternoon, in parties that go through the night. The name Zenith appears in the Middle Ages and, in general, it simply means  the highest point .

Founded in the 1950s and renovated in 2016, it has 26 rooms.  
Located near Torre dos Clérigos and in the nightlife area.  
It has a free continental breakfast, internet, laundry and luggage deposit.

A gathering place with a unique atmosphere of fun and relaxation, strongly committed to House Music, presenting a new aesthetic concept of night life: elegance, glamour, excitement and lots of emotion. The More Club is settled since July 2010 on a classified building in the city centre, consisting of three floors:  the ground floor is the disco with a more underground scene, a sort of industrial-chic with a stunning ceiling height of 5.40 metres  on the first floor there’s the lounge and there is still a basement with a more intimate and alternative atmosphere and sound. Mirrors make up much of the decor, exuberantly combining several styles.
House music dominates the environment and invites you to dance.

local: Centro de Cultura e Congressos - Ordem dos Médicos
descricao:
It has a wide range of facilities for the carrying out of professional and socialising activities, as well as various green spaces for leisure which can be enjoyed mainly by doctors but which are also available to any other non-medical entities.  
The rooms are prepared to provide the best conditions for the holding of scientific meetings, conferences, courses or congresses, as well as social gatherings, ceremonies, weddings and others.   
The Main Hall and adjacent areas have been used to hold cultural events and art exhibitions.   
Within the head office, there are two tennis courts and a swimming pool.

The street named “Rua Cândido dos Reis” is located near the Clérigos Tower and the Lello Bookshop. This area is known for its night life, offering several bars and restaurants. Different kinds of shops can also be found throughout the surrounding streets.

Monument designed by Lagoa Henriques, erected in 1960 as part of the commemorations of the Fifth Centenary of the death of Infante D. Henrique. It symbolizes the gratitude to the men of the shipyards and the people who built, gave supplies to and manned his 1st armada  1415 . These facts are represented in the group of bronze sculptures formed of three figures. In the background, there is a stone wall where the sea routes of the Portuguese are drawn and on the opposite side, we have the transcription of the  Capítulos Gerais da Cidade do Porto nas Cortes de 1436 .

Car park located in Porto historical centre, with car access by Rua Trindade Coelho and pedestrian accesses by Praça Central do Quarteirão das Cardosas, Praça Almeida Garrett  São Bento Station  and Rua Trindade Coelho  Largo dos Lóios .
https://www.dreampills.pt/collections

local: Lugar do Desenho - Júlio Resende Foundation
descricao:
With a collection of roughly two thousand drawings the painter Júlio Resende gathered along its career, which began in the 30s, the Lugar do Desenho – Fundação Júlio Resende was created.

This is a renowned private institution of public utility.

Simultaneously to the exhibition of a collection always held in the space thought out for that purpose and accessible to the public, there are temporary exhibitions, concerts, conferences, specialized seminars and occasional courses or workshops in the topics studied by the institution.

Restaurant located by the sea or with a decoration of light colours and modern lines.
Appetizers, fish, meat or vegetarian dishes and a selection of sweet food invite you for a special lunch or dinner, paired with an excellent wine list.

Started its activity in 1953, having moved to its current headquarters in 1967. Due to its unique field, it is mentioned in several publications and has been present in exhibitions and fairs of design, architecture and furniture. Along its technical history, it has created its own lines in partnership with several renowned Portuguese architects such as Siza Vieira and Souto de Moura.

Located in the building of the Coliseu do Porto, it is currently located at the old Salão Jardim Passos Manuel, the name which was afterwards adopted by this small studio. The movie theatre Passos Manual opened in 1971 and it was refurbished and equipped with a new projection and sound system in 1997.Nowadays, it is explored by Medeia Filmes, and it possesses a quality-oriented cinematographic program.The movie theatre Passos Manuel invests in the exhibition of alternative and independent movies, giving a new life to the cinematography scene in Porto.

Divided into three small floors, it has always tried to maintain the memory of a building that dates back to the 17th century, through the used materials, the objects and the decor, such as the floor imitating old tiles, the pieces in iron and the slate board, where the menu suggestions can be read. With incidence in game dishes, the menu is inspired in a traditional kitchen of Iberian influence and offers some original suggestions, such as cecina  cow ham  salad.

Cosme is located in the centre of Porto, next to the Faculty of Fine Arts, in a renovated mansion house from the nineteenth century. Featuring elegant accommodation, it has a contemporary design and hosts exhibitions of Portuguese artists. Its rooms combine the building’s elegant architecture with modern furniture. All rooms have free Wi-Fi, a living area and cable TV. The little library of the Cosme Guesthouse offers books related to Portuguese culture. Guests can relax in the living which has a small bar and a TV. Port Wine degustation is also available. A hot breakfast buffet is served daily. The backyard garden is the perfect place to socialise with the other guests. On a short walking distance there is a wide choice of restaurants.

Located in the centre of the city in a building from the late 19th century.
It has 4 rooms: one with a capacity for 8 people, another with a capacity for 6 people and two double rooms.
It has free Wi-Fi internet service in the rooms and in the lobby.

Shoe store where known trademarks like Luís Onofre, Tommy, Fly London, Miss Sixty, Hugo Boss, Vibram, etc… prevail.

Full of Glamour and life, Marmi is a store where we can find all types of shoes for woman, men and children, as well as their new product, the Vibram Fivefingers.

This product allows you to experiment the benefits of walking barefoot with the protection of a thin Vibram sole, allowing you to strengthen your feet while you enjoy your favourite activities like climbing, running, weight training, fitness, kayaking, sailing or doing yoga.

Shoe shop which has existed for more than 50 years, located in the exclusive zone of Aviz.
It has quality footwear collections from Portuguese and Italian brands of reference.

Inaugurated in 1855, the Cemetery of Agramonte was reorganized in the 1870’s hosting several grand buildings, namely its chapel, upon a project by Eng. Gustavo Adolfo Gonçalves e Sousa.

The presbytery was later increased in 1906 under the management of Marques da Silva, which was when the byzantine inspired paintings by Silvestro Silvestri were made. Highlight goes to the various mausoleums which have sculptures by Soares dos Reis, António Teixeira Lopes and Alves Pinto.

In the municipal sections there are various art personalities from Porto who have been buried here, such as the painter António Carneiro, the violinist Guillermina Suggia, the photographer Emílio Biel or Tomás Soller.


September 1st and 2nd: 8:30am to 6pm

By the eighteenth century, the British colony in Porto proceeded to burials in the bank of the river Douro, during the low tide. After many negotiations, in 1788, the consul John Whitehead managed to acquire land for the construction of a decent cemetery, with the condition of having a fence with high walls. However, only in the 1820’s the cemetery received its first monuments. 
With a romantic environment the cemetery has among its occupants many from the very well-known foreign families connected to the Porto wine trade. Highlights go to the mausoleums of Eduardo Moser, the Consulate John Whitehead, Feuherheard, Kebe, Jebb and Brindle and to the little steles of the Baron of Forrester and of the family Ktazenstein.
As it is closed to the public it is necessary to ring the bell, preferably between 09:00 and 16:00.

Urban and sophisticated space, with decoration which reflects on scenic arts giving continuity to the hotel in which it is located.
The refined cuisine of Mediterranean flavours, together with decorative and creative details, gives it a unique character.
Restaurant “Port Wine Ambassador”, selecting the adequate Port Wine to pair your meal.

ML Private Tours specializes in creating unique travel experiences for clients that are seeking out the best of what Portugal has to offer, particularly in regards to the best wine and gastronomy.

We provide a more comfortable and personal way to travel for a demanding clientele that expects exclusivity with special services and unique experiences.

We serve you with the utmost attention to detail and will adapt the experiences we offer to each client demand .

Located in a restored building from the 19th century, it has an artistic decoration and a family environment.
It has 5 double rooms with bathroom and 1 room with 4 beds and bathroom.
Free internet service and Wi-Fi internet service available throughout the house.
Breakfast included in the price.

The restaurant located in the wine hotel The Yeatman stands out thanks to its gastronomic programme and its inclination for the culture of enjoying wine, highlighting the wine dinners programme.
The excellence attributed to The Yeatman, which was awarded two Michelin Star, includes its cellar which contains more than 25,000 bottles and has more than 1,300 Portuguese and international wines.
Overlooking the Douro River, with a wonderful view of the city of Porto, it offers a classic, comfortable environment where you can enjoy a sophisticated and creative cuisine.
Restaurant “Port Wine Ambassador”, selecting the adequate Port Wine to pair your meal.

Located in the area of Boavista, it is a modern and cosmopolitan space of men s fashion, characterised by its versatility.
Some of the best international brands such as Paul Smith, Comme des Garçons, Martin Margiela, Neil Barrett, Raf Simons are represented here. It also sells a selection of perfumes, candles, limited edition trainers, magazines, leather accessories, bracelets and rings. The shop window is also an attraction, accompanying each change of the collection with an original and unique design which captures the attention of passers-by.

Inaugurated in 1839 as the first public cemetery in Porto, it is considered a true `death museum`. Porto’s funeral art is very specific, mainly with neo gothic characteristics, using granite and very monumental. From various constructions of historic and artistic value, the following can be highlighted: the cemetery chapel, the charnel house of the nuns from the old Convent of S. Bento de Avé-Maria, the cemetery cross with a Christ in iron, as well as remarkable graves and mausoleums of varied relevant people.


November 1st and 2nd: 8:30am-6pm

Space of colour and soul dedicated exclusively to the creation of traditional and contemporary objects. br 
In this shop/atelier you will always find a unique piece that will awaken your dreams and show you a little of Porto s soul. Adosinda Pereira, who has a degree in Art History, dedicated to ceramics since 1990 and in 2003 opens her atelier/shop to the public, where she produces her work.

Founded in 1994, its main objective is the international affirmation of a new generation of emerging artists, representing the current trends of contemporary art.  
The artists present a number of projects, alternatively suggesting a concept for collective work, to be represented during one season in several events. These projects include various genres: painting, drawing, sculpture, installation, video, photography and sound.
As part of a cultural exchange, exhibitions were held in partnership with several European galleries, having also participated in several art fairs.

Caldeirão da Cati opened its doors in April 2004 and although being a small shop is now able to gather the best of Portuguese ceramics.
In Ribeira do Porto, right next to the Luiz I Bridge, we can find in this yellow house the black clay from Molelos, the Red clay from Barcelos, the faience from Coimbra, the clay with inlaid stone from Nisa, the faience from Alcobaça and the pottery from São Pedro do Corval of Alentejo. You can also get acquainted with the traditional Galo de Barcelos  Rooster of Barcelos  and its legend, the Azores  certified embroideries, not to mention the traditional porcelain doll, the shawls from Minho and the famous filigrees.

There are several mouth-watering moments throughout your stay at Pestana Palácio do Freixo and you can be sure that each one has a touch of the all-day dining Nasoni bar, which you can also taste at the poolside bar and the delicacies you can enjoy in the excellent restaurant Palatium. These three places are perfect to cater to you throughout the day - and night. The flavours find their essence, in this place which favors the local products and respects their origins. But the greatest luxury you will find here is undoubtedly the incredibly attentive and present service, always ready to give you new suggestions and paves the way to new flavours and experiences.

local: Galo Louco – Gourmet, Arts and Crafts
descricao: Shop featuring handicraft, typical and gourmet products, located in the historical centre.

Housed in a restored 19th-century building in the historic centre of Porto, Ag Jewels sells gold articles specializing in the art of filigree. It’s possible to find in this traditional Portuguese jeweller’s: rings, earrings, medallions, brooches, necklaces, chains, bracelets, slave bangles, among others articles, being sales representative of the following watch brands: Seiko,  Furla, Fossil, Cerruti, DKNY, Esprit, Puma, Watch & Co, and Agatha Ruiz de la Prada, Lotus. All pieces are in Sterling Silver and upon order can be executed in standard gold of 19,2kt. The pieces revive the Portuguese tradition and culture, giving it a contemporary touch. br 
Every Saturday it’s possible to watch the art of the filigree, by the hands of a Portuguese artisan. br 
 Visit us and know a typical Portuguese art. br 
Ag Jewels, always thinking of you...

Integrated in The Yeatman Hotel, the spa evokes the spirit of wine, in perfect consonance with this wine hotel where the pleasures of wine can be enjoyed in all its aspects. 
The spa has ten therapy rooms where various treatments are performed, as well as complementary services such as an indoor panoramic swimming pool, sauna, Turkish bath and relaxation room with a magnificent view of the Douro.

Project that aims to give notice to and trade works of different authors with a unique selection of illustrations, drawings, books fanzines and design pieces. br 
The following authors participate in this project: Mariana, a Miserável  Julio Dolbeth  Rui Vitorino dos Santos  Hélia Aluai  Leonor Zamith  Tamara Alves  Luzia Brandão  Skinkape  Yara Kono  Maja B.K.  Sara Louise Tucker  Gemma Correl  David Penela  Wasted Rita  André da Loba  Rita Roque  Dylan Silva  Lara Luís  Ivo Hoogvelt  Mariana Malhão  Paula Bonet  Maria Herreros  Mariana Rio  Catarina Gomes  Luis Cepa  Susa Monteiro  Aurea Praga  Joana Pinto  Helena Perez Garcia  Ana Melo aka Amalteia  Rafaela Rodrigues  Padure  Carolina Celas  José Roda  Cachete jack  Tina Siuda  Alexandra Ramires  Noa Snir  Aitch  Amelie Fontaine  Susana Carvalhinhos  Inês Coias  Daniel Moreira  Silvia Rodrigues  Ricardo Cavolo  Bárbara Fonseca  Elisa Talentino  Yara Kono  Inma Lorente  Virginie Morgand  Eleonora Arosio  Emmanuel Kerner  Tiago Galo  Sara Cunha  Inês Costa  Adolfo Serra  Laura Liedo  Ester Garcia  Chloé Perarnaud  Karol Banach  Barbara Rocha  Laura Castelló  Joana Estrela  Cátia Vidinhas  Ana Seixas  Barbara Fonseca and Min.

The Cemetery of the Brotherhood of Nossa Senhora da Lapa is the oldest Portuguese romantic cemetery. The mausoleums settled here by the noble families whose members are buried here turn this cemetery into a “museum on death”, displaying funerary art from the 19th century. It holds the tombs of renowned people, such as the writers Arnaldo Gama, Camilo Castelo Branco and Soares de Passos, the architects and artists Marques da Silva and Marques de Oliveira, the bishop D. Manuel de Santa Inês, and the industrial José Ferreira Borges, among many others. In 2013 it was classified as Estate of Public Interest.

local: São Domingos by Oporto Tourist Apartments
descricao:
For stays of medium and short duration, these 7 apartments are located in the historical centre, just 500 metres away from the Douro River.   
This building was completely renovated, combining the traditional architecture and charm of old buildings with the comfort of modern apartments.
There are 4 studio apartments of 21m², 26m² and 35m² and 3 1-bedroom-apartments ranging from 40m² to 44m².
They have a wide range of equipment and services, which vary depending on the apartment chosen: air conditioning, wireless internet, wi-fi, television with access to several national and international channels, kitchenette, bathroom with towels, hair dryer and iron, among others upon request.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

Located near the City Park and the beach, the Oporto Excentric Hostel is a thematic hostel, with rooms decorated according to various points of interest in the city such as the Fundação de Serralves, Casa da Música, the City Park, Casa da Animação, S. Bento Station, Cafe Majestic, the Ardina etc.  
It has heating available in the bedrooms, kitchen, terrace with sofas in the summer, also including a self-service breakfast.  
It has various services such as bike, car and scooter rental, the organization of guided tours in a Mini, boat trips, surfing and kite-surfing lessons and other sports and adventure activities  www.oportocamps.com , and it also provides discounts in some surf shops, museums, restaurants, circuits, etc.

local: Maus Hábitos - Espaço de Intervenção Cultural
descricao: We can start with the name- Maus Habitos. Maus Habitos - a cultural intervention to be socially expressive and fecund, so that you don’t exhaust yourself in the mere decorative dimension, you can’t be “well behaved”. You have to be innovative, subversive and transgressive.  Maus Habitos-it’s a space which doesn’t want to be defined. It hasn’t got an Italian stage, but it offers conditions which welcome concerts and plays. It’s not a gallery, but arranges appropriate spaces to receive exhibitions, and maintain an artistic and active programme. It’s not an atelier but it develops residential programmes and artistic companionship just as practical as theoretical. It’s not a club, but it enjoys parties and drinks in the evening. It’s not a traditional kitchen, but it likes to eat well and experiment new flavours and create new dishes. Yes, it is an alternative platform and a professionalized experiment. Yes, it is a space of informalities, artistic collaborations and cultural convergence. It’s a cultural producer, with an artistic conscience of intervention, production and presenting artistic programmes which act as contemporary conditions of culture. It is an open urban space, bastard and emblematic of the city.

local: Maus Hábitos - Espaço de Intervenção Cultural
descricao:
Place that seeks to integrate heterogeneous public in urban environments, exploring new ways in the production of artistic content.
Informal environment where the cultural intervention is one of its strengths.   
With a 160m² hall and a concert hall of 100m², it is a space dedicated to musical events.  
Over its 10 years of operation, celebrated in 2011, it has held more than 1600 concerts.

National rent a car network, modern and competitive licensed for the private, company and tourist market.
It has a large offer of different kinds of vehicles, with rental ranging from 1, 2, 7 or more days, weekend, monthly and annual rental. 
It has a fleet of passenger, executive vehicles available, plus minibuses, vans, pick-ups, etc., petrol or diesel  Diesel engine  and optional extras  insurance and accessories .
The service may include delivering and collecting the vehicles.
Located near the airport Francisco Sá Carneiro.

A space that preserves the tradition of the old tea rooms where ladies from the high bourgeoisie used to gather, with a matching style and atmosphere. Inside or at the terrace, it is possible to enjoy bonbons, biscuits, nuts, Port Wine, marmelada  quince cheese , jelly, macarron de Paris, scones, Bolo Rei and other pastries.

local: Museu da Faculdade de Belas Artes da Universidade do Porto
descricao:
The Faculty of Fine Arts Museum of the University of Porto was officially created in 1992, after the Superior School of Fine Arts was included in the University of Porto. The Museum’s main goals are to preserve and study the Faculty’s Art collection and to establish the Museum as an artistic and cultural institution not only inside the University but also locally, nationally and internationally. 

The Museum organises temporary exhibitions, open to the public, at the Pavilhão de Exposições, a building that is part of FBAUP s architectural ensemble, designated a Monument of Public Interest.

In the garden, one can see sculptures from various eras and authors.

The Douro Marina represents a new concept of a marina that combines sports, leisure, tourism and nature in an innovative set of services and activities, not only for those who travel the Atlantic, but also for locals and visitors in general.  
The dock has a capacity to receive and accommodate 300 vessels with lengths of up to 20 metres.   
The marina has a petrol station, a workshop for the maintenance and repair of vessels, a portage ramp for the entry and exit of boats and a suction system for sewage and oily waters.  A travellift with a capacity of 50 tons and a harbour crane with a 3-ton-capacity are also available for travellers.

Following the national policy imposed by the Estado Novo, the buildings surrounding the Cathedral, the Casa do Cabido and the Bishop´s Palace, were demolished in 1940.  A paved terrace was built instead, where a stone monument representing local government  pelourinho  was placed. The monument, which was constructed 1945, was reproduction of a drawing dating back to 1797.

Sushi restaurant with a simple but elegant décor. With a minimalist architecture of contemporary features, the various references to Japanese culture instil this space with its own personality. The contrast of colours divides the space available into several areas, including restaurant, sushi bar and take-away service.

Born in 1955, it wasn´t until 1992 that she pursued her true passion. Following her lead lead, Portuguese and international jewelers came together for a series of workshops and conferences, and later founded the Porto Jewellery Centre.
Since then she has continued her work,  organizing and participating in various exhibitions and fairs, promoting contemporary jewellery. She was honoured for her work in 1999 with the title of Officer of the Merit Order by the president of the Portuguese Republic.

Located on the top floor of Porto Palácio Congress Hotel & Spa, it offers a unique panoramic view of the city, river and sea. br 
Perfect to enjoy a late afternoon with a cup of tea or coffee, Porto Palácio VIP Lounge also has an international list with more than two hundred selected whiskeys, as well as a wide range of coffees, teas and Porto Wines.

With modern and welcoming aspect, this is the first gourmet store specialising in candy, from which we highlight the cupcakes, macaroons, cookies and custom cakes.  
It also offers various complementary products such as jams, marmalades, honey teas, liqueurs, chocolate vodka, champagne and selected wines.

Space specialised in high-end watches and jewellery, with representation of prestigious international brands such as Rolex and Montblanc.  
The new AquaVerdi shop is the brand’s second, being located in the Palácio das Cardosas, a magnificent building from the 18th century.
In this space, which has total consideration for the surrounding architecture, the shop windows showcase the luxurious collections of Rolex and Montblanc, in addition to the exclusive AquaVerdi jewellery.  
Once inside the shop, the corners of the brands were thought out to enhance the pieces handled with the care of the ancient goldsmiths and the masters of the best Swiss watchmakers, collections that highlight technical and aesthetic innovation.
Apartment located in the heart of the historic centre of Porto, in a centennial building renovated and equipped to offer visitors a comfortable stay.

The Local Accommodation Grande Oceano is a place that offers temporary accommodation services in a family environment located in the centre of Porto.
Located in a renovated and traditional building in the city, it has 15 rooms with private bathroom facilities with a shower, colour TV, air conditioning and free wireless internet.

Mezzanine tailors for those who look for up-to-date, sophisticated and exclusive style.  It has a wide range of menswear and womenswear of innovative design and quality, along with international fashion brands such as Sita Murt, Religion, Ben Sherman, Lee 101, Insight, Gsus, Nobrand, Guava and Dr Martins.

Mercearia das Flores has a fresh and friendly atmosphere. It sells traditional Portuguese products from cheeses, sausages, olives and wine to jam, biscuits and honey. There are tables to sit in, so you can try the food in the shop.

Renovated eighteenth century house renovated with traditional construction methods. It has a 4 person 70m² duplex apartment and a 35m² studio apartment with a 2 person capacity.
A minimum stay of 2 nights  studio  and 3 nights  dulpex  is required.

The University of Porto Shop offers a huge range of products with the university logo. In this shop you can also find a set of exquisite items designed by Portuguese designers but also pieces by well known brands. It also offers a vast catalogue with hundreds of works by editors from the University of Porto, the Calouste Gulbenkian Foundation and other editors.

Sent to be constructed by the Alvo Brandão Perestrello Godinho family, whom were heirs to the Viscounts of Basemão, it was remodeled in the XIX century, by the Counts of Trinidad and in 1849 welcomed the exiled King Sardenha e Piemonte, Carlos Alberto de Saboia. Property of the Local Council of Porto since 1959, temporary exhibitions have been arranged as well as areas for different events to be held.

Gallery settled in an old house from 1904, now recovered and enlarged according to the project by the architect Óscar da Silva Lopes, aims at showing and spreading contemporary artistic creation, through different artistic expressions, as well as supporting and encouraging new artists and their creations.

Combining true bohemian style with sophistication, Champanheria da Baixa is a sort of chic tavern- a new and unique that will appeal to those who like to enjoy a glass of champers, whatever the occasion.

Located in the north part of the city, with an approximate area of 60,000 m², Quinta da Prelada belongs to Porto s Santa Casa da Misericórdia.
The property is considered one of the greatest works of Nasoni in terms of landscape architecture, and the Casa da Prelada, also designed by the Italian architect Nicolau Nasoni, is classified as property of public interest.  
The property aims to reconcile different aspects: culture, leisure, entertainment and sports.

The Pastoras beach has a Blue Flag in addition to a Golden Beach award. The beach is delimited by the Molhe de Felgueiras and the Molhe Norte  these coastal landforms protect bathers from the wind, giving this beach unique characteristics. From here, where the Douro meets the Atlantic, it is possible to take two paths: one to the North, where the stylish and cosmopolitan area of Foz begins, or one to the East, towards the romantic garden Passeio Alegre. The beach’s name comes from bygone days when shepherdesses brought their lambs to graze here and smell the sea air.

Upababy dedicates itself to renting online baby equipments, being possible to deliver and collect the equipment wherever the client desires. The Upababy portfolio includes wheeled goods, car seats, carriers, toys, travel cots, bouncers, highchairs, carrycots, among others.The equipments available for rent are always delivered in the best conservation conditions and are disinfected with products suitable to be used by children.

Tucked away on Rua do Loureiro, its homemade pastries at the shop window will leave you wide eyed with amazement.

And inside there are more wonders- on the ceiling there is a painting by famous Portuguese painter, Acácio Lino. The building was designed by Francisco Oliveira Ferreira, pupil of Marques da Silva. The solid iron staircase takes you up to a set of sculptures on the first floor that are signed by the architect´s brother, José de Oliveira Ferreira.

It is a must-go place for tasting bolas de Berlim, which are famous for its quality and generous size, but also for tourists who like to discover the charms beyond its facade.

Space dedicated to promoting and exhibiting new creators outside of the trends  circuit. The concept distinguishes itself for the versatility of the chosen pieces. They are feminine pieces of discreet sensuality that, coordinated among themselves, allow various conjugations between textures, densities and lightness.

Thanks to its central location, this hostel is an excellent starting point to discover the city, its points of interest, as well as its night life. A young, energetic, and multilingual team awaits you, so they can help you discover Porto, according to your fancies and interests, making your stay an unforgettable one. Guests may benefit from a modern, fully equipped kitchen, and a living room with games, music, films and cable TV. This hostel has four double rooms and multiple mixed ones, with bedding and also individual lockers included.

System for monitoring the tourist during the stay, maintaining the health care services he/she has in his/her home country. Upon request of the tourist to his/her tour operator, hotel, health insurer, or directly to T.H.E., he/she is presented with a health care plan suitable for his/her specific needs. When approved, all necessary entities shall be contacted in order to contract the provision of health care. This service is subject to a protocol between the Municipality of Porto and T.H.E. to ensure its dissemination to the target audience, and guarantee total customer satisfaction.

The Faculty of Architecture of the University of Porto  FAUP  is a centre of creation, transmission and dissemination of disciplinary knowledge about architecture, construction and urban planning. The Faculty is a legal person under public law with scientific, pedagogical, executive and financial autonomy, according to the applicable law, the articles of association of the University of Porto and their own. The academic offering includes: integrated master’s degree in Architecture, advanced studies course and doctor’s degree program, postgraduate courses  specialization in building construction and urban projects .

The Faculty of Nutrition and Food Science of the University of Porto  Faculdade de Ciências da Nutrição e Alimentação da Universidade do Porto-FCNAUP  is the most recent faculty of the University of Porto, established in 1996. FCNAUP is the only national public faculty that has the degree of Nutritional Sciences. FCNAUP contributes to the continuity of the development of a solid and organised academic community based on a high-quality degree, training pre-graduate and postgraduate students to get actively involved in an interdisciplinary research and in the community intervention.

The Faculty of Sciences of the University of Porto  FCUP  is one of the biggest institutions of the University of Porto in terms of students and scientific productivity. Its professors are involved in research units of well-known excellence, ensuring an education according to the highest European standards. Founded in 1911, FCUP is responsible for teaching exact and natural sciences and its applications to technology, through a varied offering which includes degrees, integrated master’s degrees, master’s degrees and doctor’s degrees. We invite you to learn more about us by accessing our website at http://www.fc.up.pt or, preferably, by visiting us.

The Faculty of Law of the University of Porto  FDUP  is the only one in the country to teach one scientific area: criminology. The cornerstone of FDUP is bipartite, so there is teaching, learning and research not only related to law but also to crime science. The creation of critical knowledge occurs in two different scientific areas: legal sciences and crime science. Just like research, education is based on two areas: law and criminology. Each one integrates three levels of studies: degree, master’s degree and doctor’s degree.

The Faculty of Sport of the University of Porto  FADEUP  gives great emphasis to sports in education and research. The academic offering includes a degree, master’s degree and doctor’s degree, therefore corresponding to the polysemy and polymorphism of the physical and sporting activities, as well as to the relevant education and research requirements. It should be noted that the Faculty takes part in student mobility programs, such as Socrates/Erasmus Program, involving many European countries, and it promotes mobility programs at degree level with several Brazilian universities.

Founded in 1836, the Academy of Fine Arts has kept a policy of awarding scholarships abroad to the best students, who traditionally went to Paris and Rome and often sent examples of their work which are, to this day, preserved in the Museum of the Faculty of Fine Arts  FBAUP . Currently, the student exchange between European institutions and FBAUP is intense and is still growing in terms of student mobility and teachers exchange programs. The academic offering includes degrees, specialization courses, master’s degrees, doctor’s degrees and different types of training courses.

The Faculty of Arts of the University of Porto, with over 3000 students, provides 13 degrees, 30 master’s degrees and 16 doctor’s degrees in the following areas: Archaeology, Information Science, Communication Sciences, Language Sciences, Teaching Methodology, Cultural Studies, Philosophy, Geography, History, History of Art, Classical and Foreign Languages, Literature, Museology, Portuguese as a Foreign Language, International Relations, Sociology, Translation and Tourism. Find out more at: http://bit.ly/YVxi0E

The Faculty of Dental Medicine of the University of Porto, referred to as FMDUP, is, according to the articles of association of the University, an organic unit of education and research with self-governance, statutory, scientific, pedagogical, cultural, executive and financial autonomy, and a taxpayer. It is endowed with the provision of services to the community, which are governed by the scientific and pedagogical interest, and is a prestigious institution, both nationally and internationally. The academic offering includes several master’s degrees and doctor’s degrees, besides a number of specialization and continuing education courses.

Universidade Portucalense Infante D. Henrique  UPT  is a cooperative higher education institution that began its activity in 1986. The UPT works exclusively in the city of Porto, in the University Campus of Asprela, with Teaching and Research in the following areas: Law, Economics, Management, Marketing, Computer Science, Psychology, Education, Tourism and Hospitality, Heritage and Culture, and International Relations.

The Escola Superior Artística do Porto  ESAP  is a university school with more than 30 years of experience in art education. Its location in Porto’s historic centre is a strategic choice that has contributed to a significant interaction with the local community and the urban environment in which it is inserted. Since its inception, it has been an UNESCO associated school, has also established various national and international protocols with universities and other institutions of an artistic and cultural nature, and its students have been awarded prizes and mentions at a national and international level. ESAP is also a space where encourages research and a meeting point for artists, theoreticians, researchers, curators or other professionals, national and international, both internal and external of the institution. The rank it occupies in the arts and architecture world, manifests itself in the growing recognition of its peers.

The Porto School of Pharmacy was founded in 1836, being always known for the quality and excellence of its education and research. Today, the Faculty of Pharmacy of the University of Porto  FFUP  is an institution which integrates education, research and development, being constantly committed to the academic excellence in the field of pharmaceutical sciences, and still a centre of creation, communication and dissemination of culture, science and technology in other areas of health, chemical and biological sciences.

Founded in 1953, the Faculty of Economics of the University of Porto  FEP  is a national and international reference in the field of economic and business sciences, both in terms of education and scientific research. With an academic offering that covers the three stages of higher education, it has thousands of Alumni working in the best companies and institutions of the country and abroad. Daily, more than 3 companies seek for applicants in FEP’s Employment Bureau, and 2300 companies are in constant contact with FEP for hiring purposes.

The Faculty of Medicine of the University of Porto  FMUP  is the second oldest Faculty of the University of Porto. It integrates more than 40 functional units divided into Services, Central Services and Dependent Institutions, besides the Student Association. It should be noted that, in the past few years, the entry grades for admission to the degree in Medicine have consistently been the highest ones among all the Faculties of Medicine across the country. Its academic offering includes several master’s degrees and doctor’s degrees.

local: Instituto Superior de Engenharia do Porto  Engineering Institute   - IPP
descricao: ISEP  School of Engineering of the Porto Polytechnic Institute  is a Portuguese brand of success for public high education and research in the field of engineering.  If offers 11 degrees, 11 masters and 12 graduate specializations in strategic areas for national competitiveness. As far as R&D are concerned ISEP is one of the most significant scientific bodies in Portugal, with groups leading its intervention areas. The Institute also provides society with its services of technologic knowledge.

Porto Lusíada University is composed of several Faculties  Faculty of Architecture and Arts, Faculty of Business and Economic Sciences, Institute of Psychology and Education Sciences, Faculty of Law  with a diverse academic offering which includes degrees, master’s degrees, postgraduate degrees, Masters and doctor’s degrees. Besides this wide offering, it should be noted that there is also Specialized Training Courses, Short Courses and Short-term Courses in the different fields.

ESMAE  Higher School of Music, Art and Entertainment of the Porto Polytechnic Institute  was founded from the Higher School of Music, following a centuries-old tradition of music teaching in Porto, and it was created with the purpose of expanding its educational offering to Theatre and Dance. It is composed of the Music and Theatre departments and the Photography, Cinema, Audiovisual and Multimedia departments as well. Teatro Helena Sá e Costa and Café-Concerto are located in ESMAE, where dance and theatre shows are frequent, as products of the students’ initiative.

Porto Higher School of Nursing  ESEP  offers high education in the  nursing field  degree, post-graduation, expertise and master . ESEP is settled in three different buildings: ESEP-main office  Asprela Campus , ESEP-Cidade do Porto Campus  Rua Álvares Cabral , and ESEP-Ana Guedes Campus  Rua Professor Álvaro Rodrigues .

UFP  Fernando Pessoa University , established by Fundação Ensino e Cultura “Fernando Pessoa”  Fernando Pessoa Education and Culture Foundation  is the result of an innovative higher education project which started in 1980. UFP objectives are education, research, community extension and the provision of services in the field of education by its faculties, according to its scientific and pedagogical structure: Faculty of Science and Technology  Porto   Faculty of Health Sciences and Higher School of Health  Porto 
- Ponte de Lima Unit, with Higher School of Health as well.

Located at the Asprela campus, one of the largest in the peninsula, the Porto Polytechnic School of Education  Escola Superior de Educação do Politécnico do Porto  provides training in 8 undergraduate degrees and 13 masters, as well as technical and professional courses and several short courses. Because someone who knows, does things, and someone who knows and does things, teaches. br 
Our mission is teaching, education and social intervention. We train professionals in scientific, technical, artistic and pedagogical areas, always aiming at an ethical training towards citizenship. The diversity of our training courses is aimed towards the provision of community care. We are committed to the society by training pedagogues linked to the Visual Arts, Sports Sciences  Music Education, Heritage Management, among other areas of knowledge. We carry out applied research and innovation in a professional context, which strongly promotes the improvement and/or transformation of the educational reality. School where education, culture, sport and citizenship converge in a unique space of training and knowledge.

ICBAS  Institute of Biomedical Sciences Abel Salazar  of the University of Porto is a university school and a centre of education, scientific research, culture and provision of services to the community, acting in the scientific fields of Fundamental and Applied Biology, in the areas of Health, Environment, Animal Production, Food Transformation and Processing, and Quality Control. ICBAS courses favour the preparation for the professional practice of human medicine, veterinary medicine, aquatic sciences, biochemistry and bioengineering, or provide master’s and doctor’s degrees in such areas and in other related areas.

Porto Lusophone University is an organization acknowledged as an institution of public interest with the purposes of Higher Education and Scientific Research. It is owned by Grupo Lusófona, the biggest private education group in Portugal, and it provides more than 80 courses covering different areas and education levels. Currently, it integrates several Faculties and Institutes. Faculties: Sciences and Health Technologies | Political Science and International Relations | Natural Sciences, Engineering and Technologies | Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technology | Law | Economic, Social and Business Sciences | Psychology | Physical Education and Sports. Institutes: Education | Psychopedagogy.

ISCET  Higher Institute of Business Sciences and Tourism , created in 1990, offers mainly degrees, courses for technologic specialization  CET , masters, post graduations and specialized training. The development of the courses is made as to grant students the better educational conditions to prepare them for active life. Degrees: Tourism, Marketing and Advertising, Social  Services, Law for Solicitors, International Trade Relations. CET: Hotel Hosting and accommodation  Commercial Management and Marketing, Free times socioeducational animation, Law services, Human Resources Management, Gerontology.

local: Escola das Artes da Universidade Católica Portuguesa
descricao: The training offering covers: Degrees in Art-Conservation and Restoration, and Sound and Image  Master in Conservation and Restoration of Cultural Goods – specialization in Painting and Sculpture/Carving, Masters in Music Teaching,  Sound and Image – specializations in Computer Animation, Digital Arts, Film and Audio-visual and Sound Design  Master in Creative Industries Management  Doctorate in Arts Science and Technology - specializations in Computer Music and Interactive Art, Doctorate in Maintenance of Cultural Assets - specializations in Painting Inorganic materials, Doctorate in Heritage Studies.

The Faculty of Psychology and Education Sciences is, at the University of Porto, the school that carries out teaching and research in the areas of Psychology and Education Sciences and began its activities in 1980. Currently, nearly 2000 students attend our degree programmes, Integrated Masters, Masters and PhDs. Our students have achieved a significant presence in the social fabric in areas such as education, justice, health, central and local public administration, sociocultural activities, work in companies, etc. br 
FPCEUP also focuses on continuing education programs, aiming to meet the requirements in terms of professional, civic and cultural training which are felt throughout life in contemporary societies. br 
In addition to teaching, the FPCEUP develops research activities, houses two research centres funded by FCT-Fundação para a Ciência e a Tecnologia and various research structures  laboratories, observatories, resource centres, etc. . Through these structures, it is also committed to provide services to the community, multiplying its research themes and respective funding institutions of the projects by offering services such as counselling and therapy in psychology, consulting services, training and services for education and family. br 
Internationalisation plays an important role, not only because of the growing number of projects and protocols established in partnership with foreign institutions, but also by the increase of the student flow and the exchange of teachers and researchers, through cooperation and mobility programmes. br 
With more than 35 years of history, FPCEUP looks towards the future and is increasingly committed to quality education and to promote an institutional culture of research and social justice.

local: Escola de Direito do Porto - Faculdade de Direito / Universidade Católica Portuguesa
descricao: The Law Degree in the Católica Porto was the first Law Degree in Porto and in the north region. It offers quality education, based on strictness and demanding, and inspired in Christian humanistic values, steadily preparing students to become good professionals. Academic offer: Degree in Law  Master in Law  expertise in Private, Commercial and Business, Taxation, Criminal, Public and International, Administrative and Labour Law  Master in Law and Management  PhD in Law  Advanced and Specialized Training.

local: School of Economics and Management of the Catholic University
descricao: The School of Economics and Management of the Catholic University is an innovative school in terms of teaching economics and management, being in close articulation with the business world and a wide network of partnerships which boosts international mobility. Master’s Degrees available for double degree with the Lancaster University  *  Academic offering including: Degrees in Economics and Management  Masters in Auditing and Taxation  Banking and Insurance  Business Economics *   Social Economics  Finance *   Management *   Service Management  Marketing, Human Resources Management.

local: Faculty of Theology - Universidade Católica Portuguesa
descricao: Main goals: to promote research and education on the Christian revelation, to evangelize culture and contemporary world, to educate Pastoral care agents  priesthood candidates , Catholic Moral and Religious Education teachers and other ministers dedicated to building communities. Degree in Religious Sciences  Integrated Master in Theology  Master in Religious Sciences  specializations in Moral and Religious Catholic Education Teaching for Basic and Secondary Education levels, and Social-Religious Animation   Mater in Studies of Religion – Health disease and cure  PhD in Theology. Special mention goes to the Theology Lectures, taking place every year.

local: Higher School of Biotechnology  of the Portuguese Catholic University - ESB
descricao: Founded in 1984, the ESB is a pioneer in teaching and research in biotechnology. Its Mission is to intervene, impressively, on scientific, social and economic communities, through the development of educational initiatives, research and development, extension and entrepreneurship in biotechnology. It is stated as a forum of international excellence. Training offer: Degrees: Bioengineering, Microbiology, Nutrition Sciences. Master s degrees: Food Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, Applied Microbiology, Biotechnology and Innovation. Doctorates: Biotechnology  Sciences and Food Engineering and Nutrition  Enology and Viticulture.

local: Institute of Health Sciences - Universidade Católica Portuguesa
descricao: Besides the set of courses offered by the ICS Porto, it also comprises the Nursing Teaching Unit  Centro de Enfermagem da Católica  that provides nursing services for the community. Courses available: degree in Nursing  master’s degrees in Nursing  specialization in Advanced Nursing, Nursing Services Management, History and Philosophy of Nursing, Nursing Education, Palliative Care, Wounds and Tissue Viability  doctor’s degree in Nursing  postgraduate courses, continuing education courses and advanced training courses, also distance learning.

local: Faculty of Education and Psychology of the Portuguese Catholic University  FEP – UCP
descricao: Academic offer: Degrees: Psychology  Masters: Psychology  specializations: Psychology of Education and Human Development, Clinic and Health Psychology, Psychology of Work and Businesses  Justice and Deviant Behaviour Psychology , Education Sciences  specializations: School Management and Organization, Social Pedagogy, Pedagogical Supervision and Teachers Evaluation, Technology and Education , Psychology and Human Resources Development. Post-graduation: Learning to Educate – Programmes for Teachers, Educators, Parents and Professionals. Available services: Psychology University Clinic  CUP  and Support Service for School Improvement  SAME .

ISAG  Higher Institute of Administration and Management  is a private polytechnic higher education institution located in Porto. Seeking to meet the needs of the business market, ISAG, has been, since 1979, an enhancer of the development of high education courses in the North of Portugal. It now offers 4 degrees: Business Management, Hotel Management, Business Relations and Tourism, as well as two masters – Business Management, and Commercial Management and Marketing.

ISET  Higher Institute of Education and Work  is a higher education institution designed for teaching and research activities in the fields of Education and Work. By means of graduated, specialized and continuing education duly updated, ISET established high standards of quality in education, paying special attention to the role of research as an educational process, with regard to graduated education. The academic offering covers Educational Administration and Educational Intervention, Pedagogical Supervision and Teaching Evaluation, Sociocultural Animation and Educational Administration.

local: Escola Superior de Saúde de Santa Maria
descricao:
Escola Superior de Saúde de Santa Maria it’s a private higher education institution, owned by Província Portuguesa das Franciscanas Missionárias de Nossa Senhora, “heir” of the history and the formative patrimony of the Escola Superior de Enfermagem de Santa Maria that, since 1952, it has always been a benchmark for quality in teaching and in personal and technical training of nursing professionals, continuously sharing its mission and promoting the principles and values of its founding matrix. br 
Aiming to adapt to current challenges, by broadening and diversifying its courses, Escola Superior de Enfermagem de Santa Maria  Santa Maria Nursing School  has become Escola Superior de Saúde de Santa Maria  Santa Maria Health School  since 9 June 2016. br 
Inspired by the Franciscan example and ideals, ESSSM s educational and training project aims to give future professionals a pluralistic education, prominently oriented to the service of others, founded on principles which add value to human beings and provide the privileged attention to the ones in need as well as the underprivileged, is also technically advanced, aligned with cutting-edge procedures in healthcare, very engaged with the community, focused in internationalising and stimulating the positive transition into the world of work. br 
ESSSM offers, in terms of degrees/courses, Masters in Rehabilitation Nursing, two undergraduate degrees: Nursing and Physiotherapy, a CTeSP: Gerontology and Long-term care and short courses. br 
The Escola Superior de Saúde de Santa Maria, heiress of a relevant past, is now concentrated in the future and seeks to train today, tomorrow’s best professionals.

local: Escola Superior de Educação de Paula Frassinetti
descricao: ESEPF  Paula Frassinetti Higher School of Education  already graduated thousands of Education Professionals through degrees, master’s degrees and postgraduate degrees. It gives greater importance to pedagogical practices in a professional context and it has established protocols with more than eighty institutions  schools, private social solidarity institutions – IPSS, municipalities and others  in the area of Porto. The international mobility of the academic community, the social volunteering in Portugal and Mozambique and the university pastoral care create a distinctive educational and cultural environment.

ISTEC, as a higher education institution, polytechnic in nature, which teaches courses exclusively in the area of information technologies, has as its mission:
- To contribute to improve the technological potential of the country s human resources 
- To provide a higher education level aimed to the pursuit of a profession in the areas of computer science and multimedia 
- To stimulate intellectual and professional training, as well as the mobility of students and graduates, both at a national and international level, namely within Europe 
- To contribute to render services to the community, mainly through the development of projects, on a partnership basis, in the area of information technologies 
- To implement, in departments set up for this purpose, applied research practices 
- To promote and institutionalize measures for a permanent integration of graduates into professional life
