- To create, maintain and promote a permanent and preferential relationship with the business and social fabric, at a national level and within its geographic area’s sphere of influence 
- To participate in international mobility programmes, preferably in the area of learning 
- To promote and contribute to training activities for teachers, researchers and staff.

To ensure the constant update of the syllabus contents and enrich the range of learning resources made available to students, namely through the use of educational multimedia and communication systems and online pedagogical interaction, aiming to increase the overall quality of the education provided and foster new ways of pedagogical interaction that, by optimizing the learning times of fundamental knowledge and aptitudes, favour and stimulate the students  initiative in order to create an education system based on skills development.

The quay, designed by Engineer Fernando Silveira Ramos, was awarded the Civil Engineering Secil Award 2009. This structure was built on the northern bank of the River Douro’s mouth to resist the direct action of the storm waves and big floods currents. It bears over 65,000 m3 of concrete and 2,500 tons of steel. It is the perfect spot for a walk into the sea, offering an amazing view. You can also walk it through inside up to the beacon.
Paranhos is a parish with a population of 44. 298 people  2011  distributed among an area of about 6,67 km2.
Tourist information point located by the ticket offices in the Campanhã Railway Station, with direct assistance from 15 June to 15 September  daily - 09:30-18:30 .

“Cheap and Chic” restaurant café, with a cosmopolitan and modern ambience, not overlooking tradition. Food is typically Portuguese at lunch, offering at dinner more international options. During the day you can enjoy good wine paired with Portuguese specialties. The covered terrace, with a view to the Church of São Francisco, displays a different perspective of the place during summer months.

Surfivor Surf Camp is the oldest and largest Surf Camp in the North of Portugal. Surfivor is more than a surf school. It is also a meeting point for surf lovers, offering accommodation and entertainment. The school welcomes amateur and professional surfers from all over Europe and provides all necessary equipment to learn surf, from surfboards to wetsuits.

Nautical tourism company settled in the Douro Marina. It seeks to provide unique experiences following each person’s preferences, offering exclusive boats to be rented by groups, friends, family, for rides on the River Douro, with skipper. Every client may choose his own options to explore the area, as well as different entertainment activities and tasting of food and wine from the Douro demarcated region, and also other significant landmarks as the cities of Porto and Guimarães using private road transportation.

At the top of the Hotel Dom Henrique Downtown, far above sea level and with a 360º panoramic view over the city, which floods the senses and thrills at first gaze, there is a singular, inspiring venue ... where all the senses come together in perfection. In the Restaurant 17º we gather all the ingredients to make each meal one of the best moments of the day. The Bar 17º is a space designed to the slightest detail, where one can relax, socialise and enjoy, unhurried. Because the best of every day is in the moments that make us happy.

The glamour coats the interior of an appealing space that extends to the breathtaking view over the city, where the look lingers. A unique environment that incites you to stay.

If you are looking for the perfect place for a pleasant afternoon or to experience, in the best way, the nightlife of Porto, you will find it here.

Welcome to the Duas Nações Guest House  Pensão Duas Nações : it´s settled in a beautiful 100 years old house with a cozy and pleasant atmosphere. It’s located in Porto’s historical area, in a little square offering a great choice of cafes, bakeries, restaurants, shops… Rooms are confortable and have been recently renewed, with a nice decoration so that guests may enjoy a relaxing stay during their visit to Porto. Rooms have different prices according to the chosen facilities.

Gourmet space which sells exclusively Portuguese-made products. Here you can find canned fish, cheeses, wines, jams, vinegar, honey, chocolates, conventual cookies and products from the Autonomous Region of Madeira. With home delivery service in Porto, Matosinhos and Vila Nova de Gaia  minimum of 15 Euros , the product line of the shop also includes cured meat/sausages, olive paste, liqueurs, olive oils and teas.

ISSSP  Instituto Superior de Serviço Social do Porto – Porto Higher Institute of Social Service  awards the degree in Social Service since 1989. It favours an education which is critical and open to the development of social changes oriented to the consolidation of the civic, social and cultural rights, with especial regard to those of the socially vulnerable citizens. The academic offering includes: Degree in Social Gerontology  Degree in Social Service  Master’s Degree in Social Gerontology  Master’s Degree in Social Intervention in Children and Youth at Risk of Social Exclusion.

The Portuguese Catholic University Library is specially directed to support teaching and academic investigation, mainly concerned with the needs of the teachers and students of the courses provided. Searching for the universal dissemination of knowledge, it is also a library at the service of the entire community with interests, professional or otherwise, in its specialization domain: human and social sciences.

ISCAP  Institute of Accounting and Administration of Porto , organic unit of the Porto Polytechnic Institute  IPP , is firmly committed to the development of a culture of quality and continuous improvement. The academic offering includes: degrees in Administrative Assistance and Translation, International Commerce, Business Communication, Accounting and Administration, Management of Tourism Activities, Marketing  Master’s Degrees in Administration Consultancy, Accounting and Finance, Entrepreneurship and Internationalization, Organization Management, Digital Marketing, Logistics, Specialized Translation and Interpretation, Postgraduate Degrees and Specialization Courses.

local: Document Centre of the São João National Theatre
descricao: The Documentation Centre emerged in 2000 to counter the fleeting nature of the creative gesture and de-dramatise the effects of many documental curses. It began a process of gathering and treatment of all the documental material created by the TNSJ. Since 2001, it manages a database, Cinfo  located at www.tnsj.pt , which references the documents that exist physically in the Documentation Centre, also functioning as an online library, with great part of those documents also available in digital format. It has a library specialized in performing arts, a collection that shares with other professionals and the general public.

local: Higher Institute of Social Service of Porto Library
descricao: The Higher Institute of Social Service of Porto Library supports the pedagogic and investigation activities through the information’s recovery, treatment and diffusion. Its documental fund is constituted by monographs, periodic publications, master’s and doctorate theses and other materials besides books, illustrating the changes that have occurred in the Social Service Course throughout time. Specialized in the Social Service area, its documental core covers the following areas: Social Service, Psychology, Sociology, Economics, Statistics, Law, Philosophy, History, Education, Anthropology and Ethnology.

local: Library and Informatio Management of the Portuguese Catholic University -  Asprela Campus
descricao: The Information Management Library, also known as Knowledge Resources Centre for the Agro-food industry, has a specialized perspective, because the documental collection that constitutes it fits predominantly in the competence and investigation domains that characterize the mother institution: Bioengineering, Biosciences, Nursing and Health Technologies. Running in a free access regime, it is organised according to the Library Congress Classification  LCC , open to the Asprela community, as well as to the whole external community.

local: Library of the Science Faculty - Porto University
descricao: The Faculty of Sciences Library has 12 specialized reading rooms, fundamentally in the domains of exact sciences and natural sciences, as well as the technologies associated to them and the whole collection is catalogued. The Faculty also has a significant bibliographic and iconographic collection with works from the 16th century to the beginning of the 20th century, gathered in its Old Library Collection.

local: Library of the Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences - Porto University
descricao: The mission of the Faculty of Psychology and Educational Sciences Library is to support teaching and investigation, providing a set of services, a wide, diverse and updated documental fund and access to various information resources. It provides documents in traditional format  paper , but it also has resources in digital format  library catalogue, databases, electronic magazines and books, etc. . The Library develops an acquisition, treatment and diffusion work of documentation and information and it works in a free access regime, which allows the free consultation of the existing documents in the reading rooms.

local: Document and Information Services - Library of the Faculty of Engineering - Porto University
descricao: The Documentation and Information Services of the Engineering Faculty of the University of Porto integrates the Library, the Archive and the Museum, the Electronic Services and the Editorial, in an integrating practice with a focus in the creation, storage, diffusion and information communication technologies. The Library belongs to the University of Porto s libraries network and it seeks to supply students, teachers, investigators and staff with the information of scientific, pedagogic, technical and cultural nature to support their academic and functional activities, cultural development and social integration, as well as to safeguard and divulge their cultural and scientific heritage.

local: Document, Information and Archive Centre  CDIA  - Faculty of Economics of the Porto University
descricao: The mission of the Faculty of Economics of the University of Porto s Library is to supply students, teachers, investigators and staff all the resources of a scientific, pedagogic, technical and cultural nature to support the permanent investigation, teaching and education.

local: Library of the Faculty of Economics - Porto University
descricao: The mission of the Faculty of Economics of the University of Porto is to supply students, teachers, investigators and staff all the resources of a scientific, pedagogic, technical and cultural nature to support the permanent investigation, teaching and education.

local: Document Services of the Faculty of Law - Porto University
descricao: The Professor Jorge Ribeiro de Faria Library is part of  Documentation Services. Its primary aim is to give priority help and support to the University s faculty and student body, and to the community in general, promoting research and the dissemination of judicial know-how and erudition. It is sourced by the general fund, Pedro Veiga and donations from the International Centre  of Comparative Criminology of the University of Montreal.    Also part of this is the Oporto University Centre for European Documentation, which specialises in documents produced by EU Institutions.

local: Library and Document Centre of the Faculty of Architecture - Porto University
descricao:
The Faculty of Architecture of Porto University Library is specialized in architecture, with emphasis to urban planning and art. It has around 14,000 monographs, more than 300 serial publications, multimedia material  audio and video tapes, CDs and DVDs , cartographic material and online catalogue. It acquires, gathers, treats and divulges pedagogical, scientific and cultural character documentation, produces and divulges information, supports the scientific-pedagogic activity, stimulates the educational process, promotes contacts with other libraries or documentation centres and ensures the conservation and recovery of information.

Since 1993, Faculdade de Arquitectura da Universidade do Porto includes in its Document Services, an Urbanism and Architecture Documentation Centre  CDUA , which is responsible for several activities:
i  in the collection, registration, deposit, conservation and safeguarding of funds / archives with “patrimonial, historical, artistic or document value relating to Portuguese and Porto’s architecture and urbanism”, particularly in the 19th century 
ii  the registration, preservation, investigation and dissemination of documentation of patrimonial, historical or artistic value carried over from ESBAP / Architecture Course and / or generated at the Faculty itself 
iii  preventive conservation of  funds  through their digitization/scanning and creating information systems 
iv  organising cultural activities  by holding exhibitions or loaning documentation of its holdings .

Visit and/or consultation by prior appointment.

local: Library of the Faculty of Medicine  - Porto Univerisity
descricao: It is the Library’s job to provide and support the researches in databases important to teaching and investigation, to provide technical support in the iconography area to all the teachers and investigators of the Faculty and develop and improve the iconographic techniques, namely in the medical photography domain.

local: Library of the Universidade Fernando Pessoa - Fernando Pessoa Library
descricao: Located in the Fernando Pessoa University main building, it covers the science and knowledge, organization, information, documentation, philosophy, psychology, religion, theology, social sciences, statistics, economics, commerce, law, politics, public administration, social assistance, education, mathematics, natural sciences, applied sciences, technology, art, recreation, entertainment, language, linguistics, literature, geography and history areas. It has the following services available: home reading, interlibrary loans, users’ training, reference and research, among others.

local: Library of the Faculty of Fine Arts - Porto University
descricao: The main goals of the Faculty of Fine Arts of the University of Porto Library are: to provide the teachers, students and staff the necessary information for the effective exercise of their activity  to boost the educational process, actively collaborating in bibliographic exhibits  to promote the contact with other Libraries or Documentation Centres  to exert its action in the acquisition, gathering, treatment and diffusion domains of pedagogical, scientific and cultural character  to ensure the information’s conservation and recovery  to take care of the restoration of degraded items  watch over the fairness and justice of the information’s distribution.

local: Library of the Universidade Lusíada - Porto
descricao: The Lusíada University Library is located in the college campus, in an autonomous building. The Library’s main goals are: to provide free access to specialized and scientific information  support the degree, post-graduation, master s and doctorate courses  provide the scientific community with support for investigations  promote training courses for its users  boost its place with initiatives that favour the educational effort developed in the University.

Clínica Médica da Foz, a general medicine practice clinic and several other services, tries to reach the most demanding clients of all nationalities, always with the concern of ensuring a follow-up by the best specialists and medical team. The team consists of Portuguese and foreign professionals, from several countries, such as Brazil and the United States, and presents pioneering treatments in Portugal. Located in the area of Foz do Douro, in a renovated building.

local: Library of the Faculty of Dental Medicine - Porto University
descricao: The Faculty of Dentistry of the University of Porto Library supports the teaching activity of the courses offered in the Faculty and its job is to process, organize, recover and provide the available information in the various formats and supports produced in the scope of the courses, investigations and works. The Library is open to everyone, as long as they follow the requirements inherent to the University of Porto library network, having always present the purpose for which it was created – to support, forward and orient its academic audience.

local: Library of the Universidade Portucalense Infante D. Henrique
descricao: The Portucalense University Library provides the scientific production repository of the University and, through Delicious, a list of electronic resources of all the areas taught in the University. The EBSCO Databases also cover all the areas taught in UPT.

local: Library of  the Institute for Biomedical Sciences Abel Salazar and the Faculty of Pharmacy
descricao: The ICBAS/FFUP Library serves the academic and scientific community of the Abel Salazar Biomedical and Sciences Institute and the Faculty of Pharmacy of the University of Porto, but also the public in general, covering areas such as Biomedical Sciences, Medical Sciences, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Nursing Sciences, Aquatic Environment Sciences, Animal Sciences and Veterinary Sciences, Agriculture and Forest Sciences, Anatomy, Physiology, Pharmacology, Toxicology, Biology, Histology, Microbiology, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Bioengineering, Analytic Chemistry, Organic Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Chemistry, Clinical Trials, Cosmetics, Dermopharmacy, Pharmacognosy, Pharmaceutical Management, Technology, Pharmaceutical, Environment and Acupuncture.

Shop located in the heart of the Ribeira, near the D. Luís I bridge, which sells exclusively Portuguese-made products. Here you can find chocolates, gourmet products and 100% handmade tapestry from Viana do Castelo, and to taste Óbidos Ginjinha  berries liqueur , Port Wine and Nespresso coffee in a chocolate cup. The store has also a tasting room and bike rental service as well.

Ltd. Edition combines in a single space the most sophisticated fashion accessories from prestigious international brands, such as Salvatore Ferragamo, Valentino, Elie Saab, Jil Sander, Marni, Gianvito Rossi, among others, which place special emphasis on the quality of the materials, the precision of the manufacturing process and the seductive design. A unique place of sober luxury, characterized by a careful selection of the accessories, a constant awareness of the last trends and personal assistance. Ltd. Edition enjoys a privileged location in the glamorous area of Aviz, in a building designed by Siza Vieira.

Cale Guest House is located at Largo de S. Domingos, which goes back to the 14th century, so it benefits from a privileged centrality. In the heart of the Porto historical centre, you can easily go to the main monuments, as well as to the commercial area of the city. The building, recently reconstructed, combines old and contemporary design and has 7 rooms full of history, but with modern decoration, providing a cosy and comfortable atmosphere. We hope you like to stay with us.

local: Document and Information Centre of the Porto Business School
descricao: The Documentation and Information Centre has as its main mission to support the students, teachers and staff of Porto Business School in their investigation projects, being also open to the whole business community. It gathers, organizes, makes available and divulges information resources and bets in the acquisition of online databases to provide the most recent and qualified information. Its documental collection has about 4,000 monographs, 400 master’s theses, periodical publications, available in free access.

local: Library of the Engineering Institute - IPP
descricao: The ISEP Library is a specialist academic library of free access, enabling any user to freely consult all documents available. The library provides information to the academic community, supporting teaching, learning and research activities. The main purposes of the library are the treatment, organisation, dissemination and preservation of the document corpus, thus meeting its users’ needs.

Located in Foz Velha, in an 18th century house, it has two apartments with 60 square meters each. Each apartment has one bedroom with a double bed, one full bathroom, one living room with two sofa beds and an American-style kitchen so you can prepare your meals. They are painted in warm colours and decorated with Indo-Portuguese furniture which creates a cosy and, at the same time, relaxing environment. They are equipped with towels and bedding, cutlery and plates, cable TV and free Wi-Fi Internet.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

local: Biblioteca Especializada do Núcleo de Estudos Latino-Americanos da Universidade Fernando Pessoa
descricao: Designated Ricardo Reis Library from August 2012, with free acess, it serves the school community of the Fernando Pessoa University as well as any other interested reader. Its document centre covers science research areas: ethics, natural sciences, medical sciences and history of medicine. The library has got a reading room, 3 spots for video screening, 8 rooms for group work provided with internet access research points, exclusively for students and University staff. It’s got lockers, printing equipment and wireless internet access.

The Álvares Cabral Guest House is located near Porto’s centre, a 5 minute walk from Trindade Metro Station  with connection to the airport . It has free Wi-Fi access and a 24-hour reception. The rooms have a private or shared bathroom. Parking is available on-site or in a secure garage in the same street upon request.

Authored by Master Francisco Simões, the statue is a tribute to one of the greatest and most prolific writers of Portuguese Literature, Camilo Castelo Branco, author of the novel “Amor de Perdição”, from 1862, a man of overwhelming passions himself.

local: Ricardo Reis Library - Universidade Fernando Pessoa
descricao: The Ricardo Reis Library is located in the Pedagogical Clinic Building, and houses books, periodicals, academic treatises and reference books and audiovisual materials in the health science area. As well as wifi there is self-access to a printer and scanner and storage lockers.

local: Museum of Anatomy of Porto´s University Faculty of Medicine
descricao:
The Museum of Anatomy of the Faculty of Medicine of the University of Porto was founded in 1825. It has a significant collection of hundreds of anatomic pieces – some of them are more than a hundred years old – distributed over several rooms. Beginning with the central nervous system and peripheral nervous system, passing through anatomic preparations of the various organ systems of the human body, and it is worth noting the demonstrations of anatomic variations. There are rooms dedicated to Teratology, Comparative Anatomy and, finally, to the skeletal system, where several skeletons and a collection of human skulls are exposed.


All visits to the Museum are guided and require prior booking, by telephone: +351 225513616

In 1984 IPAM – Portuguese Institute for Marketing Management created the firs Marketing School in Portugal. It offers degrees on Marketing Management, masters on Marketing Design and Marketing Management, as well as postgraduate degrees.

The InPátio Guest House is located in the Ribeira area, in Porto, just a 5-minute walk from Porto Cathedral and Palácio da Bolsa. All rooms have air conditioning, and a unique, smooth decoration. Some of them have stone walls or design furniture. For additional comfort, there is floor heating and a towel dryer. You can enjoy the breakfast served by InPátio Guest House in one of the living rooms, outdoors or indoors. Guests may schedule their transportation from/to the airport, as well as rent a bike at the reception.

Located in the most trendy art district of Porto, surrounded by art galleries, artist’s studios and concept stores, ROSA ET AL Townhouse is made by travelers for travelers. With only seven elegant and exclusive suites with balcony, this is not your typical hotel, but your exclusive townhouse in Porto, founded by Patrícia Sousa and Emanuel De Sousa. All suites overlook the garden and the centre of the Cedofeita quarter or Rua do Rosário, and guests can take advantage of the in-room spa service. The original nineteenth-century building design was maintained with its original, visible wood beams, original decorative plaster ceilings, as well as permanent art installations of contemporary artists. The space is decorated with a series of works of art, objects and furniture of vintage, modern and contemporary design. It also has a café, a restaurant, a shop, outdoor patios and an aromatic garden.

The O’Porto Seven Guest House, located in Cedofeita, is an elegant, cosy and family-run hotel. 100 meters away from Rotunda da Boavista and a 5-minute walk away from Carolina Michaëlis and Casa da Música metro stations, its central location favours the use of public transportation to any part of the city. The O’Porto Seven bedrooms were carefully prepared to welcome the most demanding guests and decorated with detailed objects evoking the main monuments of the city. There is a breakfast room and a welcoming bar where guests can relax and have a drink.
Located next to the Trindade Metro Station, it has equipped kitchen. All guest rooms  double and dormitories  have private bathroom and many of them have a balcony. It has a terrace.

Located in the city centre, close to Santa Catarina shopping street, it has 36 rooms and a maximum capacity of 70 people. All the rooms are equipped with private bathroom, direct telephone, cable television, central heating and air conditioning. It also has other services, like tours, 24-hour reception, bar and free Wi-Fi Internet.

The Serviced Apartments Boavista Palace include 70 T1K and T1+1K apartments, fully furnished and equipped with everything necessary so that the guests can enjoy the independence and comfort of a house. Provided with cleaning services and 24-hour reception, they allow an easy adaptation to the modern life demands. The Serviced Apartments are a short, medium or long duration renting product and the minimum renting time is of two nights.

Small building of high quality with contemporary finishing and functional decoration. It has 4 wide and spacious apartments for those who cannot do without the comfort of home with hotel standards. The apartments, some with excellent views over the Douro river, are self-sufficient, with a fully equipped kitchenette and a flat-screen television.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

CATS Porto Hostel is a lively hostel located in the centre of Porto, just 50 meters from the S. Bento metro and S. Bento Train Station. The property s 3 buildings are located in the heart of the city, next to the Sé Cathedral, designated a World Heritage Site.
The hostel provides well-decorated rooms for all types of travellers and groups. All rooms have a private bathroom, air conditioning and free Wi-Fi, and each bed has a reading light, an individual socket, a fan and an electronic locker.
Breakfast is served daily in the bar area and there is a shared kitchen, where guests can prepare their meals. The rooftop terrace provides a unique setting, ideal for relaxing and enjoying a drink.
The Ribeira area, Torre dos Clérigos and Livraria Lello are a 10-minute walk away. Francisco Sá Carneiro Airport, easily accessible by metro, is 44 minutes away.
This is the part of Porto that guests prefer, according to independent reviews.

We speak your language!

In a totally renovated building, Vivacity Porto is a concept of housing for tourists who seek quality allied with economical prices. Installed in a three-storey building, it has 10 rooms divided into three categories  double viva, double city and twin Porto  and a lounge area.

Inserted in a renovated historical building, the apartments present a modern decoration and a totally equipped kitchenette with meal and living areas separated. They are equipped with a couch, a flat screen TV with cable television and free Wi-Fi access. Each apartment has a washing machine, a dishwasher and a private bathroom. The self-catering concept can be summarized in three words: comfort, singularity and simplicity. A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

Designed to belong to the top of modern hostels, it is equipped with led lighting, contemporary furniture and minimal decoration. It has a terrace with barbecue, fireplace and a waterfall, lounge living room and bar.

The new Porto Tasting Room located on Rua das Flores, in the corner of Largo de S. Domingos, reflects the modern image of Portuguese wines, in a style very similar to the Lisbon Tasting Room.

With a huge visitor space with 4 vending machines, the Tasting Room allows the tourist, from 3 euros, is free to choose from the 28 wines that are permanently on display, and which represent the various Portuguese wine regions.

For the more demanding visitor or who wish to deepen their knowledge about Portuguese wines, the Porto Tasting Room offers the visitor the possibility of choosing, from 9 euros / per person among 4 themed tastings. These tastings take place in a private room with capacity for 10 people.

Visit the new Porto Tasting Room from Monday to Saturday between 11am and 7pm, and from April to November from Monday to Sunday, and discover the wide range of products we have for the visitor.

Set of 4 modern, practical and well equipped apartments, located in the historic centre. There are 3 apartments with no bedrooms, equipped with kitchen and private bathroom with shower, for two or three persons, with two single beds or a double bed and an individual sofa bed  extra . There is also 1 duplex one bedroom apartment with bedroom, living room, kitchen, 2 bathrooms  one with bathtub , for two to six persons, with double bed, 1 sofa bed  120x200  and a daybed  120x200 .

local: Espaço Património a Norte – Monastery of  Serra do Pilar
descricao:
Installed in the Monastery of Serra do Pilar, the venue Património a Norte, under the responsibility of the Direção Regional de Cultura do Norte  Regional Northern Culture Directorate , aims to make known the monuments heritage, managed by the DRCN and open to the public, in northern Portugal. br 
Highlighted are the various classified monuments dotted around the North of Portugal and its four classified by UNESCO World Heritage sites: Porto, Guimarães, the Alto Douro Wine Region and the Prehistoric Rock-Art Sites of the Côa River Valley.

This granite statue with a bronze spear, representing a warrior with the city s shield, by João Joaquim Alves de Sousa Alão, is from the beginning of the 19th century and was carved to surmount the Porto City Hall s pediment which was located in Praça Nova  current Praça da Liberdade , where it stood until 1915. From 2001 to 2013, the statue was an element making part of the  memorial recalling long years of life and history of the city of Porto  ,a project of the architect Fernando Távora, dated from 2001 and built upon the ruins of the former Town Hall building, from the sixteenth century . In 2013 the statue returned to Praça da Liberdade, having been placed in front of the facade of the building of the Bank of Portugal.

In a simple and cosy environment, the Creperia La Bombarde presents to the Portuguese people the real recipe of the Bretons handmade crepes. From the sweet crepes to the salty galettes, you have much to choose from. Besides the crepes, you can also discover delicious pancakes, Neveiros  handmade ice creams, a really creamy hot chocolate, colourful salads, milkshakes, cider coming directly from Britain and much more.

local: Aeroporto  Francisco Sá Carneiro Parking  Low Cost Parking
descricao: Located at the Francisco Sá Carneiro Airport, this parking lot is totally open but it is fenced and has video surveillance. The clients’ transport park – airport – park is ensured. There is the possibility of vehicle collection/delivery at the airport, with a previous mandatory reservation of 24 hours.

It is a typical stone-made townhouse with two doors opening directly to Rua da Fontinha  Fontinha Street .
It has two bedrooms, two bathrooms, kitchen and living room  and a small terrace . It is a comfortable and fully equipped habitation for touristic short-stays. House clothes and regular cleaning, as well as main ingredients for self-made breakfast, are provided.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

Volotea links more than 50 destinations with a fleet consisting exclusively of Boeings 717, equipped with Rolls-Royce engines. It also links small and medium European cities by means of direct flights at competitive prices, and always in a way that people spend more time at the destination rather than aboard the plane

By offering reliable and financially sustainable services, the main purpose of TAAG is the promotion of the Angolan image all over the world, leading the modernization of the national air transport and related services. It provides direct flights between Luanda and the capitals of some of the greatest international business and tourist centres.

The Shining View is located at the historic area, 550 meters away from Praça da Ribeira. The 8 apartments have a colourful decoration, kitchen and some of them have a balcony overlooking the Douro river. All the furnished accommodations include a living area and a dining room in an open plan, with a sofa and a dining table. Each one also includes free Wi-Fi access, television and a private bathroom, with free personal hygiene products. The Shining View is served by an elevator and has a luggage room. There is free parking nearby.
A minimum stay of 2/3 nights is required.

Machado Joalheiro is a company dedicated to the sale of jewels and watches of recognized international brands, such as Cartier, Chaumet, Hublot, Vacheron Constantin, IWC, Pomellato, Dior, Chanel or TAG Heuer.

Oporto Loft is located in the centre of Porto - right in the Arts district - 50 meters from the gardens of Palácio de Cristal and a 10 minute walk from the historical city centre where you can visit the famous Livraria Lello, the iconic Torre dos Clérigos or enjoy the local nightlife.
The unit is housed in a historic building from the 19th century. Its architectural heritage has been maintained and the interior fully adapted to provide our guests with maximum comfort in a space entirely decorated by a local interior decorator. Our Art hotel offers differentiated themed rooms, with decoration and design influenced by traditional and contemporary art. At Oporto Loft you will be able to enjoy the serenity and beauty of our splendid 2000 square metre private garden, as well as our Wine House, a place to socialize and enjoy a wide variety of national wines. Free Wi-Fi is provided throughout the unit including the garden. We also have a Gallery, a multifunctional space that fills the walls of the Breakfast Room, Wine House, Living Room and Garden.

50 meters away from the beach, all the apartments offer a unique interior design, enriched by the Flattered Art Collection. The building has 5 units with daily cleaning service, equipped kitchens, minibar service and wine cellar, and all the apartments have a private library. The homemade breakfast is served in a woven basket at the apartment. Customers may also enjoy the exclusive Flattered services: Hatha Yoga sessions, massages, babysitting, Life Change consulting, Eco tours  walking and biking tours  and Personal Concierge service.
A minimum stay of 2 nights is required.

Located near the Church of Lapa, the Gz Hostel offers luminous, modern rooms in a cosy environment. It is a small hostel with only three rooms: one with six bunk beds and shared bathroom, a twin room and a double room. It provides free Wi-Fi access to Internet, and has a living room with television and video player, kitchen for small meals, terrace with panoramic view over the city and also a garden. Gz can also help the guests in order for them to organize trips through the city, have surf classes and also geocaching. It should be noted that it accepts pets.

Serving Surf since 2003, Godzilla Surf s Cool - Surf School s philosophy is to provide its students with professional help to attain a high level of quality, through an accredited, experienced and highly motivated team. Run by Rodrigo Eça de Queiroz de  Lacerda  Godzilla , a surfer with more than 20 years of experience, Godzilla Surf s Cool also aims at attracting and training new generations of surfers. To achieve this, it has various support infrastructures, quality equipment and a programme of activities according to the level of surfing, which consists of  Personal Training , specialised fitness preparation, yoga and sports massage.

Top Bike Tour also provides an urban and trekking bike rental service, TERN and KROSS, to enjoy the wonderful Porto landscape. Products: Walking tours – Bike tours – Cycling holidays.Fold n’ visit is a shop designed for a rental of folding city bikes. It also offers other services such as full workshop service and selling of bicycles Tern, Cross, Haibike electric and of XLC accessories.

This jewellers’ shop was founded in 1880 and is a Listed Building  Imóvel de Interesse Público . It sells luxury pieces by prestigious brands and its own collections of jewellery, combining modernity and tradition. It is a family business that has passed on values and traditions from generation to generation.

In operation since 2010, this is a craft shop with works from urban designers and Portuguese artists. Transformation and reuse forms the conceptual basis for its designs, often demonstrating that there are no limits on creativity, and that what for some is waste, in the hands of others is art.

The Open University is the only public institution of higher education for Distance Learning, offering degree-level courses  first degrees, masters and doctorates  and Lifelong Learning courses anywhere in the world. The teaching programme is in accordance with the Bolonha Process and since 2008 all courses have been taught via e-learning. This was the year in which the Open University became a leader in its field in Europe in e-learning as a consequence of recognition of its Virtual Teaching Model.

The Surfing Life Club is the first surf school in Porto, established in 1994. It has always provided a unique program of sports, animation and tourism in the North of Portugal. It offers regular classes with permanent camp monitors, to equipment stores with technical counselling, surf safaris, surf camps, and classes in schools, town halls and companies as well as social projects for disabled people and support to bathing entertainment.

FEUP is an institution concerned with the generation, transmission and dissemination of knowledge, technology and culture in the field of Engineering.  Bolstered by  Research and Development, one of its aims is to prepare future engineers for the excercise of the profession at an international level whilst bringing to bear relevant scientific, technical, ethical and cultural perspectives.

The Porto Business School of the Porto University’s offers postgraduate education and advanced training in the management area. It is also committed to a constant intervention within applied research and supporting companies with varied services.

Located 12 km from Porto, it is a private club for members only but with limited access to golfers with a handicap certificate and registered with the relevant Golf Federation. 
Founded in 1931, it is a par 70 course, 35 for each 9 holes.  It has excellent holes and greens, particularily the 7th and 9th greens, as well as a great par 4 which is notebly challenging and longer in distance, with a very fast green.

The Portuense Fluvial Club created in 1876, is the oldest sports association in the city of Porto and the third oldest in the country. br 
The activities available are rowing, swimming, synchronized swimming and water polo.

The White Box House is a typical Porto house from the beginning of the last century. It has been designed to serve all those looking for new experiences on their travels but who, at the same time, enjoyed the welcoming return home.  This guesthouse, with its peaceful garden at the back, combines the charm of the past with a cosmopolitan ambience.  Located on the most traditional and lively pedestrian street in Porto, it is an ideal starting point for exploring the city.
Surf, body board and skate school. It sells and rents material from various brands: Rip Curl, Quiksilver, Insight, Obey, Analog, Polen, Pukas, O&E, Komunity, Turbo, Pride and Stealth.

The Porto Lounge Hostel & Guesthouse, housed in its fully restored and modernised building, combines the original architecture of the 18th century within a modern elegant interior.  It has mixed dormitories and one female dormitory, but also private rooms with bathrooms, suitable for couples, families and small groups of friends.  It is the perfect place for groups to stay.

Located 40 Km from Porto, it was founded in 1989. It has lots of vegetation, 1200 square metres and one of the fastest greens in the country.  
18-hole course, offering the following services: driving range, 
putting green, chipping & bunker, 
club hire, trolleys, buggies, non-metal spikes only, golf shop, terrace, restaurant, snack bar, and bar.

The VINUM Restaurant is closed at weekends in order to prevent the Covid-19 outbreak.
We decided to take this preventive step thinking about the health and well-being of all our customers and employees. We believe it is a measure that will contribute to a lesser spread of the virus as well as a quicker return to normality.
We would like to invite you to visit us from Monday to Friday, for an Executive Lunch, à la carte or a dinner with a view overlooking the river.
We will be happy to provide you with a good food and wine pairing experience.


The restaurant is part of the Graham s Cellars, in Vila Nova de Gaia, built in 1890 and today completely restored. It consists of the lounge and a  Winter Garden  room with a privileged view over the Douro River and the Luis I Bridge. The cuisine uses ingredients sourced in the North of Portugal, based in a new experience: from the best fruits and vegetables from the interior of the country, to the flour ground in a stone mill that serves as the basis for our artisan bread, the “world’s best fish” that arrives daily from the Matosinhos Market, or our exclusive old cow meat from Trás-os-Montes. An almost forgotten cuisine that enriches, comforts and win us over.
Founded in 1915, it has the following activities: tennis, squash, 5-a-side synthetic grass football, capoeira, taekwondo, kickboxing, aerobics, and ballroom dancing.

local: Centro de Educação Física Jorge Santos
descricao:
Located near the Boavista area, it has 2000 m2 of modern facilities.  
Cardio fitness, gyms, beauty salon, bodybuilding, swimming pools, sauna, spinning, Turkish bath and swimming are some of the services available.

Located about 20 km from Porto, on a space with a total of 3700 square metres, it offers a Kart track with about 300 metres.

Bar, coffee-shop and restaurant are also available.


Opening Hours  Except from July 15th to September 7th :
Tuesday to Thursday: 4pm-6:30pm / 9pm-12:30am
Friday: 4pm-6:30pm / 9om-2am
Saturday: 3pm-7pm / 9pm-2am
Sunday: 3pm-7pm / 9pm-12:30am

Located in Matosinhos and created in 1979, it was the first permanent kart track in Portugal.   
The track is 980 metres in circumference and 7 metres wide.  
It has a bar which is open at the weekend.   
It also has a workshop, available to any kart owner, with two mechanics who repair engines and carry out chassis tuning.

Pleasant space with natural light in all rooms, studios and pool.  
Activities available - body balance, body combat, body pump, pilates, kick boxing, power jump, weight training and cardio fitness, cycle, x55, localised training, Karate, mtv dance, abs, water aerobics and swimming.

Located by the exotic Garden of Passeio Alegre, this mini-golf circuit is considered one of the world’s best by those who played the European Championship there in 2012. Have fun in this open space with your friends and family, enjoying this completely renewed circuit.

Located in Matosinhos, in a covered area of 3200 m2, it has a track with a circumference of 330 m.  
It has a games area with 2 snooker tables, 1 billiards table, as well as a terrace that allows you to watch the kart racing track.
Located in the area of Foz and part of the Premier group, it has various sports from cardio fitness, step, bodybuilding, karate, jump fit, water aerobics, hip hop and rhythmic dance.

The buildings that flank Rua da Galeria de Paris, which first opened in 1903, have elegant and graceful lines, particularly number 28, built in the art nouveau style.  During the second half of the 20th century, cloth warehouses took up most of the building space in the street.  From being a relatively uninhabited part of downtown Porto, Rua da Galeria de Paris quickly changed into what has become known as one of the liveliest parts of Porto.  It all began in 2007, with the opening of a bar in an old bookshop.  The variety of events on offer, particular the jazz concerts, soon triggered the appearance of other venues. Together, these have turned the area into a high point of evening entertainment in Porto, particularly at the weekends.

local: Centro Cultural e Desportivo dos Trabalhadores da CMP
descricao: The CCDTCMP is a nonprofit association, of public interest, acting in social, education and youth, sports, culture and leisure areas. It offers several sports such as the underwater activities, with classes starting on the basic level. It has a gymnasium, the Body&Soul, offering a relaxed atmosphere and a personalized attention. Karate-Do with self-defense techniques of Shito-Ryu style can be learned by practitioners from 5 years old on. Children and youngsters from 3 to 16 years old can practice football at the Hernâni Gonçalves Football School.

Founded in 1987, it is the most recent of the sailing clubs in Leixões. With its head office at the North Pier of Leixões, next to the Marina do Porto Atlântico, its activity focuses on sailing cruises.
It has been known for organising various events on a national level, such as the Tour of Portugal / Portugal Sailing, regional and national championship qualifications for Match Racing and Rabelo boat regattas.   
On an international level, it has organised several events of the Land Rover Cup, an international Match Racing competition, and has also participated in the Rally Portugal, an event organised annually by the World Cruising Club, and Hong Kong Challenge.

Proud of our national symbols, we hope that they will be taken from here to the four corners of the world.  Combining innovation with humour can result in some surprising products.  Now you can buy a Barcelos rooster in cartoon form or with a cockscomb, plume or sequins. Portugal is no longer just Fado and Longing. It is also Humour. We customize all the pieces at your request.

Sporting body, recognised by the Portuguese Surfing Federation, is dedicated to teaching surf.   
It has practical and theoretical classes both videotaped and photographed in order to show the physical preparation and specific technique necessary to practise the sport as well as preparing athletes for top level competition.  It also promotes the values of  sportsmanship , particularly among the youngest surfers.

local: Stay In Apartments - S. Francisco de Borja I
descricao: Located by the typical and charming Ribeira in Porto, the S. Francisco de Borja apartment was completely renewed and equipped as to offer the best comfort to its users. “Two steps” away from the Douro River and by the main attraction points in the city, it’s the perfect lodging for anyone wanting to get to know Porto.
Surf school dedicated to the teaching and practice of surfing, also organising surf championships and sports and fitness events. With attitude and safety, Onda Pura offers a unique experience.
Located on the North Pier in Leixões, Clube Naval de Leça, it divides its activities into several sections: sailing, diving and recreational fishing.

Favorita is not just another design hotel, but a house where everyone will feel at home, and that offers a blend of modern furniture and vintage items. Pensão Favorita was designed to provide efficient public spaces and room and bathroom organization. The spirit of this old Portuguese house lives its second life thanks to the contemporary approach suggesting an easy and comfortable lifestyle. It has twelve rooms, restaurant with terrace and garden.

local: First Rent  - Comércio e Aluguer de Viaturas S.A
descricao: FirstRent operates mainly in Portugal and provides service points in Porto, Matosinhos, Braga, Famalicão, Lisboa and Algarve. It provides mobility and autonomy at a low cost price, offering the best solution to meet your needs.

Handicraft exclusively made by Portuguese artisans. Visit us and find the best we do in Portugal, including ceramics, embroidery, filigree, cork, soaps, tiles, as well as certified gourmet products, olive oil, liquors, chocolates, biscuits, jams, etc...

Nautical tourism company settled in the Douro Marina. It seeks to provide unique experiences following each person’s preferences, offering exclusive boats to be rented by groups, friends, family, for rides on the River Douro, with skipper. Every client may choose his own options to explore the area, as well as different entertainment activities and tasting of food and wine from the Douro demarcated region, and also other significant landmarks as the cities of Porto and Guimarães using private road transportation.

With a spectacular view of the mountains of Marão and Aboboreira, it is a course classified by the Handicap Committee and Course Rating of the Portuguese Golf Federation.

Before you start playing, you should always consult the handicap tables  for 9 and 18 holes  to determine your game handicap at Amarante Golf.

It has various services: reception, golf shop, bar, terrace, restaurant, showers, lockers, buggy, trolley and golf club rental, putting green, driving range and equipment repair shop. At the golf school you also have classes available for adults and juniors, packages of classes and golf clinics for business activities.

In 2010, with the support of Turismo de Portugal, Mário Pardo developed an international project of parachuting and created the 1st Centre for the modality in Portugal, the Sydive Europe, located on Alentejo. Directing the company Get High, Mário Pardo and his team keep sharing smiles and in 2013 they embraced another audacious project: to jump over the sea, at the Espinho aerodrome, a very beautiful city, bathed by the sea, thus giving birth to a place where you can share strong emotions. Skydive Europe – Espinho is the departure point for one of the biggest adventures of your life!

Nata Lisboa is a coffee shop located in the city centre serving mainly the “pastel de nata”  custard on pastry . The world is too sour and angry. It needs to be sweetened. It needs more joy and good mood. It needs the wonderful and comforting taste of a “pastel de nata” coming out of the oven. The brand NATA was created to take this delicious original recipe from Lisbon to the entire world, and satisfy many nata lovers and conquer more clients. The NATA mission is huge and needs more allies. Join the movement and help make the world a sweeter place.

Chic n’Cheap – Silver Outlet displays a new concept of presentation and sale of silver products.  From traditional silver, such as filigree, to a variety of products for women, men and children, as necklaces, earrings, rings, bracelets, and many others.

This set of 3 flats, each with capacity for four people, dates from the nineteenth century. Its contemporary and functional design turns this apartment into the perfect accommodation for long and short stays, and principally, for those who intend to get to know the city. With about 60 m2 in area each, it comprises a fully equipped kitchen, private bathroom, living room with double sofa-bed and a lovely terrace that extends around the whole apartment. The guests have at their disposal:  towels, bed sheets, duvets, shower gel, shampoo, and salt, pepper, olive oil, vinegar, tea and coffee for the kitchen. The time of arrival must be always communicated. The required minimum stay is 2 nights.

local: Honorary Consulate of the Republic of Croatia
descricao: The Consulate of the Republic of Croatia looks after the interests of Croatian citizens.

local: Palácio das Sereias  Palácio da Bandeirinha
descricao: The name that it is known for comes from exotic and gigantic sirens that flank its doorway. The building was constructed in the mid-18th century for residence of the family Portocarrero in a place where an old Jewish quarter was located. The Portocarrero family left the palace in 1809, the time when the populace slaughtered their son deeming him to be involved in the French invasion. Since then the palace remained closed until 1955, being sold to the Institute of the Daughters of Charity  Instituto das Filhas da Caridade  that set up there the college Casa Madalena de Canossa which still runs in present days.

Moustache Coffee House is settled in a cosmopolitan space, where you can totally breed and feel coffee. The two-floor building at Praça Carlos Alberto was recovered to create a balance between an open relation with the outside and an intimate space on the inside. You can come in, address the counter and simply order to take or remain in the coffee house and enjoy. Architecture, design, events, chats, books, musical selection and live acts give it the final touch.

The Duke of Ribeira  Deocleciano Monteiro  worked as a boatman in the River Douro and became the most famous figure in the Ribeira of Porto, being paid various tributes. The square next to the D. Luís I Bridge was named after him, with a tombstone having been laid, with a sculpture by José Rodrigues.
Raised to the status of public figure, the Duke cohabited with different Portuguese and foreign figures, and his autograph book contained the signatures of England´s Quenn Elizabeth II, Portuguese presidents Ramalho Eanes and Mário Soares, and of the president of Mozambique Samora Machel, among many others.

Situated in the parish of Ramalde, Casa da Prelada and the surrounding garden, where one of the largest labyrinths of the Iberian Peninsula is located, are part of the largest landscape complex designed by the Italian architect Nicolau Nasoni - Quinta da Prelada. Until the early 20th century, the Casa and Quinta da Prelada belonged to the Noronha e Menezes family. In 1903, the last owner, D. Francisco de Noronha e Menezes, donated the property to Misericórdia do Porto. After deep refurbishment works have been carried out, the Casa is now a cultural space where the Historical Archive of Santa Casa da Misericórdia and a library stand out, both are open to the public.

Access to the gardens for people with reduced mobility is possible although not in the entire space.

Portarossa is located in a quiet and peaceful street of Foz, recreating a typical Italian space. Here you can find a variety of pizzas and pastas in a relaxed and very cool environment. It’s already known by many as the Little Italy of the city of Porto.

local: Spa Wellness Center do Hotel Solverde
descricao: The modern Spa & Wellness Center of the Hotel Solverde is duly equipped in the interior part: indoor and dynamic swimming pool  with varied stations of jets, underwater bicycle and cross-trainer , both with heated sea water  gym, relaxation studio, sauna rea, treatment room, 2 resting rooms  one with wave dream  and physical and rehabilitation medicine office  in the exterior part – seawater outdoor swimming pool, tennis courts, multifunctional court, beach volleyball and minigolf court, football field and playground.
The shop in the Stock Exchange Palace intends to surprise its visitor with traditional souvenirs to offer or to remember long after.

The jewellery shop of the Stock Exchange Palace presents an ancient art dating back to around 2500 BC, well appreciated around the world. With varied purity indexes in Portugal, the gold mainly worked on is that of 19.2 carats, the reason why it is labelled as Portuguese Gold, one of the most precious worldwide.
With the city of Gondomar representing 50-60% of Portuguese jewellery industry, we had to count with the goldsmiths of this municipality not to let the most prominent Portuguese craft fall into oblivion.
Feel invited to visit this space that you will surely not leave indifferent to.

It was opened in 1784 to increase the lenght of an old road, by order of João de Almada e Mello. Bordering with the secular Collegiate of Cedofeita, it turned into an important residential zone. Nowadays it is one of the city´s main commercial areas. The street is flanked with buildings constructed from the 17th to the 20th centuries. During the siege of Porto, the regent D. Pedro made his headquarters in the building at the number 395, after the bombardment of the zone of the Carrancas Palace.

A Lavandeira is a Portuguese self-service launderette, located in two cozy spaces in the historic area of Porto. The process is practical and intuitive, available in multiple languages. The payment machine accepts coins and banknotes up to 20€. The washers don’t open while washing!

In the18th century, in the place of the so called ´Campo das Hortas`, was inaugurated the Praça Nova  New Square  - the city´s political and cultural centre. The City Hall stood on the northern side of the old square, and it was demolished when the Avenida dos Aliados was built. This wide avenue, flanked by a group of buildings from the 20s and 40s, is closed on the southern side by the Cardosas building  19th century , and at North by the City Hall. In the centre of the Praça da Liberdade  Liberty Square  stands the king´s D. Pedro IV statue, inaugurated in 1866.

Designed by Nicolau Nasoni, the garden that involves the Casa da Prelada also conceived by the architect, presents two distinctive areas: one to the south, concerning the yard which is accessed through a monumental armored gateway built in the 17th century  the other to the north with the labyrinth, the lake and orchard. The boxwood labyrinth is considered one of the biggest of the Iberian Peninsula and certainly the only exemplar of the 18th century that the city has. Within the arboreal and shrub inventory the following stand out: boxwood, araucaria, rhododendron, camellia japonica.
Restaurant on the mouth of Douro river, that serves traditional Portuguese food and stands out by the quality and tranquillity. It also has a take-away service.

During centuries it was called Praça dos Ferradores  Square of Blacksmiths , who had their workshops there.In the 18th century, the Palace of Viscondes de Balsemão was built in the east side of the Square. Rented out at a later time, it served as a guesthouse and hosted the king Charles Alber from Sardinia that came exiled to Porto. In his honour the Square bears its current name. In the garden´s centre, a monument by Henrique Moreira remembers the Portuguese who died during the Great War 1914-18.

The Adega Alfredo  O Portista , situated in the historical centre, is one of the city´s traditional restaurants where you can try different Portuguese tidbits. The walls are covered in blue and white tiles, full of paintings, posters, small figures and references to the “Futebol Clube do Porto”.

Built by order of Ferreira dos Santos in 1953, this coffee shop designed by the architect Carlos Neves, who chose a modernity-inclined shadow approach with lampshades. The decoration, entirely referring to the conquest of Ceuta, is one of the shop´s particularities. Ceuta is one of the rare coffee shops from the 50s still operating and also one of the most emblematic in the city. Within many years it was a meeting place for friends, ministers, congressmen, figures such as Sottomayor Cardia, Montalvão Machado, Eurico de Melo and some members of the Sá Carneiro family.

local: Museum of Porto s Hospital Centre
descricao: Take a trip back in time and come and visit a remarkable 19th-centurye apothecary incorporated into the front of the Hospital de Santo António, a national monument whose origins date back over 200 years. From a rare collection of cupboards to pots, jars, tinctures, weighing scales and formula books, there are many objects which will take you back to the therapies of the age. In another area dedicated to the pharmacy of the Hospital Joaquim Urbano you can find out about the history, people, techniques and progress made in producing registered medicines at this important hospital specialising in infectious and pulmonary diseases. Every month we look at one particular item from the huge institutional holdings for a more in-depth examination of the area of health sciences.

Around 1860, the mouth of the river Douro was little more than a fishing village. The way that led from there to Matosinhos used to be called Estrada de Carreiros  paths . The designation Carreiros  paths  refers to the fact that it was a path through which rode oxcarts with their drivers. 
In 1930 council projects concerning the embellishment of Avenida do Brasil were approved  the year after, the pergola was concluded.

Situated in the city centre, this street was already known as Rua de Santo António. It owes its name to the historical republican revolt that broke out in Porto in 1891. Nowadays it is an important commercial artery with some examples of art nouveau buildings.

local: Casa da Rua de São Miguel
descricao: Crossing the Rua de S. Bento da Vitória and the Rua de São Miguel you will find the House of Rua de São Miguel, on no. 4. The tiles dating from the 19th century that cover it and make it so special and different depict episodes from the life of Our Lady and general everyday life scenes. The street called S. Miguel was one of the main streets within the Olival New Jewish Quarter, created in 1386 following the instructions of the king D. João I.

In charge of almost 12 km of documentation of permanent conservation, the District Archive of Porto is a living body of history, information, stories and a technological vision of reference that connects it with any person in any part of the world. br 
It bears the symbolic weigh of being a regional entity with the integrated management of memory and heritage, promoter in safeguarding the identity and the rights of the State and citizens. Services: on-site/virtual/online  website: pesquisa.adporto.pt  reference and reading  document reproduction  archive system consulting/auditing  acquisitions  facilities rental/concession, etc.
Small restaurant serving Portuguese cuisine, situated in the city centre, well known for the famous shank sandwiches  sandes de pernil .

It is since 1929 that`Badalhoca` has been making delights for people from all social groups, that look just for one thing: the crusty bread filled with flavorsome sliced ham. The prices are reasonable and the place has relaxing atmosphere. The decoration is rustic, with legs of smoked ham hanging from the ceiling, and also the references to the Boavista Football club are not missing.
Restaurant known for the smoked meat and ham from Lamego. Situated in the historical centre, next to Praça da Batalha, brings flavours of Portugal to the heart of Porto.

Casas do Porto offers apartments for rent in the city centre, recently refurbished, modern and fully furnished. The apartments are situated next to the city´s main tourism attractions, 50 meters from the Stock Exchange Palace and 100 meters away from the riverside. The São Bento Railway station and metro station are within a short 5-minute walk, as well as Port Wine Cellars, the Clérigos Tower, Avenida dos Aliados and the city´s main churches and monuments. All the apartments have cable TV and free Wi-Fi.

The Adega Rio Douro or Tasca da Piedade is a place where varied Portuguese tidbits are served, in a real atmosphere of typical Porto, with the Riverside breeze of Douro to complement.

Every Tuesday between 4 and 7.30pm, it offers afternoons with `fado vadio` music.

The street of São Miguel constituted one of the main streets of Judiaria Nova do Olival  Olival New Jewish quarter , constructed in 1386 upon order of king D. João I. In the corner with Rua de São Bento da Vitória, under number 4, there is the Casa da Rua de S. Miguel. The tiles from 19th century that make it special and so different represent Our Lady’s life episodes and everyday routines.

Place where you can taste and pair Portuguese wine and specialties under the guidance of the renowned Chef Miguel Castro Silva. Food embraces the concept of innovation, diversification and modernity of the Porto Cruz Space project, challenging visitors to enjoy new tasting experiences in an informal, but selected setting. Sharing dishes among friends, without formalities, means tasting more and varied specialties.

Laventura is a shop that specializes in outdoor activities. It displays a range of quality products at good price for the outdoor activities: Trail running, trekking, in the mountain or day-to-day activities, with a wide variety of national and foreign cutlery, fishing equipment, paintball, airsoft, air pressure and archery, and markets the best brands such as North Face, Petzl, Peli, Victorinox, Icel, Zwilling, 3 claveles and Emboscada.

Museu d Avó is one of those places that invites to a long dinner of tidbits with friends before going out late at night, both because of its location in one of the city´s most trendy zones close to Casa de Ló, and because it serves food until late at night.

The place is relaxed and welcoming, with mostly candle-lighting, wooden tables and chairs and the most varied objects that we can find in our grannies´ houses. The ambient is youthful and lively, with a casual and friendly atmosphere that makes us feel at home. Since the room is small and appealing for a long meal, it is advisable to make a booking from a certain time on.

local: Oporto City Flats – Ayres Gouvea House
descricao: Situated in the centre of Porto, Oporto City Flats – Ayres Gouvea House has modern self-sufficient studio apartments with a private balcony. They have a fully equipped kitchenette, high ceilings with wooden beams and parquet floor as well as private toilet, free Wi-fi and a living room with a sofá and a flat panel TV set with cable channels. They even have a garden and bike rental service. The guests can opt for preparing their own meals in the self-sufficient studio or enjoying the local cuisine that Porto has to offer.

Taberna do Largo is a place where wines, cheese, sausages and many other Portuguese regional products mark their presence. You can try them and also buy to enjoy it later, at your place. Its principle is the direct contact with local producers of excellence, whose products are still little known in our city, thus trying to help them become known and make their products of great quality available to everyone.

In the city centre, next to Casa da Música and the art galleries zone, in renewed buildings from the 19th century, the apartments Three Houses offer an elegant and contemporary atmosphere with quality equaling the best charming hotels. It comprises 9 studios and apartments, fully equipped with WC, kitchenette, air conditioning, TV and free Wifi, with capacity for up to 12 people.

At Petiscaria Santo António we come to work with the purpose of answering a very important question - your appetite! And it has been that way since 2009, three friends decided to restore an old tasca  pub  in Porto, which since the 1940s served their snacks and drinks, typically Portuguese.
The point is that no one knew how to cook! But fortunately, the mother of one of them knew and well the traditional Portuguese recipes and so was born in the centre of Porto, a place where the snack is king!
Porto is our city and it shows! But the snacks we serve and the products that we offer are from all over Portugal, chorizo of Alentejo, tinned food of Azores, Douro wine, cheese from the Beiras, alheiras of Trás-os-Montes, and we want to share them with you!
Enough talking about us, let s talk about you. How s your appetite?
Tascö specializes in Portuguese tapas, made for order and served in aluminum dishes, also offering an uncommon wine list.

Aduela is a tavern-bar situated in front of the Carlos Alberto Theatre that serves drinks including wine by glass and good traditional Portuguese tidbits. The tavern is modern and has beautiful features such as the balcony that once was a judge’s bench. The street´s characteristics allow people to gather up and drink outside the premises.

Alma Portuense offers flavours inspired by Porto´s lifestyle. With two floors and a terrace, the decoration pays homage to the emblematic views and places in Porto. The menu presents traditional Portuguese dishes and some specialties such as Alma Steak and steak in puf pastry.

So Cool Hostel Porto is located within a short distance from the main places of cultural interest – art galleries, theatres, restaurants, bars and clubs, as well as shops of traditional commerce. 
The building, constructed in the 19th century, was refurbished in 2013, resulting in a harmonious mixture of styles. The contemporary minimalist style combined with the spectacular original architecture where luminosity reigns! In a cosy atmosphere for you to feel at home, guests will be able to choose from comfortable and spacious dormitories or cosy double rooms, and still enjoy the garden and the kitchen.
Taxca was opened in September 2013 in the heart of Porto´s city centre. It serves the famous smoked ham sandwich from Badalhoca, a well-known typical restaurant of Porto.

Trinkas delivers its clients a `beyond the palate` journey through Portugal. It seeks to present the most typical and traditional that our regional gastronomy has. Products are certified as homemade whose quality and taste are a heritage passed on from generation to generation. Sausages and smoked meats, sweets and cheese, olive oil and wines, traditional dishes and tidbits conceived with a touch and taste of homemade cooking.
Saboriccia markets regional products exclusively of Portuguese origin and from small producers. The shop provides daily tasting of regional products and thematic tasting on Saturdays.
Feelmoto is a company that hires AT bikes  2 or 4 wheels . It also organizes courses for driving Moto and Moto4, Moto and Moto4 AT tours for all levels and raids.

The three apartments of Porto Foz Velha 4 Flats have generous spaces, good sun exposure, and are of easy access, presenting designed decoration. They are a perfect place to stay whatever the plans are for visiting Porto, and a perfect base from which to explore the North of Portugal. They are situated in the Foz Velha area, close to the river and the sea, served by public transport and local businesses.

Situated at the very heart of Porto´s historical centre, with a view over the majestic Douro River, the Ribeira do Porto Hotel is located in a central and privileged zone in Porto, Praça da Ribeira, and is placed in a historical building that was completely renovated, maintaining the traditional architecture.

It has double and superior rooms, as well as double rooms for individual use to meet the needs of its guests. All the rooms are equipped with a wide range of commodities, Including Internet access, air conditioning, safe, telephone, TV and full bathroom.

local: Treze a Rir Uns dos Outros
descricao: This sculptural set in bronze, a work by the Spanish sculptor Juan Muñoz, was donated to the city by the Spanish company Crédito y Caución, S.A. during Oporto 2001 – European Capital of Culture. It comprises 4 sets of rows of seats where we can see different groups of gabby and smiling people that average out 13 figures. Each of them displays a singular movement, sometimes leaning forward or in an unbalanced position falling off the seat, always with a prevailing feature in common: hilarious laugh expression.
Saboriccia markets regional products exclusively of Portuguese origin and from small producers. The shop provides daily tasting of regional products and thematic tasting on Saturdays.

Collectus is a shop selling varied items valued by collectors, located at the heart of Porto. The place brings modern and old times together, in the attempt to help to preserve the latter in the collective memory. It is the right place to welcome collection lovers, people who are curious about our local or national past, tourists who look for an alternative souvenir from Porto or lovers of postcrossing, to name a few. Stamps, postcards, coins, notes, matches, cans, toys, calendars, sticker albums, old magazines, etc.

Traditional shop located in Rua Formosa, in parallel with the most famous street in terms of shopping – Rua Santa Catarina. With over 100 years, this house has many traditional products to offer: chorizos, Portuguese kind of sausage  Alheira , cheese, wines and jams. All potential buyers can try some of the products that the shop sells: alheiras, grilled chorizos and cheese. Tapas  small dishes  are also a specialty of this traditional house, accompanied by wine by the glass.
Portuguese Beauty is a shop that specializes in marketing Portuguese products of high quality, located in Rua das Flores, 255.

Wine shop with an extensive offer of wine products with Port Wine being the leading one. It organizes wine tastings and sells wine by glass.
We serve tapas, ham and/or cheese board with bread basket and our fantastic chips  Meat pie and fried food  Sangria and cocktails.

We are EFun Tours, a tourist animation company of the city of Porto and we have been in the market since 2013 and we are a reference in the city of Porto  Invicta  in terms of guided tours in Porto and Northern Portugal. We hold Full & Half day Tours in minivan and Walking tours in the city.

CRU is a designer’s product shop and also a coworking space located in the heart of the Art District of Porto. The shop main purpose is to represent the current best creators, by exposing and promoting their work and to give lovers of the arts and designer pieces, a careful selection of pieces, at attractive prices.
Conceived and designed for a practical, collaborative and unprejudiced artistic community, CRU Cowork leases several workspaces, sharing and collaboration, mainly directed to creative activities, in the 410m2 of its facilities.
Shop of the prestigious Spanish brand Intropia where you can find a complete collection of young women´s fashion, for any occasion.

Opened in March 2013, Chocolataria das Flores is a coffee/pastry shop dedicated to chocolates, biscuits and homemade jams, among other delicacies. For a snack you will find there pieces of homemade cakes, brownies, sponge cakes, toasts made from Mafra bread type, accompanied by tea or hot chocolate. You can also have a light lunch based on savoury snacks: quiche, meat fritters, pastries, hot dogs, etc., all made by the owner. One of the most irresistible combinations that you can find there is chocolate with Port Wine.

Fashion Clinic is a luxury store with four stores in Lisbon and Porto - Avenida da Liberdade, Avenida da Boavista and Palácio das Cardosas - which currently sells more than 60 ready-to-wear brands, accessories, shoes, perfumes, cosmetics and books. Main brands: Balenciaga / Balmain / Boglioli / Christian Louboutin / Givenchy / Golden Goose/ Gucci / Paul Smith/ Prada / Saint Laurent / Santoni / Tom Ford / Valentino

Scar-id Store is an independent design shop, Porto based, aimed at the internationalization of Portuguese designers. It sells and communicates a selection of products based on exclusivity, innovation and identity in the areas of fashion, accessories, furniture and product design. scar-id is a store and a gallery, features a careful selection of small collections, limited editions and unique pieces of global design.
Located in an old 1950s grocery store in the art district of Porto, scar-id is also a design laboratory, an architectural atelier and a photo studio.

The porcelain factory Vista Alegre was founded in 1824 in Ílhavo in central Portugal. Currently the branded pieces, always at the forefront of innovation and design, appear at the table of the most distinguished personalities, as well as in embassies and other state institutions and organizations or private individuals, being equally represented in different international museums. Combining tradition and modernity in a harmonious way, Vista Alegre Atlantis continues to invest in partnerships with designers and international artists, fledgling or consecrated ones, contributing this way to the continuous perfectioning of art in porcelain and crystal.

Opened in March 2013, Chocolataria das Flores is a coffee/pastry shop dedicated to chocolates, biscuits and homemade jams, among other delicacies. You can also have a light lunch based on savoury snacks: quiche, meat fritters, pastries, hot dogs, etc., all made by the owner. The jewellery Reis & Filhos was founded in 1880 by António Alves dos Reis. Still working as a shop, it is settled on the corner of Rua de Santa Catarina with Rua 31 de Janeiro and its main façade displays two deventures  shop front  made of cast iron and enrolled in the Nouveau Art movement, as confirmed by the elements that decorate the façade, namely the bust of a woman, upper end of the iron frame. These deventures were placed on the building in 1905 and were produced in Porto in the foundry Companhia Aliança.

In the city that saw the birth of “nce Henry , embark on “the discovery of wonderful things never seen before”. The World of Discoveries is an Interactive Museum and Theme Park that reconstructs the fantastic odyssey of the Portuguese navigators, crossing the oceans to the discovery of a world until then unknown. This meeting place of cultures proposes to honour this period and those brave ones that introduced the notion of globalization. It’s the cultural complement that will forever change your city experience.
