Opened in June 2010, the Oriental Park boasts an approximate area of 10 hectares of landscape and has served the population with its green part, with a considerable rise  of inflow of users recently being observed. Its vegetation has notably developed, with recurrent planting interventions still ongoing, in order to ensure the aesthetic and scenic part, aiming at contributing to the population´s well-being, ensuring the place´s sustainability and promoting its biodiversity.

Situated in the emblematic Museum of Futebol Clube do Porto, the Museum Caffé offers you the best suggestions for breakfast, refreshments, lunch and snacks and group dinners available upon booking. Every day the offer consists of three menus with a dish of the day and other suggestions and snacks. The Museum Caffé becomes a sport bar in the afternoons that precede the games, serving pork filets, breaded escalopes, steaks, hamburgers, fritters and cool drinks, in a real atmosphere of pre-match companionship.

Built within the event “Porto 2001 – European Capital of Culture”, the “Transparent Building” opened in June 2007. His author, the Catalan architect Solà-Morales, designates it a container building, an urban-meaning space, offering light to the city. It was later recovered by the Portuguese architect Carlos Prata, now offering a set of commercial spots dedicated to leisure, entertainment and culture, restaurants, sports shops  surf and cycling , among others. The Transparent Building seeks to be a link between the City Park and the sea, displaying vanguard and innovative traces and transparent walls.

It was originally a jewellery, and then it was bought by a Spanish businessman who wrote his name on the main façade. The construction is thought to have begun in 1914, when documents refer to the supply of the iron for the front of the shop  deventure  by the Porto foundry Companhia Aliança. Clearly inspired by the Nouveau Art movement are the interior and the façade of the shop. The façade displays large curved frames on cast iron covering the front. These iron frames, originally golden, are decorated with shells, flowers and leaves, elements enrolled with the Nouveau Art vocabulary. On top, the name of the shop – Vicent. I tis now a clothing shop.

Trás-os-Montes can be found in the Bom Sucesso market with its best traditional and regional flavours in the gourmet shop Sabores & Tradição. This shop offers a great variety of quality food products, from handmade smoked sausages  “alheiras” from Mirandela and Vinhais, “linguiça”, “salpicão” and “chouriça doce” , meat bread, olive oil and olives bread, olive oil, honey, biological marmalade, cookies, cheese and many other specialties. All products provided by Sabore & Tradição are strictly chosen and are biological and handmade, what makes them unique and authentic products.

The Surf Hostel is located in the ocean front - Foz do Douro. It’s within walking range from all the best surf spots in Porto, where the Surfivor surf lessons take place. The nearby beach of Matosinhos is excellent to learn how to surf. Nearby there s a wide choice of bars, clubs, restaurants and many cultural attractions showing the laid back and friendly lifestyle of the Portuguese!
The Escondidinho do Barredo is one of Porto´s  traditional restaurants, situated in the historical centre, where you can try Portuguese tidbits during the day.

A shop with a kitchen where cookery classes take place and a concept store with numerous temporary stores of fashionwear, homewear, kitchenwear, gourmet & lifestyle. Recently it has also a Food Bar permanently open so that you get to know the Portuguese contemporary gastronomy in light meals and enjoy a space full of history and tranquil atmosphere. An urban space of reference where to go is always worth it. 300 m2 in Porto city centre in constant activity and change. A special place for those who like to be surprised. Wine bar, wine shop and wine tasting spot offering a customized service and a strict selection of wines from all over the country, as well as a selection of cheese and sausages equally strict. The bar is settled in the first telephone central in Porto and it seeks to continue enhancing dialogues, conversations and debates by conducting workshops, courses, tasting, meetings with producers and wine and food moments.

Home17 is a totally recovered 1912 house, offering 6 double rooms, all with private bath and their own keys. The kitchen is totally equipped, open and shared with the living room, this one with a couch and TV. There’s a small terrace, free laundry and free wireless internet all over the house. We provide sheets and towels, flexible check-in/ check-out, washing machine, iron and iron board, dryer, equipped kitcken. The house is 5 minute-walk away from the city’s historical centre and close to restaurants, bars, shops and public transport. Home17 is located in the heart of the city.

Partybike is a joint bike concept with a great success in several European cities and now also in Porto! The Partybike supports 17 pax, 10 of them cycling, and allows you to explore the city in a new way, to celebrate special dates on wheels, to gather partners around a travelling bar or simply to drink beer or other alcohol-free drinks in an amusing and safe way. There are several predefined tours, but you we can also design a tour for a specific group.

Wine shop with an extensive offer of wine products with Port Wine being the leading one. It organizes wine tastings and sells wine by glass.

Located in the Vitória area, by the Church of Vitória, until very recently this staircase was known as “Escadas da Esnoga”, directly referring to the synagogue of the Olival Jewish Quarter  New Jewish quarter . This Jewish quarter was created with the king D. João I  fourteenth century , and was settled on Rua de S. Miguel where one can now find the building with nº 9. The stairway gave direct access to the Jewish temple, departing from Largo de São Domingos.

For foreign people visiting the city, this place offers interesting characteristics, from architecture to the promotion of local and quality products, combined with the friendliness of our staff. Background music is carefully selected.

The Bella Roma café, situated in the city centre, provides services in the field of catering and drink where you can enjoy nice atmosphere combined with quality sweets, savory treats, meals, drinks and grill. The specialties are miniature sweets and savories that are placed daily on the display that delight anyone, especially from the pictures of the foreigners that visit the shop. What is more, it has a kitchen where traditional Portuguese dishes are prepared daily and not only, combined with a value for money.

La Petite Coquette is settled in the iconic building of Armazéns da Capela – A Pompadour – at 25, Rua Cândido dos Reis, Clérigos area, selling second hand luxury articles. local: Depósito de Materiais da Fábrica das Devesas  old ceramics warehouse
descricao: The building was constructed from 1899 to 1901, by initiative of António de Almeida Costa, the founder of the Devesas Ceramics Factory, and served as Porto’s warehouse and showroom for the ceramic materials produced at the factory in Vila Nova de Gaia. Its originality is based on a combination of smooth and relief-pattern tiles decorating its entire main façade and the façade on Rua da Conceição. Another distinctive characteristic accentuating the building’s neo-Islamic character is revealed by the horseshoe-shaped windows and the platband with ceramic merlons interwoven with ceramic vases.

local: Museu de Anatomia Prof. Nuno Grande do ICBAS
descricao: Museu de Anatomia Prof. Nuno Grande do Instituto de Ciências Biomédicas de Abel Salazar  ICBAS  da Universidade do Porto  UP  is available for visits by advance booking. It is located next to the gardens of Palácio de Cristal  Rua Jorge Viterbo Ferreira, 228 . In addition to human body parts, it features a smaller section of Animal Anatomy, in particular of animals of veterinary interest or aquatic animals. The anatomical parts are arranged, in a unique room, in glass vials and which is particularly rich in the anatomy of the heart and vessels of the human body and also the anatomy of the central nervous system.

Our centre specialises in Surfski  canoeing  and Surfing. You can take a group tour  rowing  along the Douro River and pass under the 6 bridges. We also welcome suggestions for tours at other locations. Individual or group classes are available with specialised instructors in Surfski  canoeing , Surfing, Stand Up Paddle and Kayaksurf. Because of our ideal location on the Douro River mouth, these activities can be held on the ocean when conditions are favourable. All activities include insurance and showers.

The street called Rua de Santa Catarina das Flores was built in 1521 by order of King Manuel. This street, which was completed during a period of economic prosperity and urban expansion, attracted the city’s aristocracy who took up residence here and transformed it into one of the busiest commercial streets, to the detriment of Rua dos Mercadores, where trade began to falter thereafter. The street has maintained its original profile and currently boasts interesting civil architecture from the 17th, 18th and 19th centuries and one of Porto’s most emblematic baroque façades – the Church of Misericórdia  1749-1750 , designed by Nicolau Nasoni. It is one of the buzziest streets in the Historical Centre.

Authorship project: Inês Arisca, Pablo Cidad, André Eiras, Paula Rezende, Gustavo Carreiro, Rute Costa and Sphiza  Filipa Fonseca .

Miacomigo is a traditional Portuguese shop, where you can find accessories and women’s fashion by both Portuguese and foreign brands. There are carefully selected articles by the brands Hoss, Alba Conde, Oky Coky, Sita Murt, Kookai and Luís Buchinho. It is a sophisticated shop with a cozy and elegant ambience, offering a dedicated assistance. The shop is settled in the building of Palácio do Bolhão, a historical building designed by the architect Luís Pedro Silva. The fantastic lamp to be found in the shop gives it an unimaginable glamour.La miacomigo es una tienda portuguesa, de comercio tradicional de calle donde se puede encontrar accesorios y vestuarios femeninos de marcas portuguesas y extranjeras. El artigo es criteriosamente seleccionado de las marcas Hoss, Alba Conde, OkyCoky, Sita Murt, Kookai y LuisBuchinho. It is a sophisticated shop with a cozy and elegant ambience, offering a dedicated assistance. The shop is settled in the building of Palácio do Bolhão, a historical building designed by the architect Luís Pedro Silva. The fantastic lamp to be found in the shop gives it an unimaginable glamour.

The  Tendinha dos Acessorios   Boutique Accessories  began as an experiment to see if the  feedback  on the creation of customized fashion accessories was positive or not. In addition to the accessories made by the founder, the Boutique decided to travel the world to look for unique articles and therefore at its disposal it has some representations linked to fashion accessories, home spa, personalized soaps, and articles for the bath and fragrances for the home.
In addition to the referred articles, it also has at its disposal  Ready to wear  clothes, by prominent brands exclusive to the North.

The My Ribeira GuestHouse is located in Porto’s Ribeira district, a Unesco World Heritage Site, in a carefully restored building that dates back to 1834. The guest house has 9 elegantly decorated and exquisitely detailed apartments. My Ribeira Guesthouse offers a new style of accommodation. Breakfast is included and the apartments are equipped with a kitchenette and are cleaned daily. All the comforts a traveller might wish for. You will find that the owners of the guest house, with their extensive of experience of life in the city, are on hand daily to help you out. They aim to ensure your stay is as authentic and unique as possible and that the guest is the true centre of the experience.

As a brand, Kiko Ourivesarias  jeweller’s  aims to be something quite different in the market. For us, it is not enough just to provide our customers with the excellent service that allows them to purchase jewellery that is a perfect combination of design, value and price.
To create everlasting moments, we need to add something essential to the service and the pieces: emotion. This is our commitment, to generate, and help you generate, immeasurably beautiful and valuable emotions, through our pieces, which create unique, sublime, unforgettable and truly eternal moments.

The apartments distinguish themselves for their prime location, right in the heart of Porto, in front of the Bolhão Market, just 100m from the Bolhão Metro Station. They are located on the third floor of a historic 18th-century building that has been completely renovated. They offer all the comforts needed for a great stay and include free Wi-Fi.Fully equipped kitchen, sitting room with sofa-bed, flat-screen TV with cable channels, and a dining table.Modern bathroom with shower, hair dryer and all amenities included

La Copa opened on 21 June 2014 and is located by the Praça dos Poveiros, at the end of the Avenida Rodrigues de Freitas, on the limits of Porto’s historical area. A cosy place with background music and a private terrace “Terrazza La Copa”, where you can relax while enjoying our flavours. We are already a renowned ice-cream shop in the city. Ice-cream is handmade, daily produced in La Copa. Cream ice-creams are made exclusively with Vigor milk and fruit ice-creams exclusively with Água do Luso.

Quinta de Santa Cristina, located in Celorico de Basto, is part of the Vinhos Verdes Region, with a unique surrounding between the Marão, Alvão and Cabreira mountains. br 
The wine cellar combines modernity with tradition, with a contemporary design, integrated in the landscape, without neglecting traditional elements such as stone wine presses. br 
We offer authentic and unique experiences, and visitors have direct contact with the world of vinho verde, nature and local gastronomy through guided tours to the vineyards and wine cellar, wine/sparkling wine tastings, 4x4 tours, picnics or walking tours.

A restaurant specialising in different cuts of meat. Alternatives available for those who do not eat meat or prefer a lighter meal. A very cosmopolitan and trendy eatery, with a unique cocktail bar. Private events room for up to 16 people.

“Porto In A Bottle” is a wine cellar that originated in Porto’s historic centre in the renovated street called Rua das Flores. It’s a new wine shop concept, at which this region’s most representative alcoholic beverages are carefully selected and displayed. We decided to stock only Douro and Minho wines. br 
The shop offers a variety of drinks such as Port wine, table wine  verde, white, red and rosé , sparkling wines, grape brandies, cherry wines  ginginha , various liqueurs and some wine-related objects.

“Cantinho das Aromáticas” is located in Vila Nova de Gaia, very close to where the Douro River flows into the Atlantic Ocean, at an estate  Quinta  where D. Pedro I, King of Portugal, lived in the 14th century. The production fields  all of which may be visited for free or in guided tours  have the ideal climate, because the estate is located in the transition zone between the Mediterranean and Atlantic climates. Production is entirely certified as Organically Produced.

A shop selling organic food and handicrafts, books and all the products sold by “Cantinho das Aromáticas”. Now also in the Online shop.

Ecolã is located in Porto’s historic quarter and sells products which are made of wool from the Serra da Estrela region and are 100% Portuguese. Our company specialises in the production of items made of ‘burel’ wool, which are made in a way which preserves traditional culture, using ancestral techniques which are very specific to Portugal. We are the only company which takes wool right from the shearing stage through to the final product. We are a family-run  third generation  environmentally certified Craft Production Workshop  UPA  founded in 1925 and managed by João Clara Assunção. 
Ecolã produces a variety of products ranging from blankets to personalised decorative objects, articles of clothing, garments and fashion accessories.
Ecolã provides a diverse range of products featuring unique and original design complemented by high-quality service, filling in the gaps evident in the national, global and mass market. In this way it is responding to the needs of customers seeking items of high quality which stand out from the crowd.

Portolovers is the label behind this line of merchandising which exploits some of the symbols from Portugal’s past and presents them in a modern style redolent of the city of Porto itself. The range of Portolovers products was designed to impress. Like falling in love. First impressions count, but after that everything else is important: image, quality and the dedication we put into our work.
The result is a portfolio of highly diverse items, ranging from pens to A6 notepads, pencils, posters, magnets, aprons, bags, t-shirts and much else, in which the dominant theme is love for the city, history and modernity.

YOURS is located in the historic centre of PORTO, classified by UNESCO as world heritage patrimony, in a building completely rebuilt in 2014. The house contains an interior garden and a charming outdoor terrace, from where you can see a relaxing view over the Cathedral and the city centre. This scenario sets an ideal place for lunch/dinner with family and friends, or to eat some snacks/nibbles, or to drink a glass of wine by the end of the afternoon.  
The GUESTHOUSE is based in the calm and tranquil Rua dos Caldeireiros, a typical street of the historic centre, and close to most of the well known moments, 100 m from the Clérigos Tower, Aliados Avenue and the Cathedral. YOURS is served by an outstanding transportation service that includes the underground  150 m away  with connection to the airport, buses and the train station S.Bento that will take you anywhere in the country.

local: A Loja dos Pastéis de Chaves
descricao:
“A Loja dos Pastéis de Chaves” specialises in a traditional Portuguese product that has delighted young and old alike since 1862: the Pastel de Chaves. 
You’ll find pasties to suit every taste with fillings ranging from the savoury – chicken, salted cod or vegetarian – to the sweet – egg crème and almond or chocolate.
Its inventive salads, bagels and crunchy toasted sandwiches, made using traditional bread, are ideal for a simply delicious meal in the shop’s modern and comfortable atmosphere. 
Relax with the ambient music while examining the menu. Enjoy the service at the Coffee Shop where, with its tempting hot chocolate, coffees, fresh juices and lemonades in various flavours, you can try a different beverage every day!

Cantinho do Avillez, by the well-known and award-winning chef José Avillez, is a friendly, cosy and relaxed space. The cuisine is Portuguese inspired, influenced by travels. The Menu offers some of the top dishes of Cantinho do Avillez in Lisbon and others specially created by chef José Avillez for his bistro in Porto.

It is worth mentioning: marinated Scallops  Flaked Cod with bread crumbs, LT egg and “exploding” olives  Roasted octopus  Veal Risotto, Hazelnut  Toucinho-do-céu with raspberry sorbet.

A must-go-to and highly sought-after space. It is advisable to book in advance.

Lovingly restored historic XVIII century apartment with a unique garden and magical views over Douro river. It is located at Porto UNESCO World Heritage Site, a perfect spot precisely at heart of the city Perfect spot close to main city landmarks.
Explore the city centre either on foot or by public transport available close by. The public transport is available close by. It is only 5 minutes walk to Clerigos tower as well as to Ribeira World Heritage Site and Douro River Bank. It is a 15 minutes walk to light rail Bolhao market station or Trindade. The local bus stops at Cordoaria square. Less than 20-30 minutes away from the airport by car or taxi.

The S. Miguel apartments are located in a 100-year old building in Porto’s historic neighbourhood. The building does not have a lift. It lies in a quiet residential street in the old Jewish quarter of Olival. It is just 300 metres from the Clérigos Tower and 5 minutes from Palácio da Bolsa  The Stock Exchange .
The apartments are spacious  90 m2  and each has 3 bedrooms, a bathroom and a fully equipped kitchen, with a microwave, coffee-maker, toaster and washing machine. We provide towels and sheets. There is free internet. 
The apartments do not have TVs.
Each apartment is furnished with one double bed and 3 single beds. We can bring in an extra bed if necessary. Children are welcome and we can provide toys, a cot and a high chair, if required.
The street parking is free.
The first floor apartment, which has a picturesque patio at the back, is exceptionally comfortable and quiet. The third floor apartment is light and airy and offers rooftop views of the historic centre. From the top floor, the river views are literally breathtaking.
Pick the apartment you would like and we will do everything in our power to make you feel at home
This modern holiday apartment, housed in a fully renovated building, nestles at the very heart of Porto’s historic district and offers excellent views of the city and the Douro River.

The apartment is in the historic centre of Porto, classified World Heritage by UNESCO. Urban apartment - Casa da Portela is registered at the Tourism Board of Portugal  no. 5105/AL . It has a balcony overlooking the typical orange roofs of Porto and Clérigos Tower.
A minute walk from São Bento train station and subway station, near Avenida dos Aliados. Just a few minutes from Ribeira and beside the Porto Cathedral. The airport at about 10Km.

The Douro River Apartments, each one with its own style, are housed in a building that has won awards for the way it has been renovated. 
These self-sufficient lodgings offer views of the Douro River and are just a few metres from the World of Discoveries Museum. 
Both apartments have a living-room and a fully equipped kitchen. The building itself is amazingly light and airy.
The distinctive architecture of the building, the amenities in the apartments and the riverside setting all make this the perfect alternative for your stay in Porto.

A set of apartments in a centrally located residential building.
Each apartment has its own style and, although not a hotel, they are all equipped with the amenities you might need to ensure you have a pleasant stay in the magnificent city of Porto. The D. Luís Bridge, the Cathedral and São Bento train station are all close by and the S. João Theatre is right next door.

Each bedroom has its own colour and the living-room has a flat screen TV, and a sofa with a chaise-longue. There are two double bedrooms, a full bathroom with tub and an additional WC.
The modern kitchen is boldly decorated and all the utensils you might need to prepare your meals are provided. The iconic Bolhão Market, where guests can buy fresh ingredients for their meals, is less than a 15-minute walk away. There is also a plentiful and varied selection of restaurants in the immediate neighbourhood.

Home Made Guest Studios are 5 modern studio flats with a welcoming feel, housed in a building bursting with history and character. Come and visit us and let yourself be charmed by the warmth and authenticity of the neighbourhood and our staff. Linger a while, relax on our patio... and stay for one more day!

The Porto Solar Studios are in Rua de Santa Catarina, Porto’s most famous shopping street. The studio flats have free Wi-Fi and kitchenette facilities. The apartments are simply decorated and there is a lift. They are equipped with cable TV and are ideal for an independent stay because they have everything you might need to prepare your own meals. 
There is a supermarket 300 metres away and the Bolhão Market, with its fresh products and groceries, is not far at all.
The Café Majestic, in its inimitable Belle Époque style, is just 550 metres away. The café sports an inviting outdoor seating area where you can sit and enjoy the sun while sipping your drink. 
The Clérigos area night-life is about a 15-minute walk from Porto Solar Studios and it only takes about 20 minutes to get to the riverside cafés and eateries of the Ribeira. 
A 25-minute walk might take you across the Douro River to the port wine cellars on the other bank.
From the Trindade metro station, which is 500 metres away, you can connect to Francisco Sá Carneiro International Airport, a journey of around 30 minutes.

This unit is situated at the mouth  Foz  of the Douro, 15 minutes by car from the centre of Porto. These apartments have one bedroom, veranda or balcony with full views of the sea, a private bathroom, a dining area, kitchen, cable television and free Wi-Fi access. Guests can walk along the sea boardwalk or ride their bicycles. The historic centre of Porto is 15 minutes by car and the tourism area of Ribeira, facing the Douro River, is 12 minutes away. The Porto International Airport is 17 minutes by car from the Bartolomeu Foz Apartments sea view.

These recently refurbished apartments occupy a privileged riverside plot in Porto’s Ribeira quarter. The Ribeira Cinema Apartments have been decorated in a cinematic style and offer a range of lodgings from studio flats to two-bedroom apartments. All have a fully equipped kitchen or kitchenette, a TV and internet access.
In the summer, guests can enjoy the huge choice of outdoor restaurants along Cais de Ribeira, just 2 minutes away, or cross over to the other side of the Douro River to visit the port wine cellars.

The Rivoli Cinema Hostel was Portugal’s first hostel with a cinema theme.
The decoration of each of its 14 rooms was inspired by a different film. When you check in, you will choose the type of room you want  individual, double, triple or quadruple  and possibly your favourite film as well.
There are 6 bathrooms with 8 shared showers  a fully equipped kitchen, where breakfast is served and where you are free to prepare your meals  a living-room, where you can relax, watch
films, read, play, make free use of the computers with internet access or socialise with other guests. There is also a terrace, which is perfect for sunbathing, parties. BBQ and getting together. Wi-Fi, maps, tourist information and guides are all provided free of charge.
You can find us at Rua Doutor Magalhães Lemos, no. 83, in Porto’s historic centre, right next to Avenida dos Aliados, from where you can take in the architecture, stroll around and visit the city’s monuments and museums. There are restaurants, cafés, supermarkets, shops and shopping centres nearby, and tourist offices, too.
The metro, train, bus, tram and taxi are all just around the corner, which makes it a great base for visiting all the main monuments and museums in the city. If you want some night-time fun, there are discos, bars and cafés galore a short 3-minute walk away. If you like musicals, just cross the street and go into the Rivoli Theatre, home to the world’s most fantastical film festival - Fantasporto, which is on every February.
Deluxe 4 bedroom apartment with 3 bathrooms located in the city center, next to the bolhão metro station.

Installed in a typical Porto Bourgeois Home, originally built around 1850 and completely refurbished in 2015, Baumhaus has 9 fully independent apartments, furnished and equipped, each with its own unique character. Each apartment has a bedroom, kitchen and bathroom. Guests have at their disposal: towels, sheets, duvets, shower gel, shampoo, soap, and water, tea and coffee in the kitchen. Cleaning service included. Check-in from 3pm to 7:30pm  confirmation required after 7:30pm  and check-out until 12:00 midday.
Arrival time must always be communicated.

Casa do Gólgota, strategically located on the oceanfront and river area of the historic centre, located right in the Caminhos do Romântico, is a wonderful adaptation of an old family home, converted into modern and comfortable apartments. The old building was divided into three apartments and four studios, equipped with kitchenette, television, Wi-Fi and climate control, to give you all the comforts of home, even during longer stays. Casa do Gólgota also offers you the option of using a common garden with one of the best panoramic views over the Douro river and its mouth.
Two bedroom apartment in the heart of the histórical center of Porto with balconies facing the main cathedral.
Deluxe 1 bedroom apartment with balcony and view to the cathedral, located in the historical city center.

The M2Students Hostel is located in the centre of Porto, a 15-minute walk from the iconic Avenida dos Aliados.
The Bolhão Market and the Bolhão Metro Station are 500 metres away, while the shops of the popular Rua de Santa Catarina are a 5-minute walk away.
The unit has dormitories as well as private rooms with 1 single bed or 1 double bed.
There is free WiFi access in all areas of the hostel. 
The private bedrooms include a private bathroom, while the dormitories provide access to shared bathrooms.
Other amenities provided by the property include a place to store luggage.
Guests have access to a common kitchen, where they are able to prepare their own meals. 
As an alternative, there are various traditional restaurants, pastry shops and historic cafés, within a 10-minute walk.
The Trindade Metro Station is 500 metres away and makes it possible to reach the majority of areas in Porto and its neighboring cities.
The iconic Torre dos Clérigos and the busy Galerias de Paris are within a 20-minute walk. 
The Porto International Airport is 17km from the M2Students Hostel.
According to independent reviews, this is the area of Porto that guests prefer.

At the SPA & TEA all of your problems and stress will cease to exist, thanks to the combination of treatments and highly effective products, prepared to fulfill your needs. Put yourself in the hands of our experienced therapists and you’ll feel a deep relaxation.

The spa has been designed to provide you unique moments. Enjoy a true experience of  Salute per aqua  in the dynamic pool where you can enjoy the various jets. You can also enjoy the saunas’ therapeutic properties and the Turkish bath.

Facing Porto Bolhão Market, the pet-friendly Portas do Bolhão offers apartments is a 19th-century building classified as a Public Interest Property. Each unit features a pass-through window from the kitchen to the living room, which has a flat-screen cable TV and a sofa. Guests are welcome to use the kitchen to prepare meals made of fresh products bought in Bolhao Market, located across the street. Galerias Paris, Lello Bookshop and Clerigos Tower are within a 10-minute walk from Portas do Bolhão. Bolhão Metro Station is within 100 metres from the apartments.

Cosy and lovely apartment just 100 meter to the beach, with 2 bedrooms,1 living room, 1 dinning room, 1 fully equipped kitchen, 1 bathroom and a great balcony, where you can have your nice breakfast. All the areas have direct sunlight. Quiet area! Perfect for couples, a family or a group of friends!
And if you are a wave lovers or only want to experience the surf, you are in the perfect place. We have a surfschool with surf lessons and surf trips.
Can you picture surfing in the morning, taking a cultural trip in the afternoon and discovering the wonders of the gastronomy in the evening.

Bringing happiness since 1992. Located in the downtown part of the city  Baixa da Cidade , Ruial is a traditional eatery that stands out for the quality of its speciality – the famous Francesinha. Its traditional recipe, with a magnificent sauce that is widely acclaimed, and homemade, freshly fried chips peeled by hand make it one of the best! It is also known for its Hot Dogs  Cachorrinhos  and for typical Portuguese dishes that are renowned for their traditional and homemade flavours. Ruial is a great choice for any occasion.

Charming and welcoming apartment, with central heating, in a building with 3 lifts and 3 areas of free parking on the street.
Located in a residential area of Porto, a 5-minute walk from the Metro station. The Metro takes 15 minutes to reach the Porto Historic Centre and 20 minutes to the Airport. The VCI road exit  Boavista  is 5 minutes away.
The residential area has a pharmacy, supermarket, takeaway, coffee/pastry shop, 24/7 ATM, hair salon, gym, park and public gardens. Everything is about 5-10 minutes away on foot.
This can be an excellent place to stay while exploring Porto and the North/Centre of Portugal.

UportoHouse is modern housing intended for students, professors, researchers, technicians, as well as tourists, located in the University Area of Porto. A residence from the sixties, completely renovated and updated to guests’ current needs, it offers various spaces and services: dining room, sitting room, study, garden with patio, kitchen, internet, cleaning and heating, among others. All areas are furnished and fully equipped for an enjoyable and comfortable stay. UportoHouse has 9 bedrooms: 6 single bedrooms and one double bedroom, sharing 4 full bathrooms and 2 suites with full access to the garden. A 5-minute walk from most of the Colleges, Universities and Hospitals  IPO, São João , it has a bus stop at the door with service to the city centre  10-minute trip . There is a number of supermarkets, grocers, restaurants, bakeries and other shops in the area.

WE LOVE BIKES AND WE WANT YOU TO LOVE OUR BIKES!

Rental bikes without the unnecessary bureaucracies. We provide maps for your self guided tours and with them a super friendly team to help you in whatever you need to explore the city the way you like or with your family and friends. Biclas & Triclas provides you with the equipment and experience to have a safe and memorable trip.

We also offer you guided tours by bike and pedicab in Porto.

Our interpreter-guides are experienced local cyclists who like to share their love for the city and its unique historical narrative. They are happy to answer all your questions about restaurants, museums, traditions and events.

THE BEST WAY TO GET TO KNOW THE CITY OF PORTO.

The FC Porto Store is closed, in order to prevent the Covid-19 outbreak.

Located in one of the oldest streets in Porto inside the Vitalis Park sports complex, the FC Porto Store shares its premises with the members’ shop  Loja do Associado  and club school  Escola Dragon Force Porto . This space, where over 600 children and adolescents are taught the sporting methodology of the great Futebol Clube do Porto from a very young age, sells all of the main products in the FC Porto and New Balance ranges, with the main focus being placed on youngsters and their families.

AlegriaRide Porto is a service specializing in the rental of scooters with experience in sightseeing tours and local suggestions.
Our dedicated objective is to make sure you enjoy your time with our scooters in the best way possible.
High quality sports scooters that are safe and maintained to the highest standards.

A refurbished apartment, ideal for a family or group of friends  6 persons . The apartment has two bedrooms, a fully equipped kitchen, a bathroom and a room with a veranda. In the living room there is a sofa bed for two persons.
The apartment is 5 minutes from the Lapa Church and less than 15 minutes from the Porto City Hall and the Trindade station.
Ideal for visiting downtown since no transportation is needed. Also, its proximity to the Metro system makes it possible to easily visit other locations in the city, from Boavista to the FC Porto football stadium.

Piscina de Campanhã is the only Olympic-sized swimming pool  50 meters long  in Porto and one of the few indoor pools in the country. The facility that is loaned by Porto City Hall to FC Porto for a period of 25 years, is not only used by the club s swimming section, but also the boxing and adapted sports sections. The opinion among athletes and coaches of the respective sports is unanimous: the infrastructure will bring better results and attract more young people into sport. The new facility features several changing rooms, weight training room and nurse’s office, offices, and state-of-the-art equipment.

The facility is open to the entire population, through its Swimming School and public swim timetables, and is available to non-members.

Dear Porto Guest House occupies a prime location on Porto’s most popular shopping and pedestrian street. The guest house is just 300 metres from Bolhão metro station and its direct links to the airport and S. Bento and Campanhã train stations.
All rooms are cleaned daily and come with a private bathroom and a range of free personal hygiene products. All rooms also have heating, a hair-dryer and an LCD TV with cable service. Wi-Fi internet is available throughout the guest house. We can also provide ironing and fax services. Guests will also enjoy relaxing on our outdoor patio.

local: Porto.arte Guest Apartments – Casa da Frente B
descricao:
TRADITION . COMFORT . SERENITY br  br 

A one-bedroom apartment of 45m2, is located in the pedestrian area of Rua Miguel Bombarda.
It is cozy and practical, distinguished for being quiet and having a remarkable natural light in every room. br 
It also has a sofa bed. br 
It is fully equipped with air conditioning, a large HD TV, cable, Internet/Wifi, all the necessary kitchen equipment and accessories, safe, hair dryer, utility room, iron. br 
Also of note, the good mattresses and the high quality bed/bath fabrics.

107 rooms fully equipped with:
-Exclusive and ultra-comfortable mattresses 
-3 varieties of pillows 
-High speed and bandwidth Internet Wi-Fi 
- 48” Led TV 
- Free coffee & tea facilities  “Nespresso” coffee machine, kettle and 1 water bottle  
-Writing desk 
-Exclusive “NH Collection” bathroom amenities 
-Shower with “rain shower” and “jet-power” effect 
-Professional hair dryer.
Free Wi-Fi throughout the Hotel.
Permanent Assistance of the Exclusive Service “Guest Relations”.
Restaurant & Bar Gastrô.
4 meeting rooms with capacity for up to 90 people.
SPA with swimming pool, sauna, Turkish bath, treatment rooms and gym.

Located in a quiet neighborhood, near the Douro river, this house is the ideal place to rest or to enjoy the walks along the river and the sea
Transportation to the city center are very close.
Parking nearby.

local: Porto.arte Guest Apartments - Casa da Frente A
descricao:
TRADITION . COMFORT . SERENITY br  br 

A one-bedroom apartment of 45m2, is located in the pedestrian area of Rua Miguel Bombarda.
It’s cozy, distinguished by being separated, quiet and having direct light in the rooms. It has a private toilet in the bedroom apart from the bathroom. Access to the garden. br 
It is fully equipped with air conditioning, a large HD TV, cable, Internet/Wifi, all the necessary kitchen equipment and accessories, safe, hair dryer, utility room, iron. br 
Also of note, the good mattresses and the high quality bed/bath fabrics

In the historic centre of Porto, in the heart of the UNESCO World Heritage, you will find Galleries Paris Porto Downtown Apartments, housed in a historical building, completely rehabilitated. br 
The fusion between the contemporary and the old, provides unique spaces for you to enjoy, where comfort, good taste and well-being are present for an unforgettable holiday with family and friends. Three spacious apartments, with all the amenities, allow you to select according to the experience you want to have in Porto: Studio with 42 m2 or one of two fantastic one-bedroom duplex apartments with 70 and 73 m2 - all have a private balcony. The emblematic sites of the city can be visited on foot, so that you can admire the Clérigos Tower, Avenida dos Aliados, Sé Catedral, Ribeira, Port Wine Cellars and much more... br 
You will find cafes, bars and restaurants within 10 meters, allowing you to enjoy the lively and fun nightlife of Porto, as well as Theatres and Cinemas are nearby. Metro, train and bus less than 250 meters - to get to know the city easily. br 
A perfect stay is waiting for you!

Bacalhau, Portuguese Wine&Food is a shop and a restaurant of 100% Portuguese products of high quality. We want to represent all the regions of the country, and for this we count on the help of wine, cheese, honey, olive oil and many other surprising and fascinating products produced in Portugal!

A space that surrounds us in the most typical area of the city with a breathtaking view over the Douro River.

Besides the room you can also find in our shop a range of wines and other typical Portuguese products in which you can buy or order and receive it in the comfort of your home.

local: Porto.arte Guest Apartments - Casa da Frente C
descricao:
TRADITION . COMFORT . SERENITY br  br 

A high-end apartment with 70m2 and 2 bedrooms  1 en suite , is located in the pedestrian area of Rua Miguel Bombarda.
It is cozy and practical, distinguished for being quiet and having a remarkable natural light in every room. br 
It also has a sofa bed. br 
It is fully equipped with air conditioning, a large HD TV, cable, Internet/Wifi, all the necessary kitchen equipment and accessories, safe, hair dryer, utility room, iron. br 
Also of note, the good mattresses and the high quality bed/bath fabrics.

PanoPraMantas is the first shop/workroom in Porto, totally dedicated to fabrics and accessories for patchwork, quilting and creative sewing.
Fabrics from the best brands lined up on shelves are a real delight to the eyes.
A modern and attractive shop where you can buy unique and totally handcrafted gifts.

he Chapel of  Nossa Senhora do Postigo da Verdade   Chapel of Nossa Senhora das Verdades  was being built in 1697 at the expense of the brotherhood of Nossa Senhora do Postigo das Verdades next to the old chapel dedicated to the same Saint, located at the bottom of the Rua dos Cónegos  Rua de D. Hugo . In April of that year and with the purpose of financing the completion of the works of the new chapel, the brotherhood signed the contract with the canon Domingos Gonçalves Prada, who resided in a house contiguous to the chapel, which, in exchange for the necessary funding to finish the works, he could open a direct access to the chapel on the east side. This same document explicitly states that the chapel is owned by the City Hall, as it always was. In August 1701, the canon Domingos Gonçalves Prada sponsored the roof truss, window glasses, flagging, retable and three altar frontals. br 
The chapel is architecturally built in a Mannerist style - a rectangular plan, double-gable roof, frontispiece delimited by Tuscan pilasters and topped by a triangular pediment. br 
Inside the chapel, the retable has two overlapping designs, following a reticulated design in a Mannerist style, interspersing four paintings representing São Domingos, São José, São João Baptista and Nossa Senhora da Rosa and an image of Nossa Senhora das Verdades in a niche located at the centre of the retable. However, this statuette is of the utmost importance for the history of art of woodcarving’s evolution in the city of Porto, since it is the only statuette that has survived to the present day, showing a change of taste and style. In fact, if the retable is structurally still in a Mannerist style, the decoration clearly shows the national style, which is evident throughout the retable, in particular in the spiral columns and with the spires that has eucharistic symbols - grapevine leaves, bunches of grapes, phoenixes and naked little boys. br 
The statuette of Nossa Senhora das Verdades, made of stone from Ançã, represents the Virgin Mary holding in her arms the Infant Jesus and is probably from the fourteenth century, made in the workshops of Coimbra. It was originally in one of the arches of the old city wall - the Postigo das Mentiras.

local: Galeria da Biodiversidade - Centro Ciência Viva
descricao:
Located in the heart of the historic Porto Botanical Garden, the Hall of Biodiversity - Centro Ciência Viva is one of the poles of the Museum of Natural History and Science of the University of Porto. As a result of the creative work of a multidisciplinary team, this museological space where art, science and literature meet, invites the visitor to take part on a journey along which are told exciting stories about life, its diversity and the evolutionary processes that shape it. Through pieces integrated in innovative artistic creations, interactive modules, installations and sculptures, is built at the Biodiversity Gallery a universal narrative that is both beautiful, stimulating and accessible.

The Hall of Biodiversity - CCV is inseparable from Porto Botanical Garden, which surrounds it, which makes it a unique set of unrepeatable individuality. Centred on a romantic nucleus, the garden has expanded to include illustrative species of natural diversity, support of scientific knowledge. Also, the elegant nineteenth century house that houses the Biodiversity Gallery - CCV has gained a new meaning through a museology discourse that covers the living world as seen by science.

On the southwest corner of the square was located the Porta do Cimo de Vila on the Fernando Wall. In the eighteenth century, the area underwent major transformations, and the wall was demolished. br 
On the eastern side of the square we can see an emblazoned palace built at the end of the 18th century commissioned by José Anastácio da Silva da Fonseca, knight of Royal House. At the time of the Siege of Porto, the owners, who were Miguelists, abandoned the palace, which made the liberal government to establish there, using it for various public institutions. In 1842, it was returned to its former owners. It was the Post, Telegraph and Telephone Main Office throughout much of the 20th century. br 
The square is, since 1866, dominated by the monument to D. Pedro V. In the square are also located the São João National Theatre and Cine-Teatro Batalha.

The Cultura Portuguesa shop is the place to dress sustainably and enjoy the inspiration of good Portuguese artisans.
High quality cork is queen. Bags, shoes, and a wide variety of accessories come to life with a natural, ecological, animal-free material that gives it an ethical, distinctive style designed for daily use.

Originally opened in 1972, the Bessa Stadium was rebuilt at the beginning of the XXI century and has a capacity of 30,000 seats.

Subenshi is a restaurant specializing in Japanese cuisine, being a Portuguese leading restaurant for the quality of its sushi, full of amazing flavours and amazing textures. It is established in a small palace from the 19th-century, fully restored, the Parada Leitão Building. At the same time, 4 beautiful hand-painted tile panels were elaborated exclusively, according to the traditional Portuguese technique, portraying Japanese themes and episodes by the painter Francisco Cunha, which give the space a very special ambience. Our motto:  a gastronomic experience in a historical heritage , in a harmonious fusion between Portuguese and Japanese cultures and identities.

The guided tours of the Porto Jewish Museum/Synagogue will help visitors understand the architectural and symbolic importance of this, the largest synagogue in the Iberian Peninsula. You will also learn something about the religious, cultural and historical sides of Judaism.

The museum houses a varied collection of objects and documents relating to Judaism as a religious practice and to the history of the Jews of Porto, from medieval times through to today. There is a special focus on the life and work of Captain Barros Basto, the founder of the community here in the city.

The Museum is only open for training sessions and school visits. For other visits, prior registration is required and subject to availability  Monday to Friday from 10 am to 12 pm .

Founded in 1912, the façade of this jewellery shop was restored about 25 years ago to look as it was in the 1940s. This establishment is currently recognized as one of the official appraisers licensed by the National Press of the Mint and specialises in selling gold, silver and antique jewellery.

The establishment has free parking for customers.

Official Appraisers Licensed by the Portuguese Mint and Official Printing Office  Imprensa Nacional da Casa da Moeda .

This watchmaker’s is one of the most iconic of Porto. It opened its doors in 1926 in the heart of the city. Although it has had several different owners and refurbishments and over its 90 years of history, this shop continues to preserve its founder s name. The imposing Art Nouveau façade building showcases collections of luxury watch brands and unique pieces of international jewellery in a sophisticated setting sought-after by collectors.
Italian restaurant with authentic products imported from Italy.

Shop founded in 1926, it moved in 1958 to its current location, on the first floor of an imposing building, without shop windows to Rua de Passos Manuel. Being a tough specialist in selling wool products, this activity gives it some exclusivity.

The firm that gave birth to Bazar Mabril was founded in 1941.
Its activity is closely associated with selling toys, crockery, fashion and design for children, a business in which it specialised and became a reference in the city.

With over 100 years of history and a well-established brand in the market, this establishment combines in its pieces the best of both worlds: the traditional and the modern, highlighting the marble and granite that have all kinds of applications.

Founded in 1860, this house shortly after moved to its current location. It sells exclusively Portuguese handicraft products and has on display several different embroideries. It holds workshops for those who like to learn this noble craft.

Founded on 2 January 1902 by photographer Domingos Alvão, features a rare collection that delights visitors: thousands of photographs by this renowned photographer, a key figure of the city of Porto, depicting decades of history of the city and its people.

Family owned, began its activity on 13 March 1962. Being more than half a century old, it has adapted to market developments and gained public recognition. The mother house, located in Porto’s city centre, continues to have a major fame.

Founded in 1858 by a Fine Arts student, it was the first frame workshop in Porto.

Several artists designed frame models. Marques de Oliveira, António Carneiro, Fernando Lanhas are some of them.

Among the institutions that have frames made in this house, it includes Câmara Municipal do Porto, Biblioteca Municipal do Porto, Palácio Nacional da Pena, Palácio da Bolsa, Museu Nacional de Soares dos Reis, among others.

The technique of making the frame evolved alongside the restoration of paintings, frames and gilded woodcarving.

It is currently the oldest frame workshop in the world.

This commercial establishment represents the preservation of a traditional business without the loss of identity.

Porto for Weddings is a company that provides services of wedding planning based in Porto, Portugal. Marrying in Porto is an unforgettable experience - with the choice of a Church Wedding or a civil marriage ceremony.   The Douro river gives a special beauty to the city and there are many excellent restaurants, both in the city and in the surrounding countryside, that offer delicious food based on traditional local produce, and of course good wines.  After all, this is the city that gave its name to one of the world´s most famous drinks – Port wine.

In this tailoring shop founded in 1918, the know-how passed down over several generations, preserving the rigor of personalised service and customisation as its identity aspects. All pieces are made in its facilities, with the detail that has been marking this historic house.

This firm has a history of over 150 years. It was in the mid-nineteenth century, in 1866, that began its activity, having later moved to the current address. Dedicated to the manufacture of hats and other uniform accessories for security and public order institutions. It has gained a high industrial prestige and public recognition by personalities and institutions.

This restaurant opened in 1962 and is featured in various gastronomic guides. Its main attraction is the famous hot dog, which take domestic and foreign customers to queue at the door to taste this delicacy. This beer hall has always been renowned but reached an even bigger visibility when it was featured in one of Chef Anthony Bourdain s tv shows.

It is a traditional wholesale and bulk grocery shop. Opened in 1945, this house is renowned for its teas, coffees, fine grocery and intoxicating aromas. Entering this space is a true time travel because it has preserved its traditional trade and genuine identity.

It was founded in 1947. Throughout its history it has been a shop where are sold quality products to repair shoes and leather goods. This business supplies many shoe repairers in the city and works with shoe factories.

The management of this shop founded in 1928 is already in its third generation. It is a local business with a sewing machine repair and fine-tuning workshop. It has become a distributor of major brands of sewing and embroidery machines. In 2016, it underwent remodelling works, taking care to preserve much of the identity of the initial project.

Founded in 1908, this establishment has specialised in the trade, handling and specialised services of all types of glass, mirrors, frames, acrylics, ironmongery and emergency services. With over 100 years of experience, it provides varied services to the commercial, industrial and residential markets, for architectural and decoration purposes.

Founded in 1902 as a goldsmith s workshop, it moved in 1966 to Rua Santos Pousada - current location. Over the years, it has held several exhibitions always with the concern to preserve the art and history of goldsmiths, constantly adapting to modernity.

It is a factory / shop for the making of handmade candles and candles made with the help of machines. Its records show that it has been 124 years in business.

The passing on the know-how has always been done in this establishment, so the artisanal method of candle making has been ensured. The shop has a unique and identity-filled atmosphere thanks to the hanging candles that line the ceiling, a feature that has been attracting many tourists.

It is a jewellery shop with more than a century and a half of history. This traditional and family business has its main identity feature in the representative pieces of Portuguese jewellery and in the filigree pieces, which it has been selling for decades. This house’s age proves that it is a historical and cultural reference of the city.

This jewellery shop was inaugurated in 1857, becoming a luxurious and welcoming space, which gives a lot of refinement and good taste to the pieces. This house participates in large Portuguese fairs and holds jewellery pieces of important heritage, lending them to several art exhibitions.

Founded over 80 years ago, it is a leading house and is under the management of the third generation of the founding family. Specialising in selling vegetables seeds, tree fruits, seeds, gardening equipment and tools is much sought after by the vast experience in this business.

Founded in 1851, this establishment is one of Porto s oldest jewellery shop. Still in the nineteenth century, there is its modernization with the entry of partner José Rosas, who institutes personalised customer service by the first woman jeweller. Its history includes the restoration of the Portuguese Crown Jewels and the application of filigree in crystal and ceramic pieces. With over 150 years of accumulated experience, the shop is already in its fifth generation.


Visits on Saturdays by booking.

The first business records of this house specialising in the trade of buttons and haberdashery date from 1936. However, some people associate it with the birth date of filmmaker Manoel de Oliveira in 1908. This shop is filled with hundreds of boxes with numerous buttons. Due to the diversity and richness of its offer, it is here that many fashion designers come to buy their supplies.

The history of this bakery began to be written in 1878. Its founders imprinted the establishment with the tradition and quality of its products - identity characteristics that their successors have been able to preserve and keep alive to this day. There is no one from Porto who has never heard of this house and its famous products.

There is uncertainty about the start of activity of this Armazéns  department store , but it is likely that it began its activity between the late 19th and early 20th centuries. The store is easily recognized for the proud peacock s ostentation on the building s Art-Deco façade - a staple of this historic commercial establishment.

Luiz Ferreira  1909-1994  had one of the strongest artistic personalities in the gold/silversmithing field, a tradition that was already rooted in his grandfather and his father. He opened his jewellery shop in 1970 and began his career as a creator and businessman. In this shop one can find the spirit and art of its creator, expressed in its silver pieces, jewellery or famous decorative animals.

Company founded in 1934, sells Portuguese raw material and wool. In recent years, it has been providing consulting services of Knitting, Crochet and Embroidery. This shop is an integral part of the city s history and is visited by many customers from the third or fourth generation of families loyal to this commercial house.

This house in the barber business started its activity in 1930, with an Art Deco decoration that adds richness to its interior. To preserve its history, the current owner tries to maintain a traditional activity and establishment with unique and very striking features in the city.

It began its activity in 1922 and is a stationer’s with a striking historical identity and an exclusive environment where the artistic and academic tradition is recognised. It has been a loyal ally of painters and students of Fine Arts and Architecture, but it also attracts an unskilled audience looking for those unique items sold in this establishment.

Café founded in 1964 and frequented by the population of Foz do Douro is part of the memory of different generations. It is mentioned in numerous publications as a historical café of the city of Porto, but also for its excellent confection and its family atmosphere.

This commercial establishment, which has been in existence for over a century, has the mission of preserving and disseminating genuine Portuguese tradition, culture and handcrafted products. According to records, this business started business in 1897, but may have started even before that. Since then, from generation to generation, this house continues to sell regional, traditional and folk Portuguese products.

The Oficina de Encadernação António Sousa Oliveira is one of the few that withstands the passage of time, bridging the gap between ancient and modern. Founded in 1969, it moved in 1974 to its current location. The art of binding has been passed down from generation to generation in a family business. It specialises in simple and luxurious binding, restoration and gold-leaf applications.

With over half a century of history, this iconic barbershop in the city centre is a space cherished by generations of customers. The large mirrors reflect the padded armchairs and the barber’s craft. It has had several illustrious clients over the years, including figures from FC Porto s history, such as former coach José Maria Pedroto, who was for decades a client.

local: Casa Portuguesa do Pastel de Bacalhau
descricao: Under the motto “It is the voice of the earth yearning for the sea”, from the poem D. Dinis by Fernando Pessoa, and in a great tribute to the shepherds of Serra da Estrela, the Pastel de Bacalhau com Queijo da Serra® was born, as a way of bringing the coastline closer to the inland, celebrating two icons of Portuguese gastronomy: codfish fritter and Serra da Estrela DOP cheese. To this duo, which combines two traditional flavours in a unique speciality, Port wine was added, in a trio that promises a true «Portugal Experience».

FEUPmuseu is a university museum unit, created at the Faculdade de Engenharia da Universidade do Porto, whose mission is to appreciate the historical, scientific, cultural heritage of this school and its predecessor institutions, to awaken new and diverse perspectives on Engineering, and to encourage public understanding on technology. This mission is achieved through the preservation, research, interpretation and communication around the collections of recognized patrimonial, scientific and technological value, as well as their respective documentation.

FEUPmuseu also aims to provide technical support and collaboration in safeguarding, studying and disseminating the knowledge products currently generated at FEUP, with a view to constitute the intangible and intangible heritage for the future.

The museum, with its polynucleated configuration, is in the process of being installed.


Visits by prior booking.

This Museum, which operates in the premises of Igreja do Corpo Santo de Massarelos, has on display seventy pieces of sacred art, paintings, jewellery, vestments and manuscripts, dating from the sixteenth to the twentieth centuries.

These pieces include the Queen D. Maria I’s Royal Mantle Veil, given by the Monarch to the Confraria do Corpo Santo de Massarelos in 1790, and which after 8 months of conservation and restoration works in a laboratory, can now be contemplated.

In addition to the Queen D. Maria I s Royal Mantle Veil, there is also a silver monstrance  16th century , the book “A Arte de Navegar”  from 1746 , the triptych canvases  17th century  and the nativity scene by the school “Machado de Castro”  18th century .


April to September - Monday to Saturday: 10am-1pm / 2pm-6pm
October to March - Monday to Saturday: 2pm-6pm

local: Gabinete de História e Memórias da ESEP
descricao:
The Gabinete de História e Memórias da Escola Superior de Enfermagem do Porto aims to revive and disseminate the past of ESEP, including it in the history of nursing, through the appreciation of the museum collections as well as planning the creation of the nursing museum.

In 2019, the Gabinete de História e das Memórias was awarded the Prémio Valoriza 2019, whose main purpose is to distinguish outstanding nurses in various fields, as well as to praise, encourage and give the proper recognition to the meritorious work in the social and clinical fields.


Visits by prior booking.

Cervejaria Brasão was born to share a timeless experience, where the present is combined with the good know-how of other times. A comfortable and simple place, where one can enjoy a wide and personalised offer of craft beers, francesinhas, snacks and steaks. All of this, in a Portuguese Beer Hall for sure!

local: Museu de História da Medicina Maximiano Lemos
descricao:
Dates from October 1933, at the initiative of Professor Luís de Pina, the foundation of Museu de História da Medicina Maximiano Lemos da Faculdade de Medicina da Universidade do Porto. Each room of the Museum was named after a professor from Porto’s Medical School who improved the studies on the history of medicine. The exhibition is arranged in a chronological order, from the dawn of human existence to the present day. Centred on the history of medical science, the Museum s collections are diverse and of great value for their rarity and quality of execution. Paintings, photos, medical and surgical equipment and machines, Pharmacy’ objects, medical book, among others, make up the vast and diverse holdings of the Museu Maximiano Lemos.


Visits by prior booking.

The passion for the sea has always been a symbol and a distinctive feature of the Portuguese people. From that passion Comur was born, in a saga where ancient wisdoms and flavours witness the reunion of the sea with its heroes. As a tribute to the work of the fishing industry and the women of Murtosa, each Comur shop is a showcase where the stars of this constellation twinkle: tinned products.

At the Museu Judiciário do Tribunal da Relação do Porto, included in the Palácio da Justiça do Porto, one can see objects related to the judicial life, such as furniture, professional attire, ink bottles, ballot boxes for selecting cases/lawsuits, documents of historical interest, register books, codes, emblematic cases/lawsuits and painting and sculpture works. Some sculptural and pictorial pieces from the old premises of the Cadeia da Relação stand out, such as a beautiful statue of Nossa Senhora da Conceição, a smaller statue from Santo António and emblematic cases/lawsuits of Camilo Castelo Branco, Ana Plácido, Zé do Telhado and the doctor Urbino de Freitas.


Visits by prior booking.

local: O Mundo Fantástico da Sardinha Portuguesa
descricao: The queen of all fish and the prodigy of the ocean, the sardine, a fish that is both noble and popular, ascends the throne in the shop of O Mundo Fantástico da Sardinha Portuguesa, whose cans recover and dignify the colourful past of the canning industry. Full of colour and fantasy, they bear the years from 1916 to the present day, marking an important event and the birth of the most relevant personalities of that year. On a permanent festive date, the court’s entertainment takes place with the queen on the throne and a banquet of music, movement, light, colour and smiles from a staff dressed up to welcome you.

Located in a recently restored 19th century building, Menina Colina Guesthouse has at its disposal 9 magnificent rooms, combining the old character with a modern and sophisticated furniture that provides all the comfort.

In addition, you can enjoy the common areas, among which we highlight the entrance and the two grandiose landscape paintings of the time, the magnificent music room and its ornaments, the original breakfast room and the tranquil and lush garden, as well as its terrace.

Fonoteca Municipal do Porto  FMP  is simultaneously a sound archive of the city - representative of a large part of the record production in Portugal, as well as imported records, by international artists and works of non-musical content, such as poetry or political speeches - and a public space of musical enjoyment.

The collection, comprised by a collection of more than 35 thousand vinyl records, was created mainly through donations from Rádio Renascença and Rádio Difusão Portuguesa, as well as institutions and individuals that recognize the importance of the project. Continuing this mission and archiving and documentation, FMP will remain open to donations from everyone.

The project takes on an informational and pedagogical commitment, open to all audiences, by producing podcasts, articles and visual displays on artists, themes, genres, among others, of the materials that make up the collection. FMP features a series of activities that reflect the intention of connecting the history of music to contemporary culture, which includes concerts by guest artists based on the existing archive, weekly active listening programmes, school visits and in-house artists.

Serralves Park, from the first half of the 20th century, was the first national private garden built based on a landscape architecture project.

Completed in 1940, the garden adapts itself to the neoclassical layout of the art of French gardens, slightly modernised, moving away from the traditional concept of gardens in effect in Porto.

Whether for its historical and artistic characteristics, or for its size  18 hectares , landscape diversity and state of conservation, Serralves Park is a unique place in the national and international scene.

It includes very diverse landscapes, from the sloping terraces to the thick forests, from the labyrinthine geometry of some gardens to the agricultural land, from the rose garden to the romantic lake.

Comprised by remains of a 19th-century garden, Quinta do Mata-Sete, Serralves Villa, Serralves Museum of Contemporary Art and Treetop Walk, whose gardens were designed to blend harmoniously with the surrounding landscape and the built structure. Building designated a monument of public interest.

Founded in 1829 and owned by the same family for 5 generations, Araújo & Sobrinho started its activity as a paper warehouse. It is currently one of the oldest stationery shops in the world and certainly the oldest stationery shop in Porto. Located in the historic centre of the city, in a charismatic building where the façade with a unique tiled panel stands out, it has on display a diverse range of museum artifacts, artwork and typography for those who visit to contemplate. Araújo & Sobrinho is still able to meet all stationery and office needs, both in its historic establishment and just a click away, in its online sales platform.

The specialties are the same as the Chez Lapin, as well as the quality of the dishes served. With a capacity for up to 48 people, this is the perfect place to hold a lunch or dinner meeting with family and friends, as well as work and / or business meetings.

The Queijaria Amaral is the oldest cheese dairy in the city, opened since 1920. It is an establishment with tradition, specialized in making cheese Serra da Estrela, traditional smoked meats from the best regions of the country and bread from Bragança, Mirandela and Avintes.
