The style and elegance of a landmark hotel in the historic area of Porto.

Exe Almada Porto 4* offers its guests the opportunity to stay in the historic area of the city of Porto, which is a designated UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Situated in a prime location on the riverside area of Porto and 15 minutes away from the airport, NEYA Porto Hotel is close to the banks of the Douro, offering a stunning river view that brings together the ideal conditions to escape the daily routine and go on a family trip, or for a business or leisure stay. It has 124 rooms, Business Center, Day Spa, Kids Club, the Viva Porto restaurant inspired by traditional Portuguese cuisine and the bar Último is a mandatory meeting point in the city of Porto and where you can enjoy a panoramic view over the Douro River.

Porto Innovation Hub  PIH , is an initiative of the Municipality of Porto which aims to be a platform for the strengthening of the city’s innovation, technology and entrepreneurship ecosystem. Through an open space that enhances the experimentation and exploration of new ideas, PIH promotes initiatives and multidisciplinary projects that demonstrate the direct impact of innovation on the improvement in the citizens  quality of life, in the increase of services, products and businesses efficiency, and in the transformation of the city, making it a living laboratory.

Arcádia is a Portuguese family-owned company which produces handmade chocolate and almonds. Founded in 1933 in the city of Porto by Manuel Pereira Bastos, the brand is currently run by the founder s grandchildren.

Known for its Traditional Assortment, which maintains the original recipe since its foundation, as well as the Languages of Chocolate Línguas de Gato, innovation is an integral part of this company, for example the famous chocolate bonbons with Port wine, brandy or even ginger and cinnamon. Arcádia has 70 types of chocolates available and at certain times of the year it even produces about 60,000 chocolates a day.

Located on floor 0 of Francisco Sá Carneiro Airport, next to the public area of the arrivals, it has an experienced and multilingual professional team, who will recommend you accommodation, restaurants and tours. You can also conveniently book, and purchase travel tickets and shows, museum admission tickets, souvenirs, guidebooks...

On December 25th, the Tourist Office will be closed. It will be open on December 24th from 9am to 5pm and on January 1st from 10am to 6pm.


It is now possible to book Antigen and PCR tests at our Tourist Offices.


Located in the historic centre, next to São Bento Station, it has an experienced and multilingual professional team, who will recommend you accommodation, restaurants and tours. You can also conveniently book, and purchase travel tickets and shows, museum admission tickets, souvenirs, guidebooks ..

Belonging to the Bastos family since 1933, Arcádia is a brand of traditional chocolates with its own shops and it is a reference in the national chocolatery. Known for its chocolates, ‘línguas de gato’  cat s tongues - hard textured, cinnamon and lemon-flavoured biscuits in the shape of filled eight  and dragees, the brand still maintains tradition in its manufacturing processes. The chocolates of the brand are therefore produced from traditional recipes and methods, using natural and proven quality raw materials in their production. Arcádia also offers several products produced from chocolate, such as chocolate bars or bonbons. For each one of these products it offers a variety of flavours, such as Port Wine bonbons.

The Holocaust Museum of Porto has the mission of promoting education and memory about the Holocaust, making its visitors aware and sensitized so that this, which is one of the darkest episodes in our history, will not be repeated.

Visitors will have the opportunity to visit the reproduction of the Auschwitz’s barracks, as well as a hall with the names of the victims, a memorial flame, a cinema, a conference hall, a study centre, corridors with the whole story and, like in the Holocaust Museum in Washington DC, photographs and screens showing real footage about the before, during and after the tragedy. 


Closed: national and Jewish holidays  Moveable feast

A unique opportunity in Europe to visit the arch of a bridge, experiencing an iconic and impressive bridge for its size and elegance  it’s the only national monument of the twentieth century in Porto.

The tours are guided and include various safety equipment - harness, lifeline and handrail  even those who have vertigo can climb up!

When we reach the top, we can enjoy unique views of Porto and Gaia, in a quiet and relaxing place that is open to the public for the first time in 53 years.

At the bottom of the bridge, an exhibition on the construction of the bridge and the other 5 bridges that connect Porto and Gaia will make you want to take a stroll along the coastal avenue.


By appointment, we can have guides who speak Italian and German.

FOOTWEAR: Flip-flops and heeled/high-heeled shoes are forbidden  we recommend sneakers or closed shoes.
CLOTHING: Skirts/dresses are prohibited, as they are incompatible with the placement of the harness.

The Mira Mira restaurant is part of the Fashion & Design experience. A haute cuisine restaurant, whose menu is prepared with rigorous nutritional care and meticulous choice of ingredients. In addition, and for the parade of flavours to be more fun, the concept is based on sharing.

Located in Parque da Pasteleira, in the surprising space of an old water tank, Reservatório was rehabilitated in 2017 by architects Alexandre Alves Costa and Sérgio Fernandez, and has a museological project by João Mendes Ribeiro.

The Reservatório tells the material history of the city, its foundations, its walls and squares, the river that runs through it, its streets and monuments, the evolution of its urban fabric and its successive existences, designed and built by hands of designers and builders.

Station 1 of Museu da Cidade summons fragments to narrate moments that indicate existences of this and other times. Materials from different eras coexist with audiovisual devices and different types of images, to show us how PortuCale, the city that gave Portugal its name, became Porto.

The Vinha Boutique Hotel is the result of the rehabilitation of an old manor house located by the river, with an area of 3.5 hectares surrounded by gardens and lakes, which is joined by a new building that reinterprets, in a clean line, the elegant terraces which are drawn on the slopes of the Douro wine region.

Vibrant, welcoming, and with a distinctly contemporary interior language, which extends to the 38 existing rooms, of which 19 have an aesthetic-oriented decor of the major international fashion and decor houses, the modern and sophisticated Vinha Boutique Hotel offers a personalised service of excellence.

At the SISLEY Paris SPA in Vinha Boutique Hotel, you will be able to take care of your body and soul, in an inspiring and relaxing environment. Stimulate your taste senses with a gastronomic experience at the Vinha Restaurant, run by the charismatic Chef Renato Cunha, or simply savour a wine from the region, while enjoying the cosy Bar Reserva, with a panoramic view of the bucolic banks of the Douro River, are also some of the suggestions that await you.

In the outdoor surroundings of the hotel, you can enjoy the panoramic swimming pool with a front view overlooking the Douro River, choose to do outdoor activities, such as hiking or cycling in the riverside area, have a picnic, walk along the lakes or woods of the hotel, or even go on a boat trip on the Douro River and admire the iconic views that the region and the city of Porto have to offer.

For guests looking to explore the riches and wonders of the Douro region, and/or the city of Porto, they will find exclusive, personalised, and maximum comfort solutions at the Vinha Boutique Hotel.

“Life is an endless summer”, is the motto of this new WOW’s museum, which tells the story of rosé wine and highlights the more rosy, improbable and fun side of wine and life.

The Pink Palace is the most eccentric of all WOW’s venues. It has several settings that beckon to be photographed and to interact. In addition, throughout the experience, five drinks will be served: Quinta do Vale do Bragão Rosé, AIX Gran Vin de Provence Rosé, Mateus Rosé, Vértice Rosé Bruto and Croft Pink Rosé Port.

Throughout eleven rooms, visitors are led to realise that the rosés are made from grapes from different regions and climates around the world and using a series of winemaking techniques. In other words, there is a real diversity of wine under this “pink patch”. Thus, the trip crosses Portugal, but also Provence and ends, unexpectedly, in a saloon. To embark on this expedition, you need a sense of humour, be relaxed, have an instinct for discovery and, of course, a fully charged smartphone.

A new multifaceted space has been reborn inside the mythical Casa Margaridense, famous for its incomparable pão-de-ló  sponge cake . There is a shopping area offering Portuguese products made the traditional way and a tearoom that, at night, becomes a bar offering a wide-ranging cultural programme. It has a nice terrace, ideal for lively conversation.

At night, try the “pão-de-ló” paired with red wine, while enjoying the DJ’s music selection.

This reopening made different products available: varied jellies, marmalades and sweet food, made with fig, tomato, apple with raisins, pumpkin with nuts or almonds, biscuits and cookies, honey, pieces of jam, teas, chocolates Vianense, candies from Régua and also Douro wines, soap and traditional Viarco pencils.

l Basilico is the new Italian restaurant in the city of Porto, with direct access onto Rua Sá da Bandeira, right at the entrance of the refurbished PortoBay Teatro.

The restaurant is inspired by Italian comfort cuisine, offering delicious risottos, tortellini, raviolis and speciality pizzas cooked in a wood-fired oven. A varied menu, served in a space combining Mediterranean décor and a relaxed, comfortable and inviting atmosphere.

In charge of Il Basilico s kitchen are chefs Pedro Spínola and Italian Irene Vietti, under the guiding hand of Chief Executive Nuno Miguel.

Hello, I am Cedofeita Village.

I was born in the mid-1950’s in Rua da Cedofeita, one of the most iconic historic areas of Porto and a place full of meaningful colours and contrasts that makes my identity so unique.

A careful refurbishment in 2015 brought to life all the comfort and tranquillity that a traditional building can offer. My mid-century architecture style breaths out the atmosphere of Porto - a timeless city that celebrates life in each sunset. I live by a pedestrian zone, so everything you might need will be at your door.

I have 6 apartments with an unusual open-space design concept, blending in monochromatic colours with strong contrasts, wooden surfaces and packed with exquisite design furniture. The bright and cosy apartments are fully equipped and a fabulous 200m2 common garden invites all the guests to step outside to relax after a good day in the city. Alone, with family or friends, you can always count with Cedofeita’s Village warm welcome.

At Cedofeita Village we create stories, stories for you, with you.

PS: we can host up to 19 guests, and it can also be fully booked just for you and your crew.

Right in the middle of the Gardens of Palácio de Cristal, the Super Bock Arena - Pavilhão Rosa Mota is a striking building in the city of Porto, recently renovated.

Built in the 1950 s on the site where for more than 80 years there really was a  Crystal Palace , like those in London and Madrid.

We tell this long story as we visit inside, and save the best for last: in the dome, 150 steps lead us to the top. There, a 360-degree balcony enable us to see Porto like never before, from Boavista to Marquês, from Ribeira to Foz...

A unique tour, an infinite view!

With a privileged location, Pousada Porto – Historic Hotel is the ideal choice for those who wish to stay closer to the city s dynamics on the historic Rua das Flores, an exclusive pedestrian street, within walking distance of the city s main attractions. The 84 rooms have been carefully furnished and decorated to ensure comfort and quality during your stay. The Pousada s P Gastro Bar invites you to take a break and enjoy the delicious snacks from the seasonal menu. It is also possible to take another break in the indoor swimming pool, relax in the sauna or work out in the fitness center.

With long tables and intimate décor, the new WOW restaurant has the perfect menu to enjoy good times with your family. Among the many delicious options that cater to the most different tastes, you can find pizzas, hamburgers, pastas and salads.

Viral Virtual Reality is a 300m2 gaming and simulation entertainment centre with virtual reality technology. Located in the centre of Porto, it offers high-tech virtual reality experiences with unique sensations of fun and challenging games.

It is the restaurant of the new five-star hotel PortoBay Flores [Porto, Portugal].

With a concept of bistronomy, the Bistrô Flores restaurant provides a one-of-a-kind culinary experience in an exquisite venue full of history. The creations of Chef Nuno Miguel and his team can be enjoyed every day, at lunch and dinner. In the former noble halls of a small palace, the authenticity of the bistro comes to life with touches of haute cuisine, quality ingredients, Portuguese flavours and simple service.

House inside a villa in the traditional city of Porto, recently refurbished in a contemporary style, with a smart key, air conditioning, TV and free Wi-Fi. Located in the centre of everything  historic centre, downtown, metro, pharmacy, Estádio do Dragão- Dragão Stadium , but in the peace and quiet of a peaceful residential area. 

We have 2 floors with a maximum capacity for 5 guests plus 1 baby up to 2 years old: 1 bedroom, cot, balcony with outdoor table, dressing room, bathroom with shower, half-bath, kitchen and living room with 2 sofa beds.

--

The reservation implies a security deposit that can refunded if upon inspection of the accommodation after check-out, everything is in order and there is no material damage to the property and its contents.

We are at the guest s disposal for any questions or clarification during their stay, in person or by telephone. We can also organize activities according to the tourist area to be explored  boat cruises on the Douro river, the purchase of train tickets to travel between cities, namely on the Douro, Guimarães, Braga, Aveiro, Coimbra and Lisbon Lines, upon advance payment by the client . We also do the transfer service between the accommodation and the airport for an additional charge.

Note: The decoration of the place can vary according to the season.

The Most Beautiful Bookshop in the World.

Livraria Lello has been a bookshop very much of its times ever since 1906. It carries within it over a century of heritage, imbued into its architectural surroundings and the bookish knowledge that are part of the experience of those who visit it.

It shares intact the beauty of literature that only good books can keep for successive decades. It also preserves the beauty that makes it an architectural and heritage icon of the city, internationally renowned. The neo-Gothic building, the unmistakable facade, the magical staircase, the iconic stained-glass window and the shelves filled with books from the most diverse eras and in different languages, tell unique stories.

One of the oldest Portuguese bookshops, Livraria Lello is an iconic place in the city of Porto, a must-visit for anyone who stays here. Although it is a hundred years old, it continues to live in perfect harmony with its time, being more alive, more inhabited and more dynamic than ever. Keeping books at the centre of all its activities, part of it to host and promote a rich cultural programme, which includes various events, artistic installations, and temporary and permanent exhibitions.

Focused on its mission of getting the whole world to read and internationally recognised as one of the most beautiful bookshops in the world  acclaimed by Time magazine, CNN, Lonely Planet, The Guardian, among many others , Livraria Lello seeks to be a meeting point in Porto and from Porto, for all lovers of Literature.


Closed: Jan 1st, Easter Sunday, May 1st, Jun 24th and Dec 25th

Located in Porto’s historic area, right in front of Palácio da Bolsa, PANAMAR Concept Store seeks, in a historical environment, to offer a catalog of essentially Portuguese products.

Inspired by Henry the Navigator’s motto, we seek to make our products reflect and translate the “talent of good know-how” in several areas: Fashion, Decoration, Jewellery, Art, Culture and Design...

PANAMAR is pleased to welcome all those who wish to visit us and “discover anew” the Portuguese World.

On our 10th anniversary, we are opening our first flagship store. Porto is an obvious choice and the perfect location to find our entire collection in a single location. At the iconic Mota Galiza building you will also find a selection of products from international brands that perfectly match with our designs. We offer a full range of furnishings for the 21st century home.

Located in a historic building on Rua de José Falcão, The Feeting Room’s new project comprises an area of 800m² divided into two retail floors, a restaurant with outdoor seating and an art gallery that opens onto a garden perfect for summer afternoons.

The new project LOT - Labels of Tomorrow, appears as a space that aims to provide a unique retail experience, enhanced and complemented by the collaboration with the art gallery Underdogs Gallery, which will have its first collection in Porto and the vibrant and healthy cuisine of the Eleven Lab Concept restaurant.

LOT will be a part of the city s cultural programme, through the promotion of widely diversified events such as concerts, exhibitions, workshops, launch parties and activities promoted by partner brands.

Partner brands: A LINE, Algori, Alohas, Camper, Cinco, FORA, Goodies Sportive, Guaja, Hanken, Judy Sanderson, Next Memory, Nobrand, Oof Wear, Osklen, Rains and Sanjo.

The Museu do Vitral has opened in the heart of Morro da Sé. It compiles a selection of works by João Aquino Antunes, from traditional stained glass windows to decorative panels and abstract art installations. In addition, it includes a recreation of part of the artist s studio, with sketches and maquettes.

João Aquino Antunes is, it should be noted, the last Portuguese painter who dedicated his life to stained glass and belongs to the family of craftspeople from Atelier Antunes, founded in 1906 and the oldest in Portugal. He is internationally recognised and has works on display all over the world.

The Casa da Vandoma is still a heritage of this family of craftspeople. In addition to the strategic location due to its proximity to the Sé [Cathedral] and the São Bento Station, it is also important to note that the area is part of Morro da Sé, the core area of the Historic Centre of Porto, which is on the UNESCO World Heritage List.

A Cafezeira is an establishment of many years, initially focused on coffee and spices, having introduced new products tailored to the demands and needs of its customers. Coffee is still the protagonist, from several origins, batches of pure coffee, coffee blends with barley, just to mention a few, freshly ground according to the customer’s machine, assorted teas, seeds, dried fruits – honey figs, from the Douro region, Turkish figs, dried pear, walnut with and without shell, hazelnut, grape, currant, plum with and without stone, dates, apricot, pine nuts – dehydrated fruits and spices. They also sell individually and with environmental concerns, which means that the customers can bring their own containers.

The Bolhão Market reopens after a demanding restoration and modernization work to welcome traders, customers and visitors.

In the heart of the city, Bolhão presents itself with its usual soul, but also with new features. The produce market continues to be the main protagonist of this iconic building in the city. Altogether, the Market reopened its doors with more than 70 traders with various products, ranging from fish, vegetables and fruits, but also to handicrafts and coffee shops.

In addition to the best seasonal products in the stalls on the ground floor, Bolhão now has 38 shops on the exterior of the building. There is also space for 10 restaurants on the first floor.

The Bolhão building now has a direct connection to the Metro station, through Praça do Metro, making it possible for all visitors to disembark directly at the Bolhão station and enter the fresh produce market floor.

But the news doesn t stop there! Bolhão also has the Cozinha do Bolhão, a fully equipped kitchen that hosts cookery demos and other gastronomic events, next to the central staircase.

Casa Moreira started as a shop open to the public and a small workshop. It grew and expanded and currently comprises two shops, a workshop and a sawmill area, which has 10 employees. Here you can find a wide range of products for jewels, watches and eyewear. For example, cases for carrying and storing jewellery, jewellery trays, leather bags and briefcases, folding rolls, to name a few, are restored and made from scratch. High quality original products are also designed and created here in different materials and for different purposes, according to the needs and demand of the customers.

Traditional Portuguese food restaurant created in 1982. For over 30 years it has been serving the best of Portuguese food to both Portuguese and foreigners. Casa Rocha is praised for the quality of its homemade food, for the family and genuine atmosphere, for the good service and also for the best value for money. For example, at lunchtime, for €6.50, you can enjoy a meal with soup, main course, beverage and coffee included. It offers, among other specialties, Tripas à moda do Porto, arroz de cabidela, cozido à portuguesa, pataniscas, bolinhos de bacalhau, caldo verde and papas de sarrabulho. To complement your meals, it also serves delicious homemade desserts.

They have Take Away and make home deliveries.

Visit the Living Museum of Conservas Pinhais.
Visit the home of Portuguese artisanal canned fish NURI and Pinhais. Discover the century-old history and feel it with your hands. Discover a unique process, using the freshest fish and the best ingredients.
Explore every corner of our factory, say hello to our family and end your visit with a tasting of typical Pinhais flavours.

The New York Times has already been here! Have you visited us yet?
The American newspaper The New York Times paid a visit to Porto and participated in our Factory Tour, is the home of Conservas Pinhais, which houses internationally renowned brands such as NURI and Pinhais.

Porto s multi award-winning Distillery and Gin School. We produce the INVICTA GIN collection and are producing Rum, premium spirits and unique products that represent the produce, history and beauty of Portugal.

Our location  on a park  and our distillery  you should see it…  are special and we are Portugal’s first urban distillery, have founded the first Gin School in Portugal and are the world’s first producer of Rum in the City of Porto.

At Lóios 80 you will find dining experiences with Portuguese and Porto Mediterranean cuisine, the best cocktails to the sound of good music and excellent service. We offer 3 floors with rooms prepared for intimate meetings, ideal for group celebrations or business dinners and a room with a bar where, in a more relaxed atmosphere, you will have daily live music from guest artists who promote their original work. Come visit us and enjoy the best dishes and cocktails overlooking the beautiful architecture of the city of Porto to the sound of good and original music.

This is one of the oldest bakeries in the north of the country and has maintained its quality and tradition since 1898. Located in Foz do Douro and which makes everything in-house, it has the most exquisite delicacies such as brown bread, saloio bread [sourdough], chaparro bread, beetroot bread, corn and sunflower bread, tomato bread, carrot bread, spinach bread, soy bread, sweet broa, bolo-rei, pão-de-ló [sponge cake], convent sweets, cakes, biscuits, jams, honey, cheese and quince cheese. Baked in a wood-fired oven, the bread and broa [cornbread] fill the space with an unmistakable aroma. With a heritage of over 100 years, Padaria Formosa continues to provide quality bread to the city of Porto on a daily basis.

Chapelaria Centro de Moda was founded in 1897 and has always been owned by the same family. It is currently in the hands of the fourth generation. All its products have been designed, created and made in accordance with the highest quality standards. This hat shop has an extensive selection of caps and hats, in a wide variety of styles and colours. It also produces hats and caps to order, in large quantities. At Chapelaria Centro de Moda you will find great quality items and an excellent customer service and advice, characteristics that make this establishment renowned in the hat trade and industry.

Padaria Oriental is a traditional bakery company founded in October 1927 and is characterised by making bread in a wood-fired oven, which gives it a different texture and unique flavour throughout the day. All bakery products are made in the oven itself and so its aim is to satisfy all those who taste its products. There is a deep respect for traditional recipes, making their creation process far from automatic, in order to preserve flavour and quality. Of note are the biju, paris, cantos, pão d água [bread], seeded bread, broa  with or without chouriço , bread with chouriço, tosta bebé, brown baguette and bran roll, assorted regueifas [twisted bread], among many others.

Regular film screenings in the city of Porto began in the summer of 1906 at the Salão High-Life, a shack at Feira de S. Miguel, located where the Rotunda da Boavista is today. This project was started by Manuel da Silva Neves and Edmond Pascaud — who would go on to found Neves & Pascaud, an iconic company in the city s cinematography history. The Salão High-Life, which brought together in its audience different social strata of the city, was at the Feira de S. Miguel for only two months, and was then moved to Jardim da Cordoaria. Two years later, under the name Novo Salão High-Life, it moved definitively to Praça da Batalha, but it only started to take its familiar shape in the 1940s! The building, which housed the Novo Salão High-Life, was demolished and replaced by the new Cinema, a building designed by Artur Andrade. But Cinema Batalha would end up closing, for the first time, in 2000.

In 2012, Cinema Batalha was listed Monument of Public Interest, a designation that safeguarded the building’s integrity. Finally, in 2017, Porto City Hall took over the management of Cinema Batalha for a period of 25 years and announced its intention to renovate the historic building. It was then returned to the city in December 2022.

Built within a modernist approach of integrating visual arts into architecture, the building features works by five artists. The most iconic, due to their symbolic value in the history of censorship under the Estado Novo regime in Portugal, are the low and high relief on the façade and the murals by Júlio Pomar. At Cinema Batalha we can also find works by Arlindo Rocha, António Sampaio and Augusto Gomes.

The iconic Centro de Cinema is back up and running with new spaces to explore, such as the Library and Film Library, Sala-Filme, Bookshop and the Café/Bar. The programme also offers a new look to film that includes the presentation of retrospectives, themed cycles, focuses on contemporary practices and the connections between film and other arts.

local: Associação Humanitária Bombeiros Voluntários do Porto
descricao: Em finais do século XIX, dada a grande expansão e desenvolvimento da Cidade, um grupo de notáveis Portuenses decidiu criar uma Associação, cuja finalidade seria dotar a Cidade e a Região com um Corpo de Bombeiros Voluntários que a Cidade há muito pedia e exigia.   Nasce, assim, em 1875 a Associação Humanitária dos Bombeiros Voluntários do Porto, inicialmente instalada no Pátio do Paraíso, na Rua do Bonjardim.  Actualmente estão instalados no quartel localizado na Rua Rodrigues Sampaio, muito próximo da Câmara Municipal.

A Polícia de Segurança Pública, designada por PSP, é uma força de segurança, uniformizada e armada, com natureza de serviço público e dotada de autonomia administrativa.   A PSP tem por missão assegurar a legalidade democrática, garantir a segurança interna e os direitos dos cidadãos, nos termos da Constituição e da lei.  A PSP compreende a direcção nacional, as unidades de polícia e os estabelecimentos de ensino policial.

A Esquadra de Turismo da Polícia de Segurança Pública ou PSP - Comando Metropolitano do Porto, foi criada em 1998. Tem como objetivos a segurança e o contato com o turista, estando os agentes habilitados no domínio de várias línguas estrangeiras, nomeadamente inglês, espanhol, francês, italiano. Fiscaliza, também, meios de transporte públicos disponíveis sobretudo para turistas, bem como os autocarros de turismo, em termos de qualidade do transporte.

Os Bombeiros Voluntários Portuenses são uma associação humanitária fundada em 9 Abril de 1924 que presta auxílio no combate a incêndios florestais, urbanos e industriais, socorro a náufragos, transporte de doentes e prestam assistência pré-hospitalar na área do Grande Porto.  Os serviços prestados incluem serviços de ambulância, abertura de porta, escoamentos, abastecimentos de água, corte de árvores, serviço de reboques - até 1.750 kg P. B., limpezas e/ou lavagens em espaços abertos ou fechados.

Criada por Decreto de 3 de Maio de 1911, a Guarda Nacional Republicana é uma força de segurança de natureza militar, constituída por militares organizados num corpo especial de tropas e dotada de autonomia administrativa, com jurisdição em todo o território nacional e no mar territorial.  No Porto está instalada no Quartel junto da Igreja dos Carmelitas Descalços.

Em 1938 é criado o primeiro Corpo de Polícia Municipal do Porto. A Polícia Municipal do Porto, encontra-se na dependência directa do Exmo. Presidente da Câmara Municipal para efeitos de serviço, podendo o mesmo delegar essa competência num dos seus Vereadores. Disciplinarmente, por se tratar de um destacamento da PSP e como tal sujeito a regulamento disciplinar próprio, depende da Direcção Nacional da Corporação.  As competências são transversais a várias áreas desde a habitação social, obras, comércio, licenciamentos, saúde pública e policiamentos.

O Regimento de Sapadores Bombeiros ou RSB é um corpo de bombeiros profissionais e uma unidade orgânica da Câmara Municipal do Porto. Com cerca de 300 colaboradores, divididos por 264 operacionais e 36 civis, o BSB tem como missão, garantir a proteção de pessoas, de bens e do ambiente na área do Município do Porto, prevenindo as situações que os ponham em perigo, ou limitando as suas consequências minimizando os danos pessoais, de acordo com elevados padrões de qualidade, rigor e profissionalismo, de forma a constituir uma referência nas áreas de prevenção e da prestação de socorro em Portugal.  Breves Apontamentos Históricos: Em 1728 D. João V promulga, em 29 de janeiro, a Provisão Régia que cria a Companhia do Fogo ou da Bomba. Em 1832 passa a designar-se por Companhia de Incêndios, com estatuto de força militarizada. Em 1853 a Câmara introduziu o sistema de alarme das chamadas Caixas de Sinais e em 1889 passa a designar-se como Corpo de Salvação Pública. Já em 1927, é condecorado com o Grau de Cavaleiro da Ordem Militar do Valor, Lealdade e Mérito da Torre e Espada. Em 1946, é fixada a atual designação de Batalhão de Sapadores Bombeiros. Em 1959 é inaugurado o atual Quartel Sede. Em 1978 é condecorado com o Crachá de Ouro da Liga dos Bombeiros Portugueses e com a Medalha de Ouro da cidade do Porto. No ano seguinte recebe a condecoração da Ordem da Benemerência  e em 1984 é condecorado com a Medalha da Região Militar do Norte. Já em 1987 é inaugurada a Estação n.º 1, Sé, Zona Histórica da Cidade. Em 26 de junho de 2014, nas comemorações do seu 286º aniversário, o BSB recebe a Fénix de Ouro, a mais alta condecoração da Liga dos Bombeiros Portugueses. Em 10 de abril, recebe a Medalha de Mérito de Proteção e Socorro, no grau Ouro e distintivo azul, pelo Ministério da Administração Interna.
